[H10D 44/454] Output structures -- The H10D 44/454 entry covers the output structures of charge-transfer devices, a subclass of inorganic electric semiconductor devices such as certain types of diodes and transistors. It deals with the design and arrangement of electrodes, terminals, and interconnections that deliver electrical signals from the semiconductor to external circuits.
[H10D 44/456] Structures for regeneration, refreshing or leakage compensation -- H10D 44/456 covers structures integrated in inorganic semiconductor charge-transfer devices that provide regeneration, refreshing or leakage-compensation functions, such as electrodes, layers or circuits designed to restore charge states or counteract leakage currents and thereby maintain device performance. It applies to renewal and balancing elements within such semiconductor components.
[H10D 44/462] Buried-channel CCD -- A buried-channel CCD is a charge-coupled device whose charge-transfer channel is located below the semiconductor surface, reducing surface traps and improving charge transfer efficiency and noise performance. This design is employed in imaging and scientific sensors that need high sensitivity and low dark current, and it belongs to the inorganic electric semiconductor devices class.
[H10D 44/464] Two-phase CCD -- Two-phase CCDs are charge-coupled devices that shift charge packets with two clock phases per pixel, allowing efficient charge transfer in inorganic semiconductor structures such as silicon. They are used in imaging sensors and other applications that require precise, low-noise charge manipulation.
[H10D 44/466] Three-phase CCD -- The H10D 44/466 entry covers three-phase charge-coupled devices, a type of inorganic semiconductor charge-transfer device that uses three sequential clock phases to move stored charge packets across the chip. This architecture is employed in imaging sensors and other applications requiring precise, low-noise charge transport.
[H10D 44/468] Four-phase CCD -- The H10D 44/468 subclass pertains to four-phase charge-coupled devices, a type of charge transfer semiconductor where charge packets are moved by four distinct clock phases. These devices are used primarily in imaging sensors and other applications requiring precise charge shifting across an inorganic semiconductor substrate.
[H10D 44/472] Surface-channel CCD -- Surface-channel CCDs are inorganic semiconductor charge-transfer devices in which charge packets are moved along a surface channel formed at the semiconductor's interface, typically in silicon. The structure uses patterned electrodes to create potential wells that shift the charge sequentially for image sensing or signal processing.
[H10D 44/474] Two-phase CCD -- Two-phase CCDs are charge-coupled devices that move stored charge packets using a pair of clock phases rather than the more common three-phase scheme. The two-phase architecture simplifies electrode design and can reduce power consumption while still enabling efficient charge transfer for image-sensor and signal-processing applications. This subclass covers the structures, operation and related manufacturing methods of such two-phase charge-transfer semiconductor devices.
[H10D 44/476] Three-phase CCD -- Three-phase CCDs are charge-coupled devices that move charge packets along an inorganic semiconductor substrate using a three-phase clocking scheme. The three-phase drive enables precise control of charge transfer, improving image-sensor performance and signal-processing speed in semiconductor applications.
[H10D 44/478] Four-phase CCD -- Four-phase CCDs are charge-coupled devices that use a four-phase clocking scheme to move charge packets across an inorganic semiconductor substrate. This architecture improves charge transfer efficiency and allows higher readout speeds, making it useful in imaging sensors and other charge-transfer applications within the broader class of electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 48/00] Individual devices not covered by groups -- In this group, when the manufacture or treatment of a device is determined to be novel and non-obvious, the device itself is also classified. Group H10D48/00 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D48/30 . Groups H10D48/30 and H10D48/00 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 48/01] Manufacture or treatment -- H10D 48/01 covers manufacturing and treatment processes for individual inorganic semiconductor devices that are not assigned to a specific group, such as methods of forming, doping, annealing, packaging, surface modification or other treatments applied to discrete power diodes, thyristors, or similar components.
[H10D 48/021] of two-electrode devices -- Group H10D48/021 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D8/051 . Group H10D48/021 is also impacted by reclassification into groups H10D1/025 , H10D1/045 - H10D1/048 and H10D8/01 - H10D8/055 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 48/031] of three-or-more electrode devices -- Group H10D48/031 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D10/01 - H10D10/061 , H10D12/01 - H10D12/038 and H10D18/01 - H10D18/031 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 48/032] of unipolar transistors having ohmic electrodes on emitter-like, base-like, and collector-like regions, e.g. hot electron transistors [HET], metal base transistors [MBT], resonant tunneling transistors [RTT], bulk barrier transistors [BBT], planar doped barrier transistors [PDBT] or charge injection transistors [CHINT] -- H10D 48/032 covers inorganic unipolar transistors that use ohmic contacts on regions functioning like emitter, base and collector. The code includes hot-electron, metal-base, resonant-tunnelling, bulk-barrier, planar doped-barrier and charge-injection transistors.
[H10D 48/04] of devices having bodies comprising selenium or tellurium in uncombined form -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Manufacture or treatment of individual devices having bodies comprising selenium or tellurium in uncombined form other than as impurities in semiconductor bodies of other materials.
[H10D 48/042] Preparation of foundation plates -- H10D 48/042 covers processes for making the base or foundation plates that serve as substrates for inorganic semiconductor devices, including methods of casting, shaping, polishing, doping or otherwise treating the plate material before device layers are added. It addresses preparation steps that ensure the mechanical stability, flatness and electrical properties needed for subsequent semiconductor fabrication.
[H10D 48/043] Preliminary treatment of the selenium or tellurium, its application to foundation plates or the subsequent treatment of the combination -- This group covers processes for pre-treating selenium or tellurium before they are used as the base material in inorganic semiconductor devices, including methods of applying the treated material to foundation plates and any further treatment of the resulting combination.
[H10D 48/0431] Application of the selenium or tellurium to the foundation plate -- H10D 48/0431 covers the use of selenium or tellurium as a material for the foundation plate in inorganic electric semiconductor devices such as photovoltaic cells or light-emitting diodes. The classification is part of the CPC hierarchy under electricity - semiconductor devices - inorganic electric semiconductor devices - individual devices not covered by groups. It addresses the specific application of these chalcogen elements to the device substrate.
[H10D 48/044] Conversion of the selenium or tellurium to the conductive state -- The H10D 48/044 class covers methods for turning elemental selenium or tellurium from their non-conductive, semiconductor form into a conductive state, such as by doping, alloying, thermal treatment, illumination, or other physical or chemical processes that increase carrier concentration.
[H10D 48/045] Treatment of the surface of the selenium or tellurium layer after having been made conductive -- The subclass covers post-conductivity surface treatments of selenium or tellurium layers in thin-film semiconductor devices, including processes such as doping, passivation, coating or cleaning, aimed at improving electrical contact, stability or overall performance.
[H10D 48/046] Provision of discrete insulating layers -- The class covers semiconductor devices made of inorganic materials where separate insulating layers are added to isolate components or improve electrical performance. It includes methods and structures for providing discrete insulating layers within individual devices not otherwise classified, such as diodes, transistors, or other semiconductor elements.
[H10D 48/047] Application of an electrode to the exposed surface of the selenium or tellurium after the selenium or tellurium has been applied to foundation plates -- The CPC subclass H10D 48/047 covers the step of attaching an electrode directly to the exposed surface of a selenium or tellurium layer after that layer has been deposited on a foundation plate, within inorganic electric semiconductor devices such as individual semiconductor components. This procedure is used to create electrical contacts on semiconductor materials that are not otherwise covered by broader groups.
[H10D 48/048] Treatment of the complete device, e.g. by electroforming to form a barrier -- The CPC subclass H10D 48/048 relates to processes that treat an entire inorganic semiconductor device, for example by electroforming a protective or functional barrier on the finished component. It covers methods applied after device fabrication to modify surface characteristics, improve electrical isolation or enhance reliability through the formation of barrier layers. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of electricity-semiconductor devices-inorganic electric semiconductor devices-individual devices not covered by groups.
[H10D 48/049] Ageing -- H10D 48/049 covers the ageing of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that are individual devices not classified elsewhere, addressing the processes, effects and methods related to performance degradation over time. It includes mechanisms such as thermal, electrical and environmental stress that impact device reliability.
[H10D 48/07] of devices having bodies comprising cuprous oxide [Cu -- The code H10D 48/07 designates semiconductor devices whose active body is composed of cuprous oxide, an inorganic copper(I) oxide material. These devices belong to the class of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that are not otherwise grouped, and are used for functions such as rectification, photodetection, or switching.
[H10D 48/071] Preparation of the foundation plate, preliminary treatment oxidation of the foundation plate or reduction treatment -- H10D 48/071 covers processes for preparing the base plate of inorganic semiconductor devices, including oxidation or reduction pretreatments applied before device fabrication. It pertains to the foundation plate of individual inorganic electric semiconductor devices and is part of the CPC hierarchy under electricity, semiconductor devices, inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 48/073] Preliminary treatment of the foundation plate -- The code covers methods for preparing the foundation plate of inorganic semiconductor devices, such as cleaning, surface conditioning, oxidation, etching or other pre-treatment steps applied before forming the active layers. It pertains to processes that improve adhesion, reduce contaminants and modify surface properties of the base substrate.
[H10D 48/074] Oxidation and subsequent heat treatment of the foundation plate  Reduction of copper oxide  H10D48/075 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10D 48/074 pertains to the process of oxidizing and then heat treating a foundational plate, specifically addressing the reduction of copper oxide, as detailed in the subsequent classification H10D48/075. This falls under the broader categories of Electricity, Semiconductor Devices, and Inorganic Electric Semiconductor Devices.
[H10D 48/075] Reduction of the copper oxide or treatment of the oxide layer -- The H10D 48/075 classification covers methods for reducing copper oxide or otherwise treating the oxide layer on inorganic semiconductor devices, such as copper-based contacts or interconnects. It pertains to processes that remove or modify copper oxides to improve electrical conductivity, reliability and overall performance of the device. This entry falls under the hierarchy of electricity, semiconductor devices, inorganic electric semiconductor devices, and individual devices not covered by other groups.
[H10D 48/076] Application of a non-genetic conductive layer -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10D 48/076 pertains to the application of a non-genetic conductive layer in individual inorganic electric semiconductor devices not covered by other specific groups. This classification is used for patents detailing methods or processes that involve adding a conductive layer to semiconductors, where the layer's conductivity is not derived from genetic modification.
[H10D 48/078] Treatment of the complete device, e.g. electroforming or ageing -- This CPC subclass pertains to post-fabrication operations applied to whole inorganic electric semiconductor devices, such as electroforming, ageing, annealing or other treatments that modify the complete device. It is grouped under H10D 48 for individual devices not covered by other more specific groups.
[H10D 48/30] Devices controlled by electric currents or voltages -- Group H10D48/30 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D48/00 . Groups H10D48/30 and H10D48/00 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 48/32] Devices controlled by only the electric current supplied, or only the electric potential applied, to an electrode which does not carry the current to be rectified, amplified or switched -- H10D 48/32 covers devices that are regulated solely by the magnitude of the electric current or the voltage applied to an electrode that does not itself conduct the signal being processed. Such devices manipulate the flow without the controlling electrode carrying the rectified, amplified or switched current, typical in certain types of semiconductor control circuits.
[H10D 48/34] Bipolar devices -- H10D 48/34 refers to bipolar semiconductor devices, which are individual inorganic electric components that use both electron and hole charge carriers for operation. The class includes bipolar junction transistors, heterojunction bipolar transistors and similar devices that are not allocated to more specific groups within the semiconductor hierarchy.
[H10D 48/341] Unijunction transistors, i.e. double base diodes -- This subclass covers unijunction transistors, also known as double-base diodes, which are single-junction semiconductor devices that combine a base and a collector region to provide a sharp trigger voltage. They fall under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and are listed under individual devices not covered by other groups.
[H10D 48/345] Bipolar transistors having ohmic electrodes on emitter-like, base-like, and collector-like regions -- In the CPC system H10D 48/345 refers to bipolar transistors whose emitter, base and collector regions are all equipped with ohmic contacts rather than rectifying ones. The classification covers devices where each region behaves like a metal-semiconductor junction providing low-resistance electrical connection.
[H10D 48/36] Unipolar devices -- H10D 48/36 covers unipolar devices, which are inorganic semiconductor components that rely on a single type of charge carrier for conduction. These include diodes, field-effect transistors and similar elements where only electrons or only holes dominate the current flow, and are not assigned to more specific groups within the CPC.
[H10D 48/362] Unipolar transistors having ohmic electrodes on emitter-like, base-like, and collector-like regions, e.g. hot electron transistors [HET], metal base transistors [MBT], resonant tunnelling transistors [RTT], bulk barrier transistors [BBT], planar doped barrier transistors [PDBT] or charge injection transistors [CHINT] -- H10D 48/362 covers unipolar transistors whose three ohmic contacts act like emitter, base and collector regions, allowing charge injection and control similar to bipolar devices. It includes hot-electron, metal-base, resonant-tunnelling, bulk-barrier, planar doped-barrier and charge-injection transistor structures that use these emitter-like, base-like and collector-like regions.
[H10D 48/366] Multistable devices; Devices having two or more distinct operating states -- H10D 48/366 covers inorganic semiconductor components that can reside in two or more separate operating states, such as bistable or multistable switches, memory cells and flip-flop circuits. The classification is used for devices whose functionality relies on distinct, stable states rather than continuous variable operation. It falls under the broader groups for electric semiconductor devices within the Cooperative Patent Classification system.
[H10D 48/38] Devices controlled only by variation of the electric current supplied, or only the electric potential applied, to one or more of the electrodes carrying the current to be rectified, amplified, oscillated or switched -- Group H10D48/38 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D1/40 . Groups H10D1/40 and H10D48/38 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 48/381] Multistable devices; Devices having two or more distinct operating states -- The CPC subclass H10D 48/381 covers multistable semiconductor devices that can sustain two or more distinct operating states, such as flip-flops, memory cells, and bistable switches. These are inorganic electric semiconductor devices whose function relies on the ability to retain separate states without continuous power.
[H10D 48/383] Quantum effect devices, e.g. of devices using quantum reflection, diffraction or interference effects -- Group H10D48/383 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D48/3835 . Groups H10D48/383 and H10D48/3835 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 48/3835] Semiconductor qubit devices comprising a plurality of quantum mechanically interacting semiconductor quantum dots, e.g. Loss-DiVincenzo spin qubits -- Group H10D48/3835 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D48/383 . Groups H10D48/383 and H10D48/3835 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 48/385] Devices using spin-polarised carriers -- This subclass covers electronic components that exploit the spin orientation of charge carriers, such as spin-polarized injection, transport, or detection, to perform functions like data storage, signal processing, or magnetic sensing. It includes spintronic devices, spin valves, magnetic tunnel junctions and related structures where the carrier spin, rather than just charge, is utilized.
[H10D 48/387] Devices controllable only by the variation of applied heat -- These are inorganic semiconductor components whose electrical behavior is regulated exclusively by changing the temperature applied to them. By heating or cooling the device the conductivity or barrier properties shift, enabling functions such as thermal switching, temperature-sensing, or phase-change operation, without any electrical bias or other control signals.
[H10D 48/40] Devices controlled by magnetic fields -- The H10D 48/40 subclass groups semiconductor devices whose operation is governed by magnetic fields, such as magnetically switched power transistors, inductively triggered thyristors and other inorganic electric semiconductor components that are not otherwise classified. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices → individual devices not covered by groups.
[H10D 48/50] Devices controlled by mechanical forces, e.g. pressure -- H10D 48/50 pertains to inorganic electric semiconductor devices whose operation is directly influenced by mechanical forces such as pressure. It includes semiconductor sensors, actuators and other components whose electrical characteristics are modulated by applied mechanical loading.
[H10D 62/00] Constructional details -- Group H10D62/00 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D62/01 . Groups H10D62/00 and H10D62/01 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/01] Manufacture or treatment -- Group H10D62/01 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/00 . Groups H10D62/00 and H10D62/01 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/021] Forming source or drain recesses by etching e.g. recessing by etching and then refilling -- The CPC subclass H10D 62/021 covers the manufacturing step in inorganic semiconductor devices where source or drain regions are formed by etching recesses into the substrate and subsequently refilling them, detailing the etch-and-fill technique used to shape these functional areas.
[H10D 62/051] Forming charge compensation regions, e.g. superjunctions -- Groups H10D62/051 - H10D62/058 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/111 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/052] by forming stacked epitaxial layers -- The subclass H10D 62/052 covers methods for producing inorganic electric semiconductor devices by growing successive epitaxial layers that are stacked on one another. This approach creates multilayer structures such as heterojunctions, quantum-well regions or superlattices for improved electrical performance.
[H10D 62/054] by high energy implantations in bulk semiconductor bodies, e.g. forming pillars -- The H10D 62/054 entry covers techniques that use high-energy ion implantation to modify bulk inorganic semiconductor bodies, creating three-dimensional structures such as pillars or columns. It relates to constructional details of semiconductor devices where the implant creates functional features within the crystal lattice.
[H10D 62/056] by out-diffusing dopants from applied layers -- The subclass covers techniques where dopant atoms migrate outward from a deposited layer into the underlying semiconductor material, forming doped regions without additional implant steps. It includes processes that rely on thermal or ambient-driven diffusion of the applied layer to modify the device's electrical characteristics.
[H10D 62/058] by using trenches, e.g. implanting into sidewalls of trenches or refilling trenches -- The code H10D 62/058 covers semiconductor manufacturing techniques that form or utilize trench structures, such as doping the sidewalls of a trench or refilling a trench with conductive, insulating or other functional material. It is applied to inorganic electric semiconductor devices where trench-based construction provides isolation, channel formation or other device functions.
[H10D 62/10] Shapes, relative sizes or dispositions of the regions of the semiconductor bodies -- Group H10D62/10 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D62/128 and H10D62/129 . Groups H10D62/10 , H10D62/128 and H10D62/129 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/102] Constructional design considerations for preventing surface leakage or controlling electric field concentration -- H10D 62/102 covers constructional design measures in inorganic semiconductor devices that aim to prevent surface leakage and control electric field concentration, such as geometry optimisation, passivation layers, guard-ring structures, isolation techniques and material selections that reduce high-field regions and improve device reliability.
[H10D 62/103] for increasing or controlling the breakdown voltage of reverse-biased devices -- The H10D 62/103 subclass concerns methods and structures that raise or regulate the breakdown voltage of reverse-biased inorganic semiconductor devices. It includes design features such as guard rings, field plates, doping profiles or surface treatments that modify the electric field distribution to achieve the desired voltage tolerance.
[H10D 62/104] having particular shapes of the bodies at or near reverse-biased junctions, e.g. having bevels or moats -- This subgroup covers semiconductor devices where the geometry of the crystal or die near a reverse-biased p-n junction is deliberately formed with features such as beveled edges, trenches or moats to modify electric field distribution or breakdown characteristics. These shape modifications are applied to inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/105] by having particular doping profiles, shapes or arrangements of PN junctions; by having supplementary regions, e.g. junction termination extension [JTE]   IGFETs having LDD or drain extension regions  H10D30/601 -- This classification code pertains to specific constructional details in inorganic electric semiconductor devices, focusing on those with particular doping profiles, shapes, or arrangements of PN junctions, including supplementary regions like JTE. It specifically addresses IGFETs featuring LDD or drain extension regions.
[H10D 62/106] having supplementary regions doped oppositely to or in rectifying contact with regions of the semiconductor bodies, e.g. guard rings with PN or Schottky junctions -- The entry covers semiconductor structures that incorporate additional regions doped opposite to the main device areas, forming rectifying contacts such as guard rings with PN or Schottky junctions. These supplementary regions are used to improve electrical isolation, reduce leakage or enhance breakdown performance in inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/107] Buried supplementary regions, e.g. buried guard rings   multi-RESURF  H10D62/111 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10D 62/107 pertains to the constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices, specifically addressing buried supplementary regions such as buried guard rings and multi-RESURF structures. This classification is used by the European Patent Office (EPO) and the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO).
[H10D 62/108] having localised breakdown regions, e.g. built-in avalanching regions   in self-protected thyristors  H10D18/211 -- Class H10D 62/108 concerns inorganic electric semiconductor devices whose construction incorporates localized breakdown regions, such as built-in avalanche zones that give self-protection to thyristors. These built-in regions allow controlled breakdown to limit fault currents within the device.
[H10D 62/109] Reduced surface field [RESURF] PN junction structures -- Reduced-surface-field (RESURF) structures are semiconductor designs that use a lightly doped region beneath the surface to spread the electric field, allowing higher breakdown voltages in pn-junction devices. The classification covers the geometry, implantation, epitaxial growth and masking techniques that create these RESURF layers for power and high-frequency applications.
[H10D 62/111] Multiple RESURF structures, e.g. double RESURF or 3D-RESURF structures -- Group H10D62/111 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D62/051 - H10D62/058 . Groups H10D62/111 and H10D62/051 - H10D62/058 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/112] for preventing surface leakage due to surface inversion layers, e.g. by using channel stoppers -- H10D 62/112 covers techniques that prevent surface leakage caused by inversion layers on semiconductor surfaces, such as using channel stoppers or other surface-passivation structures. These measures are applied to inorganic electric semiconductor devices to improve reliability and suppress unwanted current paths.
[H10D 62/113] Isolations within a component, i.e. internal isolations -- H10D 62/113 concerns internal isolation structures built into inorganic semiconductor devices. It includes designs and methods for electrically separating regions within a single component, such as trenches, pn-junction isolation or dielectric barriers, used to prevent undesired current flow between circuit sections.
[H10D 62/114] PN junction isolations -- This classification covers methods and structures for isolating PN junctions in inorganic semiconductor devices, focusing on constructional details such as isolation trenches, guard rings, and insulating regions that separate devices on the same chip. It includes techniques like ion implantation, diffusion, or epitaxial growth to create electrically isolated zones.
[H10D 62/115] Dielectric isolations, e.g. air gaps -- H10D 62/115 covers constructional measures that provide dielectric isolation in inorganic electric semiconductor devices, specifically using air-gap techniques to separate conductive parts and prevent electrical coupling. It addresses how such gaps are formed, positioned and maintained within the device structure.
[H10D 62/116] adjoining the input or output regions of field-effect devices, e.g. adjoining source or drain regions -- H10D 62/116 relates to inorganic electric semiconductor devices and specifies structures that are positioned directly next to the input or output regions of field-effect devices, such as adjacent to the source or drain areas of a transistor. This classification addresses the constructional detail of adjoining those regions.
[H10D 62/117] Shapes of semiconductor bodies -- H10D 62/117 classifies the geometric forms of individual inorganic semiconductor bodies used in electric devices. It covers the overall shape, cross-section and dimensional variations of chips and wafers, such as planar, epitaxial, cylindrical or custom profiles, as part of constructional details.
[H10D 62/118] Nanostructure semiconductor bodies -- H10D 62/118 pertains to the constructional features of inorganic semiconductor devices that employ nanostructured semiconductor bodies, such as quantum dots, nanowires, nanorods, and other nanoscale geometries. It covers designs that exploit the unique electrical and optical properties of these nanostructures for improved device performance.
[H10D 62/119] Nanowire, nanosheet or nanotube semiconductor bodies -- H10D 62/119 covers the constructional details of inorganic semiconductor devices that employ nanowire, nanosheet or nanotube bodies as the active semiconductor material. It includes the geometry, arrangement and integration of these one-dimensional or two-dimensional structures in devices such as transistors, sensors or optoelectronic components. The subclass focuses on the physical configuration rather than the material composition or circuit function.
[H10D 62/121] oriented parallel to substrates -- H10D 62/121 covers constructional features of inorganic electric semiconductor devices in which the active crystalline or polycrystalline material is aligned parallel to the supporting substrate, such as epitaxial layers, thin-film stacks or patterned structures that are deliberately oriented to improve electrical or optical performance.
[H10D 62/122] oriented at angles to substrates, e.g. perpendicular to substrates -- This subclass covers inorganic semiconductor components whose active structures are placed at an angle to the supporting substrate, such as layers or elements that are positioned perpendicularly or otherwise tilted relative to the substrate surface. The orientation is a constructional feature intended to influence electrical or optical performance. It falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/123] comprising junctions -- H10D 62/123 covers inorganic electric semiconductor devices whose construction includes one or more junctions, such as p-n, Schottky or heterojunctions, that form the basic active region of components like diodes, transistors, thyristors and similar devices. It addresses the structural aspects of integrating these junctions into the device architecture.
[H10D 62/124] Shapes, relative sizes or dispositions of the regions of semiconductor bodies or of junctions between the regions -- H10D 62/124 covers the geometric design of semiconductor bodies, including the shapes, relative sizes and spatial arrangements of doped regions and junctions within a device. It addresses how these configurations affect electrical characteristics and is used to classify patents dealing with layout optimization of diodes, transistors and integrated circuits.
[H10D 62/125] Shapes of junctions between the regions -- H10D 62/125 covers the geometric configuration of junctions between regions in inorganic electric semiconductor devices, such as p-n, n-i-p or similar interfaces, describing how the shape of these junctions is formed and arranged within the device structure. It is a constructional-detail subclass within semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/126] Top-view geometrical layouts of the regions or the junctions -- This subclass covers patents that disclose the planar top-view arrangement of regions, junctions or other features on an inorganic semiconductor device, such as the geometry of doped zones, contacts and gate structures as seen from above. The focus is on the layout pattern rather than the three-dimensional structure or material composition.
[H10D 62/127] of cellular field-effect devices, e.g. multicellular DMOS transistors or IGBTs -- This subclass covers semiconductor devices composed of multiple field-effect cells integrated in a single package, such as multicellular DMOS transistors and insulated-gate bipolar transistors, focusing on their constructional details.
[H10D 62/128] Anode regions of diodes -- Group H10D62/128 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/10 . Groups H10D62/10 and H10D62/128 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/129] Cathode regions of diodes -- Group H10D62/129 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/10 . Groups H10D62/10 and H10D62/129 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/13] Semiconductor regions connected to electrodes carrying current to be rectified, amplified or switched, e.g. source or drain regions -- This group covers only semiconductor regions for devices that comprise three or more electrodes.
[H10D 62/133] Emitter regions of BJTs -- The CPC subclass H10D 62/133 covers the design and manufacturing of the emitter region in bipolar-junction transistors, including its doping profile, geometry, contact formation and integration with other device layers. It pertains to semiconductor structures where the emitter's material composition, thickness or shape is specifically defined to control carrier injection. This subclass falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and relates to constructional details of BJTs.
[H10D 62/134] of lateral BJTs -- H10D 62/134 covers the constructional details of lateral bipolar junction transistors, a type of inorganic semiconductor device where the active region extends parallel to the substrate surface. The entry addresses layout, geometry, and fabrication features specific to the lateral BJT architecture, distinguishing it from vertical configurations. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/135] Non-interconnected multi-emitter structures -- The H10D 62/135 subclass covers inorganic semiconductor structures in which several emitter regions are formed on the same chip but remain electrically isolated from one another. It pertains to constructional details of multi-emitter arrangements used in devices such as bipolar transistors or integrated circuits where each emitter functions independently.
[H10D 62/136] of heterojunction BJTs   vertical heterojunction BJTs having one or more non-monocrystalline Group IV elements  H10D10/861 -- The code H10D 62/136 covers constructional details of vertical heterojunction bipolar transistors that incorporate one or more non-monocrystalline Group IV elements in the heterojunction. It falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and specifies designs where the heterojunction BJT is built in a vertical geometry.
[H10D 62/137] Collector regions of BJTs -- The entry covers the design and structural features of the collector region in bipolar junction transistors, including its geometry, doping profile, material composition and integration with the base and emitter. It addresses how these constructional details affect current handling, breakdown voltage and overall device performance in inorganic semiconductor circuits.
[H10D 62/138] Pedestal collectors -- Pedestal collectors refer to a specific type of collector electrode in inorganic semiconductor devices where the collector region is formed as a raised or recessed structure resembling a pedestal. This geometry improves electrical isolation, heat dissipation, and current handling by providing a defined area for carrier collection, and is commonly employed in power transistors and high-voltage switching devices.
[H10D 62/141] Anode or cathode regions of thyristors; Collector or emitter regions of gated bipolar-mode devices, e.g. of IGBTs -- Group H10D62/141 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D62/145 . Groups H10D62/141 and H10D62/145 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/142] Anode regions of thyristors or collector regions of gated bipolar-mode devices -- The CPC subclass H10D 62/142 addresses constructional details of inorganic semiconductor devices, specifically the anode region of thyristors and the collector region of gated bipolar-mode devices, covering aspects such as geometry, doping profiles, isolation structures and other features that define the performance and reliability of these regions.
[H10D 62/145] Emitter regions of IGBTs -- Group H10D62/145 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/141 . Groups H10D62/141 and H10D62/145 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/148] Cathode regions of thyristors -- H10D 62/148 classifies inventions that relate to the constructional features of the cathode region of thyristors, including its geometry, doping profile, contact structures and methods for improving charge carrier injection or blocking capability. The subclass addresses design variations that affect switching speed, voltage rating and reliability of the device.
[H10D 62/149] Source or drain regions of field-effect devices -- H10D 62/149 covers the constructional details of the source and drain regions in field-effect semiconductor devices, specifically those made from inorganic materials. It addresses the design, geometry, doping and formation processes of these regions within the broader category of electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/151] of IGFETs   of IGFETs having LDD or DDD structure  H10D30/601 ; of thin film transistors  H10D30/6713 -- The code H10D 62/151 covers constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices, specifically integrated gate field-effect transistors that incorporate lightly doped drain or double-diffused drain structures and thin-film transistors, with reference to sub-groups H10D30/601 and H10D30/6713 for those designs.
[H10D 62/152] Source regions of DMOS transistors -- Group H10D62/152 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D62/156 . Groups H10D62/152 and H10D62/156 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/153] Impurity concentrations or distributions -- H10D 62/153 covers the analysis, measurement, control, and specification of impurity levels and their spatial distribution within inorganic semiconductor devices, such as silicon or gallium arsenide components. It includes methods for detecting dopant homogeneity, contaminant profiling, and techniques to adjust or monitor impurity gradients during or after device fabrication.
[H10D 62/154] Dispositions -- H10D 62/154 falls under the CPC group for inorganic electric semiconductor devices and relates to constructional details concerning dispositions. It covers the arrangement, positioning and layout of semiconductor elements such as layers, contacts, and other structural features within the device. The classification is used to identify patents that describe how these components are positioned to achieve specific electrical functions.
[H10D 62/155] Shapes   cell layout of DMOS  H10D62/127 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10D 62/155 pertains to the specific constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices, focusing on the shapes and cell layout of DMOS structures. This classification is part of a detailed hierarchy under ELECTRICITY, SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES, and INORGANIC ELECTRIC SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES.
[H10D 62/156] Drain regions of DMOS transistors -- Group H10D62/156 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/152 . Groups H10D62/152 and H10D62/156 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/157] Impurity concentrations or distributions -- H10D 62/157 addresses the specification, measurement and control of impurity concentrations or their spatial distribution in inorganic electric semiconductor devices such as diodes, transistors or integrated circuits. It covers how dopants, contaminants or defects are introduced, profiled or managed to influence device performance.
[H10D 62/158] Dispositions -- The subclass H10D 62/158 addresses the way inorganic electric semiconductor devices are arranged and mounted, covering their positioning, packaging, interconnections, and overall placement within a circuit or system. It includes details on mounting methods, holder designs, bonding techniques, and spatial configuration to ensure proper electrical and thermal performance. This falls under constructional details for inorganic semiconductor components.
[H10D 62/159] Shapes -- H10D 62/159 concerns the overall shape and geometric configuration of inorganic semiconductor devices, covering the form of the crystal, die, or package and how it is arranged in planar, three-dimensional or stacked layouts. The entry addresses how these shapes influence device performance and integration within electronic assemblies.
[H10D 62/161] of FETs having Schottky gates -- H10D 62/161 covers the structural details of field-effect transistors in which the gate electrode is formed as a Schottky barrier metal contact, typically used to control carrier flow without a conventional oxide layer. The classification applies to inorganic semiconductor devices such as MOSFETs, MESFETs or JFETs that employ a metal-semiconductor junction for gate operation.
[H10D 62/165] Tunnel injectors -- Tunnel injectors are structural elements in inorganic semiconductor devices that enable carrier injection across a potential barrier by quantum tunneling, often used to improve speed and efficiency in high-frequency or high-power circuits. The H10D 62/165 subclass covers the detailed construction and integration of these injectors within semiconductor chips.
[H10D 62/17] Semiconductor regions connected to electrodes not carrying current to be rectified, amplified or switched, e.g. channel regions -- A semiconductor device where certain regions are linked to electrodes that do not carry the main rectifying, amplifying or switching current, such as channel or control regions, falls under this classification. It covers constructional details of inorganic semiconductor structures in which these non-current-carrying electrodes serve biasing, gating or other auxiliary functions.
[H10D 62/177] Base regions of bipolar transistors, e.g. BJTs or IGBTs -- This CPC subclass H10D 62/177 covers the design and construction of the base region in bipolar transistors such as BJTs and IGBTs. It falls within electricity, semiconductor devices, inorganic electric semiconductor devices and constructional details, focusing on the base's material composition, geometry and doping.
[H10D 62/184] of lateral BJTs -- The H10D 62/184 subclass covers constructional details of lateral bipolar-junction transistors, an inorganic semiconductor device in which the emitter, base and collector are arranged side-by-side on the same substrate. It includes features such as geometry, doping profiles, contact arrangements and integration methods specific to the lateral BJT layout.
[H10D 62/192] Base regions of thyristors -- The H10D 62/192 subclass covers the structural design of the base regions in thyristors, which are the intermediate semiconductor layers between the p-type and n-type zones that control the device's break-over voltage and current-carrying capability. It includes specifications of doping concentration, geometry and material composition used to achieve desired switching characteristics.
[H10D 62/199] Anode base regions of thyristors -- This CPC subclass covers the design, composition and manufacturing of the anode base region in thyristors, including doping, geometry and material choices that affect forward voltage drop and reverse blocking capability. It pertains to inorganic semiconductor devices used in power control.
[H10D 62/206] Cathode base regions of thyristors -- The H10D 62/206 entry covers the constructional details of the cathode base region in thyristors, a type of inorganic power semiconductor. It includes design, geometry, doping and manufacturing approaches for the cathode side that affect the device's blocking and switching characteristics.
[H10D 62/213] Channel regions of field-effect devices -- H10D 62/213 covers the structural and material features of the channel region in field-effect semiconductor devices. It addresses the geometry, doping profiles and layer configurations that define the conductive path between source and drain in inorganic electric transistors such as MOSFETs and JFETs. This subclass is used to classify patents dealing with the design and fabrication of those channel sections.
[H10D 62/221] of FETs -- The CPC subclass H10D 62/221 covers the structural features of field-effect transistors, including the design of source, drain and gate regions, channel geometry, insulating layers and contact arrangements. It pertains to inorganic semiconductor devices and addresses constructional details that affect the electrical performance and manufacturing of FETs.
[H10D 62/228] having delta-doped channels -- H10D 62/228 covers constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that employ delta-doped channels, where an ultra-thin dopant layer creates a highly conductive region within the semiconductor crystal. The subclass pertains to the design and fabrication aspects of such channel structures.
[H10D 62/235] of IGFETs  IGFETs having buried channels  H10D30/637 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10D 62/235 pertains to the constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices, specifically focusing on Insulated Gate Field-Effect Transistors (IGFETs) with buried channels. This classification is part of a broader hierarchy that includes electricity and semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/292] Non-planar channels of IGFETs  resulting from the gate electrode dispositions, e.g. within trenches  H10D64/512 -- H10D 62/292 covers insulated-gate field-effect transistors whose channel is formed in a non-planar shape, typically created by gate electrode arrangements such as trench or buried-gate structures that shape the conductive path. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices → constructional details, and references the more specific subclass H10D 64/512.
[H10D 62/299] having lateral doping variations  IGFETs having lightly doped source or drain extensions  H10D30/601 -- Group H10D62/299 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D62/314 and H10D84/0156 . Groups H10D62/314 , H10D84/0156 and H10D62/299 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/307] the doping variations being parallel to the channel lengths -- The H10D 62/307 subclass covers constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices in which the dopant concentration varies parallel to the channel length. It pertains to semiconductor structures such as transistors where the doping profile is intentionally non-uniform along the direction of current flow to modify electrical characteristics. This subclass is nested under electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/314] having vertical doping variations   vertical IGFETs  H10D30/63 -- Group H10D62/314 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D62/299 . Groups H10D62/314 and H10D62/299 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/328] having PN junction gates -- H10D 62/328 classifies inorganic electric semiconductor devices whose construction includes gates formed by p-n junctions. This subclass is used for patents describing the structural implementation of such PN-junction gated components within broader semiconductor device technologies.
[H10D 62/335] of charge-coupled devices -- The H10D 62/335 subclass covers the structural and constructional aspects of charge-coupled devices, detailing their architecture, layout and manufacturing features within inorganic semiconductor devices. It includes descriptions of pixel arrays, transfer registers, insulating layers and other physical elements that define CCD operation and integration.
[H10D 62/343] Gate regions of field-effect devices having PN junction gates -- This subclass covers the structural design of the gate region in field-effect devices whose gates are formed by pn-junctions, detailing the placement, dopant profiles and geometry of the junction within the transistor.
[H10D 62/351] Substrate regions of field-effect devices -- H10D 62/351 covers the design and engineering of substrate regions in field-effect semiconductor devices, such as MOSFETs and JFETs. It includes the definition, doping, isolation and structuring of the silicon or other inorganic substrate that forms the channel, source, drain and surrounding areas, often addressing epitaxial layers, wells and trenches.
[H10D 62/357] of FETs -- H10D 62/357 covers constructional details of field-effect transistors, such as the geometry of the channel, gate structure, source and drain contacts, and methods of forming these elements in inorganic semiconductor devices. It falls under the CPC hierarchy electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/364] of IGFETs -- Within the CPC, H10D 62/364 falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and addresses constructional details of insulated-gate field-effect transistors, including gate electrode layouts, channel doping, isolation structures and related manufacturing features.
[H10D 62/371] Inactive supplementary semiconductor regions, e.g. for preventing punch-through, improving capacity effect or leakage current -- The entry H10D 62/371 covers semiconductor structures that incorporate inactive supplementary regions-areas deliberately left non-conductive-to block punch-through, enhance capacitance effects, or lower leakage currents in inorganic electric semiconductor devices. These regions are part of the device's constructional details rather than its active functioning zones.
[H10D 62/378] Contact regions to the substrate regions -- Group H10D62/378 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D64/529 . Groups H10D62/378 and H10D64/529 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/386] of charge-coupled devices -- H10D 62/386 addresses the constructional details of charge-coupled devices, a class of inorganic electric semiconductor components that transfer charge across a semiconductor substrate for imaging and signal processing applications. This entry falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/393] Body regions of DMOS transistors or IGBTs   cell layout of DMOS  H10D62/127 -- This subclass defines the geometric and functional layout of the body regions in double-diffused MOS (DMOS) transistors and insulated-gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs), focusing on how the individual cells are arranged and connected. It covers the design of diffusion zones, field plates, and other structural features that determine the device's electrical performance and isolation.
[H10D 62/40] Crystalline structures -- H10D 62/40 covers the constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that involve crystalline structures, such as the arrangement, lattice parameters, defects and growth methods of the crystal material used in the device. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Inorganic Electric Semiconductor Devices.
[H10D 62/402] Amorphous materials -- H10D 62/402 covers the design and manufacture of inorganic semiconductor devices that use amorphous materials, focusing on structural features such as layers, films and interfaces. It includes methods for forming and patterning amorphous semiconductor layers and the related device architectures.
[H10D 62/405] Orientations of crystalline planes -- It pertains to the orientation of crystalline planes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices, describing how the alignment of specific lattice planes is selected, controlled or specified during device construction to influence electrical properties and fabrication processes.
[H10D 62/50] Physical imperfections -- H10D 62/50 covers the structural and material flaws in inorganic electric semiconductor devices, such as cracks, voids, inclusions, surface roughness or lattice defects that arise during manufacturing or operation. The code is used to classify documents describing how these physical imperfections influence device performance, reliability or failure modes.
[H10D 62/53] the imperfections being within the semiconductor body -- H10D 62/53 covers the presence and handling of internal imperfections in inorganic semiconductor bodies, including defects, impurities, dislocations or voids that affect device performance. It addresses methods for detecting, characterising or mitigating such imperfections in the construction of semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/57] the imperfections being on the surface of the semiconductor body, e.g. the body having a roughened surface -- The classification H10D 62/57 covers semiconductor devices whose bodies have surface imperfections, such as a deliberately roughened or textured surface, that affect electrical performance or fabrication. It includes methods and structures that exploit or manage these surface features for improved device characteristics. This subgroup falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices within the broader electricity and semiconductor device categories.
[H10D 62/60] Impurity distributions or concentrations -- H10D 62/60 covers methods, apparatuses and analyses related to the spatial distribution and concentration of impurities in inorganic semiconductor devices such as silicon or germanium chips. It includes techniques for measuring, controlling and modelling dopant profiles that affect device performance and reliability.
[H10D 62/605] Planar doped, e.g. atomic-plane doped or delta-doped -- The entry H10D 62/605 covers inorganic semiconductor devices in which dopants are confined to a single atomic plane or ultra-thin layer, often called planar or delta-doping. It includes structures and processes that create these precisely doped layers to tailor electrical characteristics such as carrier concentration and mobility. This classification pertains to constructional details of such planar-doped semiconductor components.
[H10D 62/80] characterised by the materials -- When classifying in this group, constituents of a material are considered irrespective of any dopants or other impurities. In this group: groups H10D62/81 - H10D62/8181 , covering quantum or superlattice structures, take precedence over groups H10D62/82 - H10D62/8281 , covering heterojunctions; groups H10D62/82 - H10D62/8281 , covering heterojunctions, take precedence over groups H10D62/83 - H10D62/883 , covering other materials; groups H10D62/881 - H10D62/883 , covering two-dimensional materials, take precedence over groups H10D62/83 - H10D62/875 , covering other materials. Group H10D62/80 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/81 . Group H10D62/80 is also impacted by reclassification into groups H10D62/82 , H10D62/8271 , H10D62/8281 , H10D62/871 , H10D62/874 , H10D62/875 , H10D62/881 and H10D62/883 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/81] of structures exhibiting quantum-confinement effects, e.g. single quantum wells -- Group H10D62/81 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D62/80 . Groups H10D62/81 and H10D62/80 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 62/812] Single quantum well structures -- H10D 62/812 covers inorganic semiconductor devices that incorporate a single quantum-well layer, focusing on the constructional details of the well's geometry, materials and interfaces. It pertains to devices where charge carriers are confined in one dimension to tailor electronic or optical behavior.
[H10D 62/813] Quantum wire structures -- The CPC subclass H10D 62/813 relates to constructional details of quantum-wire structures used in inorganic electric semiconductor devices. It covers the design, fabrication and geometrical features of one-dimensional nanowire elements that guide charge transport within semiconductor circuits.
[H10D 62/814] Quantum box structures -- Quantum box structures are three-dimensional semiconductor nanostructures that confine charge carriers in all spatial directions, enabling discrete energy levels and quantum-size effects for devices such as lasers, detectors and transistors. The classification covers the design, layer arrangements, material compositions and fabrication techniques used to create these confined regions within inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/815] of structures having periodic or quasi-periodic potential variation, e.g. superlattices or multiple quantum wells [MQW] -- The code H10D 62/815 classifies inorganic semiconductor devices whose internal potential varies periodically or quasi-periodically, such as superlattices and multiple quantum-well structures. It covers the design and construction of these layered semiconductor architectures that are engineered to modify electronic and optical behavior.
[H10D 62/8161] potential variation due to variations in composition or crystallinity, e.g. heterojunction superlattices  lateral superlattices, lateral surface superlattices  H10D62/8181 -- H10D 62/8161 pertains to the constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices, specifically addressing potential variations caused by differences in composition or crystallinity, such as those found in heterojunction superlattices and lateral surface superlattices.
[H10D 62/8162] having quantum effects only in the vertical direction, i.e. layered structures having quantum effects solely resulting from vertical potential variation -- The code covers semiconductor devices whose quantum-mechanical behavior is confined solely along the vertical axis, typically in layered structures such as quantum wells, superlattices or vertically stacked stacks where the potential varies only with depth. These devices exploit vertical carrier confinement for functions like emission, detection or transport while remaining unquantized in the lateral dimensions.
[H10D 62/8163] comprising long-range structurally-disordered materials, e.g. one-dimensional vertical amorphous superlattices -- H10D 62/8163 relates to inorganic electric semiconductor devices that incorporate long-range structurally disordered materials, such as one-dimensional vertical amorphous superlattices, focusing on the constructional details of these semiconductor structures.
[H10D 62/8164] comprising only semiconductor materials   potential variation in long-range structurally-disordered materials  H10D62/8163 -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10D 62/8164 pertains to the constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices, specifically those comprising only semiconductor materials with a potential variation in long-range structurally-disordered materials. This classification is a subset of broader categories related to electricity and semiconductor devices.
[H10D 62/8171] Doping structures, e.g. doping superlattices or nipi superlattices -- H10D 62/8171 classifies the constructional details of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that employ doping structures, such as doping superlattices or NIPi superlattices, where layers of different dopant types are periodically arranged to modify carrier profiles and device performance.
[H10D 62/8181] Structures having no potential periodicity in the vertical direction, e.g. lateral superlattices or lateral surface superlattices [LSS] -- The CPC subclass H10D 62/8181 groups semiconductor constructions whose periodic structure is confined to the plane rather than the growth direction, meaning they lack vertical potential periodicity. It includes lateral superlattices and lateral surface superlattices, where compositional or doping variations are patterned horizontally to tailor electronic or optical behavior.

[H10D 64/00] Electrodes of devices having potential barriers -- The H10D 64/00 subgroup covers electrodes incorporated in inorganic semiconductor components that create or exploit potential-barrier junctions, such as p-n diodes, Schottky contacts, MOS structures and similar devices. It includes designs, materials, doping profiles and manufacturing methods for the electrodes that form the barrier region, influencing carrier injection, blocking, or control within the semiconductor element.
[H10D 64/01] Manufacture or treatment -- Processes or apparatus specially adapted for the manufacture or treatment of devices, or parts thereof, covered by class H10 , which are generically applicable to these devices are covered by subclass H10P

All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

[H10D 64/0113] the conductive layers comprising highly doped semiconductor materials, e.g. polysilicon layers or amorphous silicon layers -- This subclass covers conductive layers formed from highly doped inorganic semiconductor materials, such as polysilicon or amorphous silicon, that serve as electrodes in devices containing potential barriers.

[H10D 64/01302] the insulator being formed after the semiconductor body, the semiconductor being silicon -- Groups H10D64/01302 - H10D64/01354 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/011 .
