[H10D 64/01304] characterised by the conductor -- The subclass covers inorganic semiconductor devices with potential-barrier electrodes whose defining feature is the conductor material or structure, such as conductive layers that also form the barrier. It includes designs where the electrode is characterised by the conducting element rather than by a separate barrier layer.
[H10D 64/01306] the conductor comprising a layer of silicon contacting the insulator, e.g. polysilicon -- The code H10D 64/01306 covers electrodes in semiconductor devices where the conducting element includes a silicon layer that directly contacts an insulating material, typically realized as a polysilicon film. This configuration is used in devices that rely on potential-barrier structures, such as diodes or transistors, to control charge flow.
[H10D 64/01308] the conductor further comprising a non-elemental silicon additional conductive layer, e.g. a metal silicide layer formed by the reaction of silicon with an implanted metal -- This CPC subclass covers electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices that include a non-elemental silicon conductive layer, typically a metal silicide formed by reacting silicon with an implanted metal. The additional layer serves to improve conductivity and barrier properties of the device's electrodes.
[H10D 64/0131] the additional conductive layer comprising a silicide layer formed by the silicidation reaction between the layer of silicon with a metal layer which is not formed by metal implantation -- The subclass H10D 64/0131 covers semiconductor devices whose electrodes include an extra conductive layer made of a silicide formed by reacting a silicon layer with a metal layer that was not introduced by ion implantation. This silicide layer provides a low-resistance contact without requiring implanted metal species.
[H10D 64/01312] the additional layer comprising a metal or metal silicide formed by deposition, i.e. without a silicidation reaction, e.g. sputter deposition -- This subclass covers electrodes that include an extra metal or metal-silicide layer created by a physical deposition process such as sputtering, rather than by a silicidation reaction. The layer is added to devices with potential-barrier electrodes in inorganic semiconductor components.
[H10D 64/01314] the conductor comprising a layer of Ge, C or of compounds of Si, Ge or C contacting the insulator -- This CPC subclass covers electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices where the conductive element is a layer of germanium, carbon or silicon-germanium-carbon compounds that directly contacts the insulating material. It applies to structures that form potential barriers, such as diode or transistor contacts, where the described material stack forms the interface between the conductor and the insulator.
[H10D 64/01316] the conductor comprising a layer of elemental metal contacting the insulator, e.g. Ta, W, Mo or Al -- The classification code H10D 64/01316 pertains to electrodes in semiconductor devices with potential barriers, specifically detailing conductors that include a layer of elemental metal such as tantalum, tungsten, molybdenum, or aluminum contacting an insulator. This entry is part of the broader categories of Electricity and Inorganic Electric Semiconductor Devices within the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[H10D 64/01318] the conductor comprising a layer of alloy material, compound material or organic material contacting the insulator, e.g. TiN  comprising a layer of alloys of Si, Ge or C  H10D64/01314 -- The classification code H10D 64/01318 pertains to electrodes in semiconductor devices with potential barriers, specifically detailing conductors that include a layer of alloy, compound, or organic material contacting the insulator, such as TiN, and may also involve layers of alloys containing Si, Ge, or C.
[H10D 64/0132] the conductor being a metallic silicide -- The entry H10D 64/0132 covers electrodes of inorganic electric semiconductor devices in which the conductive element is a metallic silicide. It pertains to barrier-type semiconductor structures where the silicide serves as the electrode material, providing low resistance contact and thermal stability.
[H10D 64/01322] the conductor contacting the insulator having a lateral variation in doping, composition or deposition steps -- This subclass covers semiconductor devices in which the conductor that contacts the insulating layer is deliberately varied laterally in doping level, material composition, or deposition steps, creating localized potential-barrier regions that affect the electrical behavior of the device.
[H10D 64/01324] characterised by the sectional shape, e.g. T or inverted-T -- This subclass concerns electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices that incorporate potential-barrier structures and are defined by a T- or inverted-T-shaped cross-section. The sectional geometry is used to shape the electric field and improve the device's barrier and performance characteristics.
[H10D 64/01326] Aspects related to lithography, isolation or planarisation of the conductor -- The code H10D 64/01326 covers techniques for lithography, isolation and planarisation of conductors in electrodes of inorganic semiconductor devices that contain potential barriers, such as those used in power or high-frequency components. It falls under the hierarchy Electricity, semiconductor devices, inorganic electric semiconductor devices, electrodes of devices having potential barriers.
[H10D 64/01328] by defining the conductor using a sidewall spacer mask, a transformation under a mask or a plating at a sidewall -- H10D 64/01328 covers processes for creating electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices in which the conductor pattern is defined by a side-wall spacer mask, transformed under that mask, or formed by plating on the sidewall. The focus is on using masked sidewalls to shape or deposit the conductive material that forms the device's potential-barrier electrode.
[H10D 64/0133] at least part of the entire electrode being a sidewall spacer, being formed by transformation under a mask or being formed by plating at a sidewall -- H10D 64/0133 refers to electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices where at least part of the whole electrode functions as a sidewall spacer. The sidewall spacer is produced either by a transformation process using a mask or by depositing metal or conductive material onto the sidewall.
[H10D 64/01332] Making the insulator -- This subclass covers methods for producing the insulating layer that separates the metallic contact from the active semiconductor in electrodes of inorganic semiconductor devices with potential barriers. Techniques include deposition, formation, or treatment of dielectric films to control leakage and barrier properties. The processes are intended to improve device performance and reliability.
[H10D 64/01334] by defining the insulator using a sidewall spacer mask, a transformation under a mask or a plating at a sidewall -- The H10D 64/01334 subgroup covers electrode formation in inorganic electric semiconductor devices where the insulating region is defined by a side-wall spacer mask, enabling a patterned transformation under the mask or metal plating directly on the sidewall. It addresses processes that shape or modify electrodes using sidewall-defined insulators to create potential-barrier structures.
[H10D 64/01336] on single crystalline silicon, e.g. chemical oxidation using a liquid -- The entry covers techniques for creating barrier layers on electrodes made of single-crystalline silicon, particularly by applying a liquid-phase chemical oxidation. It relates to inorganic semiconductor devices where such barrier formation improves device performance, and it falls under the broader group concerning electrodes of devices having potential barriers.
[H10D 64/01338] with a treatment, e.g. annealing, after the formation of the conductor -- This subcategory covers inorganic semiconductor devices with barrier-type electrodes and specifies post-formation treatments of the conductor, such as annealing or similar processes aimed at modifying the electrode's structure or performance.
[H10D 64/0134] with a treatment, e.g. annealing, after the formation of the insulator and before the formation of the conductor -- The entry covers semiconductor electrodes in inorganic electric devices where, after the insulating layer is formed, the electrode structure undergoes a treatment such as annealing before the conductive layer is added. The post-insulator, pre-conductor processing aims to modify the barrier properties or improve device performance.
[H10D 64/01342] by deposition, e.g. evaporation, ALD or laser deposition  H10D64/01344  takes precedence -- The subclass concerns electrodes of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that are produced by deposition methods such as evaporation, atomic-layer deposition or laser deposition, and it is overridden by the more specific subclass H10D64/01344.
[H10D 64/01344] in a nitrogen-containing ambient, e.g. N 2 O oxidation -- This subgroup deals with processes where the electrode of an inorganic semiconductor device is exposed to a nitrogen-containing atmosphere, such as nitrous oxide, to carry out oxidation or other surface modifications that affect the potential barrier. It includes methods that use N2, N2O, or similar gases during fabrication or treatment steps to tailor electrical characteristics.
[H10D 64/01346] in a gaseous ambient using an oxygen or a water vapour, e.g. oxidation through a layer  H10D64/01344  takes precedence -- This subclass covers electrodes of inorganic semiconductor devices that are formed or altered by oxidation in a gaseous environment containing oxygen or water vapour, such as oxidation through a surface layer. It specifies processes where the ambient gas drives the creation or modification of the electrode's potential-barrier characteristics.
[H10D 64/01348] with substrate doping, e.g. N, Ge or C implantation, before formation of the insulator -- This subgroup covers electrodes of inorganic electric semiconductor devices where the substrate is doped-by nitrogen, germanium or carbon implantation-before the insulating layer is formed, altering the device's potential-barrier characteristics.
[H10D 64/0135] by deposition of a layer, e.g. metal, metal compound or polysilicon, followed by transformation thereof into the insulator -- The CPC subclass H10D 64/0135 covers electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices that are formed by first depositing a layer such as a metal, metal compound or polysilicon and then converting that layer into an insulating material, typically by oxidation or similar transformation. This approach creates a potential-barrier layer that isolates or passivates the electrode within the device.
[H10D 64/01352] with sacrificial oxide -- The code H10D 64/01352 refers to electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices with potential barriers that are fabricated using a sacrificial oxide layer. The classification applies to structures where the oxide is temporarily formed during processing and later removed or transformed as part of device production.
[H10D 64/01354] passivation or protection of the electrode, e.g. using re-oxidation -- This class covers methods for protecting or passivating electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices, especially those with potential barriers. Techniques such as re-oxidation of the electrode surface are included to improve stability and performance.
[H10D 64/01356] the insulator being formed after the semiconductor body, the semiconductor being a Group IV material and not being silicon, e.g. Ge, SiGe or SiGeC  H10D64/01364 ,  H10D64/01366  take precedence -- Group H10D64/01356 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/011 . Groups H10D64/011 and H10D64/01356 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/01358] the insulator being formed after the semiconductor body, the semiconductor being a Group III-V material -- Group H10D64/01358 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/011 . Groups H10D64/011 and H10D64/01358 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/0136] characterised by the sectional shape, e.g. T or inverted-T -- Group H10D64/0136 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D64/011 and H10D64/0125 . Groups H10D64/011 , H10D64/0125 and H10D64/0136 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/01362] asymmetrical sectional shape -- Group H10D64/01362 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D64/011 and H10D64/0126 . Groups H10D64/011 , H10D64/0126 and H10D64/01362 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/01364] the semiconductor being diamond, semiconducting diamond-like carbon or graphene -- Group H10D64/01364 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D64/011 and H10D64/0114 . Groups H10D64/011 , H10D64/0114 and H10D64/01364 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/01366] the semiconductor being silicon carbide -- Group H10D64/01366 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D64/011 and H10D64/0115 . Groups H10D64/011 , H10D64/0115 and H10D64/01366 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/015] removing at least parts of gate spacers, e.g. disposable spacers -- This CPC subclass concerns techniques for eliminating portions of gate spacers, including removable or disposable spacers, during the fabrication of inorganic semiconductor devices that use potential-barrier electrodes. It is categorized under H10D 64/015 within the electricity-semiconductor device hierarchy.
[H10D 64/017] using dummy gates in processes wherein at least parts of the final gates are self-aligned to the dummy gates, i.e. replacement gate processes -- Group H10D64/017 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D30/0273 . Groups H10D30/0273 and H10D64/017 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/018] Spacers formed inside holes at the prospective gate locations, e.g. holes left by removing dummy gates -- This CPC subclass covers methods and structures in which spacers are formed inside recessed holes at prospective gate locations, typically after dummy gates are removed, to define gate spacing, control stress and improve isolation in inorganic semiconductor electrodes that contain potential barriers.
[H10D 64/021] using multiple gate spacer layers, e.g. bilayered sidewall spacers -- H10D 64/021 covers semiconductor devices whose electrodes incorporate multiple gate spacer layers, such as bilayered sidewall spacers, to modify the electric field or barrier characteristics at the gate region. The classification applies to inorganic electric semiconductor structures where these stacked spacers are used to improve performance or control of the device.
[H10D 64/025] forming recessed gates, e.g. by using local oxidation -- H10D 64/025 covers methods for forming recessed gates in inorganic semiconductor devices, such as those created by locally oxidizing the surface to define the gate region. The technique produces potential-barrier electrodes with a lowered gate structure that can improve device performance.
[H10D 64/027] by etching at gate locations -- This subclass covers semiconductor devices in which the electrode structure at the gate is formed or modified by etching processes at the gate locations, typically to create or adjust potential barriers in inorganic electric semiconductors.
[H10D 64/031] of data-storage electrodes -- This subclass concerns electrodes employed in data-storage devices, such as those in semiconductor memory or magnetic-recording components, where the electrode forms or incorporates a potential barrier to control charge flow. It falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and addresses the design, materials, and structures of such storage-electrode configurations.
[H10D 64/033] comprising ferroelectric layers -- The H10D 64/033 subclass covers inorganic semiconductor devices whose electrodes incorporate ferroelectric layers that form potential-barrier contacts, such as ferroelectric-gate field-effect transistors or memory cells where the ferroelectric material modulates charge transport across the electrode.
[H10D 64/035] comprising conductor-insulator-conductor-insulator-semiconductor structures -- The CPC subclass H10D 64/035 covers inorganic semiconductor devices whose electrodes are built from a conductor-insulator-conductor-insulator-semiconductor stack, creating potential-barrier contacts. It pertains to structures where alternating conductive and insulating layers are placed between the external conductors and the semiconductor active region, typical in certain diodes, sensors and capacitor designs.
[H10D 64/037] comprising charge-trapping insulators -- H10D 64/037 covers electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices that incorporate charge-trapping insulator layers to modulate potential barriers. By embedding an insulating material that can capture and release charge carriers, the electrode can adjust barrier height, improve switching behavior, and enhance device stability in applications such as diodes and transistors.
[H10D 64/111] Field plates -- Field plates are conductive electrodes placed adjacent to semiconductor junctions to modify the electric field distribution, lowering peak field intensity and enhancing breakdown voltage and reliability in power devices such as MOSFETs and IGBTs. They are classified under inorganic electric semiconductor devices with potential-barrier electrodes.
[H10D 64/112] comprising multiple field plate segments -- This subclass covers semiconductor electrodes that incorporate several field-plate segments to shape the electric field across a device with a potential barrier, such as power diodes or MOSFETs. The multiple segments allow more precise control of the barrier profile and improve breakdown voltage or reduce leakage.
[H10D 64/115] Resistive field plates, e.g. semi-insulating field plates -- Resistive field plates, such as semi-insulating plates, are conductive structures placed on or near semiconductor device surfaces to shape electric fields while providing high resistance to current flow, thereby reducing leakage and enhancing breakdown performance in devices that incorporate potential barriers.
[H10D 64/117] Recessed field plates, e.g. trench field plates or buried field plates -- H10D 64/117 refers to recessed field plates, such as trench-type or buried plates, incorporated in inorganic electric semiconductor devices to shape and control the electric field at potential-barrier electrodes, often improving breakdown voltage and device reliability.
[H10D 64/118] Electrodes comprising insulating layers having particular dielectric or electrostatic properties, e.g. having static charges -- H10D 64/118 covers electrodes that incorporate insulating layers designed to exhibit specific dielectric or electrostatic characteristics, such as retaining static charges. These layers modify the electrode's electrical behavior and are used in inorganic semiconductor devices where potential-barrier effects are important. The classification falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices → electrodes of devices having potential barriers.
[H10D 64/20] Electrodes characterised by their shapes, relative sizes or dispositions -- H10D 64/20 classifies electrodes used in inorganic electric semiconductor devices that contain potential barriers, specifically those whose functional characteristics are defined by their shape, relative size or spatial disposition within the device.
[H10D 64/205] Nanosized electrodes, e.g. nanowire electrodes -- This subclass covers nanoscale electrodes, such as nanowire electrodes, employed in inorganic semiconductor devices that incorporate potential barriers. The electrodes are designed with dimensions at the nanometer scale to improve electrical contact, field emission, or tunneling characteristics in devices like diodes and transistors.
[H10D 64/23] Electrodes carrying the current to be rectified, amplified, oscillated or switched, e.g. sources, drains, anodes or cathodes -- Group H10D64/23 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D64/232 . Groups H10D64/23 and H10D64/232 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/231] Emitter or collector electrodes for bipolar transistors -- This CPC subclass deals with emitter or collector electrodes used in bipolar junction transistors, focusing on their material composition, doping, geometry, and methods of forming electrical contacts with the semiconductor substrate. It addresses how these electrodes create and manage potential barriers essential for transistor operation.
[H10D 64/232] Emitter electrodes for IGBTs -- Group H10D64/232 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/23 . Groups H10D64/23 and H10D64/232 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/233] Cathode or anode electrodes for thyristors -- H10D 64/233 covers cathode or anode electrodes specifically designed for thyristors, which are inorganic semiconductor devices used in power-control circuits. The entry falls under the hierarchy ELECTRICITY → SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES → INORGANIC ELECTRIC SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES → Electrodes of devices having potential barriers. It addresses the material and structural features of the electrodes that enable the thyristor's switching behavior.
[H10D 64/251] Source or drain electrodes for field-effect devices -- H10D 64/251 covers source or drain electrodes used in field-effect transistors and similar devices built from inorganic semiconductor materials. The classification applies to electrode structures that form the charge-carrier injection or extraction contacts, including those with potential barriers at the semiconductor interface. These electrodes are part of the overall device architecture for controlling current flow.
[H10D 64/252] for vertical or pseudo-vertical devices -- Group H10D64/252 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D64/2523 and H10D64/2527 . Groups H10D64/252 , H10D64/2523 and H10D64/2527 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/2523] for vertical devices wherein the source or drain electrodes extend entirely through semiconductor bodies -- Group H10D64/2523 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/252 . Groups H10D64/252 and H10D64/2523 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/2527] for vertical devices wherein the source or drain electrodes are recessed in semiconductor bodies -- Group H10D64/2527 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D64/252 and H10D64/256 . Groups H10D64/252 , H10D64/256 and H10D64/2527 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/254] for lateral devices wherein the source or drain electrodes extend entirely through the semiconductor bodies, e.g. via-holes for back side contacts -- Group H10D64/254 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D64/256 - H10D64/2565 and H10D64/257 . Groups H10D64/254 , H10D64/256 - H10D64/2565 and H10D64/257 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/256] for lateral devices wherein the source or drain electrodes are recessed in semiconductor bodies  source or drain electrodes of TFTs  H10D30/673 -- Group H10D64/256 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D64/254 and H10D64/257 . Group H10D64/256 is also impacted by reclassification into group H10D64/2527 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/2565] wherein the source or drain regions are on a top side of the semiconductor bodies and the recessed source or drain electrodes are on a bottom side of the semiconductor bodies -- Group H10D64/2565 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/254 . Groups H10D64/254 and H10D64/2565 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/257] for lateral devices wherein the source or drain electrodes are characterised by top-view geometrical layouts, e.g. interdigitated, semi-circular, annular or L-shaped electrodes  source or drain electrodes of TFTs  H10D30/673 -- Group H10D64/257 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/254 . Group H10D64/257 is also impacted by reclassification into group H10D64/256 . Groups H10D64/254 , H10D64/257 and H10D64/256 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/258] characterised by the relative positions of the source or drain electrodes with respect to the gate electrode -- H10D 64/258 covers semiconductor devices whose source or drain electrodes are positioned relative to the gate electrode in a specific manner, affecting the device's electrical characteristics. This subclass falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and pertains to electrodes with potential barriers.
[H10D 64/259] Source or drain electrodes being self-aligned with the gate electrode and having bottom surfaces higher than the interface between the channel and the gate dielectric -- H10D 64/259 pertains to semiconductor devices whose source or drain electrodes are formed by a self-aligned process with the gate electrode, and whose bottom surfaces rise above the interface between the channel and the gate dielectric, providing improved electrical connection and reduced parasitic resistance.
[H10D 64/27] Electrodes not carrying the current to be rectified, amplified, oscillated or switched, e.g. gates -- The subclass H10D 64/27 covers control electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices that do not carry the main rectified, amplified, oscillated or switched current, such as gate electrodes. These electrodes create a potential barrier to modulate carrier flow without conducting the primary signal itself.
[H10D 64/281] Base electrodes for bipolar transistors -- H10D 64/281 covers base electrode structures used in bipolar transistors, focusing on the design and materials of the electrode that contacts the transistor's base region to form the necessary potential barrier. The classification falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and relates to electrodes that control charge flow in bipolar junction devices.
[H10D 64/291] Gate electrodes for thyristors -- Gate electrodes for thyristors are specialized semiconductor contacts that control the triggering of the device by injecting charge carriers into the potential barrier region. The classification covers their material composition, geometry, doping and integration methods designed to initiate and regulate conduction in power-control thyristor switches.
[H10D 64/311] Gate electrodes for field-effect devices -- This subclass covers gate electrodes used in field-effect transistors, detailing the materials, geometry, doping and work-function characteristics that form the controlling electrode of the channel. It includes designs for MOSFETs, JFETs and other inorganic semiconductor devices where the gate creates a potential barrier.
[H10D 64/411] for FETs -- This subclass covers electrodes designed for field-effect transistors, including source, drain and gate contacts that create potential barriers, as well as the materials, structures, doping levels and fabrication methods used in inorganic semiconductor FETs. It falls under electrodes of devices having potential barriers in the inorganic electric semiconductor devices section of the CPC.
[H10D 64/511] for IGFETs -- H10D 64/511 covers electrode designs and materials used in insulated-gate field-effect transistors (IGFETs), including source, drain, and gate contacts that form the device's potential barriers. It addresses configurations that influence charge injection, extraction, and overall transistor performance.
[H10D 64/512] Disposition of the gate electrodes, e.g. buried gates -- H10D 64/512 addresses the structural arrangement of gate electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices that create potential barriers, focusing on how the gates are positioned to control device operation. It includes configurations such as buried gates that are embedded within the substrate.
[H10D 64/513] within recesses in the substrate, e.g. trench gates, groove gates or buried gates -- H10D 64/513 covers electrodes that are formed inside recesses of the semiconductor substrate, such as trench, groove or buried gates, used in inorganic electric semiconductor devices. These recessed electrodes create potential barriers within the device and are common in advanced power and high-frequency components.
[H10D 64/514] characterised by the insulating layers -- This CPC subclass covers inorganic semiconductor devices whose electrodes are defined or isolated by insulating layers that create potential barriers controlling charge flow. It includes structures where the insulating material is integral to the electrode architecture, affecting the device's electrical performance.
[H10D 64/516] the thicknesses being non-uniform -- H10D 64/516 pertains to inorganic semiconductor devices that include electrodes forming potential barriers whose thickness is intentionally non-uniform across the electrode surface. The classification captures designs where varying thicknesses are used to influence electric field distribution, carrier injection or heat dissipation in the device.
[H10D 64/517] characterised by the conducting layers -- This CPC subclass covers inorganic semiconductor devices whose electrodes are distinguished by the nature of their conducting layers, including material composition, doping or structure that create the potential barriers.
[H10D 64/518] characterised by their lengths or sectional shapes -- The subgroup H10D 64/518 covers semiconductor electrodes whose operation is defined by their physical dimensions, specifically the length or cross-sectional shape of the electrode, which affect the potential barrier characteristics of the device. It includes designs where size or shape is a primary defining feature of the electrode.
[H10D 64/519] characterised by their top-view geometrical layouts -- This subclass concerns inorganic electric semiconductor devices with barrier electrodes whose planar geometry is defined by the arrangement of features when viewed from above, emphasizing the top-view layout of the electrode pattern. It applies to devices such as diodes, transistors and similar structures where the top-view geometry of the electrodes influences electrical behavior.
[H10D 64/529] Electrodes for IGFETs contacting substrate regions, e.g. for grounding or preventing parasitic effects -- Group H10D64/529 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D62/378 . Groups H10D62/378 and H10D64/529 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/60] Electrodes characterised by their materials -- H10D 64/60 relates to electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices where the distinguishing feature is the material composition of the electrode, such as specific metals, metal oxides, doped semiconductors or other conductive compounds. It applies to devices that incorporate potential barriers at the electrode interface and classifies them by material rather than shape or size.
[H10D 64/602] Heterojunction gate electrodes for FETs -- The code H10D 64/602 falls under electricity semiconductor devices inorganic electric semiconductor devices electrodes of devices having potential barriers. It relates to heterojunction gate electrodes for field-effect transistors, where the gate forms a heterojunction with the semiconductor channel to modulate carrier flow.
[H10D 64/605] Source, drain, or gate electrodes for FETs comprising highly resistive materials -- This class covers source, drain or gate electrodes of field-effect transistors that are formed from highly resistive materials, such as wide-bandgap oxides or lightly doped semiconductors, which provide controlled leakage and isolation in the device.
[H10D 64/608] being superconducting -- H10D 64/608 covers electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices that operate as superconductors, providing zero-resistance contacts for devices with potential barriers such as Josephson junctions.
[H10D 64/62] Electrodes ohmically coupled to a semiconductor -- H10D 64/62 covers semiconductor devices where the electrodes form an ohmic, low-resistance contact to the semiconductor material. The classification includes designs and structures that ensure linear current flow without rectifying behavior, typically used in inorganic electric semiconductor devices with potential barriers such as diodes, transistors and similar components.
[H10D 64/64] Electrodes comprising a Schottky barrier to a semiconductor -- The entry covers electrodes that form a Schottky barrier with a semiconductor, i.e., metal-semiconductor contacts where a rectifying potential barrier is created at the interface. It applies to devices such as Schottky diodes and other structures that rely on the barrier for charge carrier control.
[H10D 64/647] Schottky drain or source electrodes for IGFETs -- A Schottky drain or source electrode for an insulated-gate field-effect transistor (IGFET) is a metal-semiconductor junction that forms a low-forward-voltage barrier, improving switching speed and reducing leakage compared with conventional doped silicon contacts. This electrode type is classified under CPC H10D 64/647 within inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 64/649] Schottky drain or source electrodes for FETs having rectifying junction gate electrodes -- H10D 64/649 covers field-effect transistors where the drain or source electrode is a Schottky contact, forming a metal-semiconductor rectifying junction, used in conjunction with a gate that also has a rectifying junction. The class is part of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that employ electrodes with potential barriers.
[H10D 64/66] Electrodes having a conductor capacitively coupled to a semiconductor by an insulator, e.g. MIS electrodes -- This subclass covers electrodes where a metal or other conductor is separated from the semiconductor by an insulating layer, forming a metal-insulator-semiconductor (MIS) structure that provides capacitive coupling. Such configurations are used to control charge injection, improve reliability, and enable threshold voltage adjustment in various semiconductor devices.
[H10D 64/661] the conductor comprising a layer of silicon contacting the insulator, e.g. polysilicon having vertical doping variation  having lateral variation in the gate structure  H10D64/671 -- H10D 64/661 pertains to electrodes in inorganic electric semiconductor devices where the conductor includes a silicon layer that directly contacts an insulating layer. It focuses on configurations such as polysilicon electrodes that feature vertical doping gradients and lateral variations in the gate structure to tailor device performance.
[H10D 64/662] the conductor further comprising additional layers, e.g. multiple silicon layers having different crystal structures -- The code H10D 64/662 pertains to electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices that incorporate a conductor made of several stacked layers, such as multiple silicon layers each having a distinct crystal structure, to improve electrical performance.
[H10D 64/663] the additional layers comprising a silicide layer contacting the layer of silicon, e.g. polycide gates -- A semiconductor electrode structure in which a silicide layer is formed directly on a silicon layer, typically as a polycide gate, providing a low-resistance contact and controlling the potential barrier at the interface. This applies to inorganic electric semiconductor devices that use such barrier-forming electrodes.
[H10D 64/664] the additional layers comprising a barrier layer between the layer of silicon and an upper metal or metal silicide layer -- The CPC entry H10D 64/664 covers semiconductor devices where a barrier layer is placed between the silicon region and an overlying metal or metal-silicide contact. It relates to additional layers that prevent interdiffusion, improve adhesion, or act as diffusion barriers in inorganic electric semiconductor electrodes.
[H10D 64/665] the conductor comprising a layer of elemental metal contacting the insulator, e.g. tungsten or molybdenum  having lateral variation  H10D64/671 -- H10D 64/665 pertains to semiconductor devices with potential barriers, specifically addressing electrodes that feature a layer of elemental metal, such as tungsten or molybdenum, in direct contact with an insulator, characterized by lateral variations.
[H10D 64/666] the conductor further comprising additional layers -- H10D 64/666 covers electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices where the conductor incorporates additional layers, such as barrier, passivation or adhesion coatings, to modify electrical, mechanical or chemical performance.
[H10D 64/667] the conductor comprising a layer of alloy material, compound material or organic material contacting the insulator, e.g. TiN workfunction layers  having lateral variation  H10D64/671 -- Group H10D64/667 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D64/669 . Groups H10D64/667 and H10D64/669 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/668] the layer being a silicide, e.g. TiSi 2 -- Group H10D64/668 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D64/669 . Groups H10D64/668 and H10D64/669 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/669] the conductor further comprising additional layers of alloy material, compound material or organic material, e.g. TaN/TiAlN -- Group H10D64/669 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D64/667 and H10D64/668 . Groups H10D64/667 , H10D64/668 and H10D64/669 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/671] the conductor having lateral variation in doping or structure -- Group H10D64/671 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D64/675 . Groups H10D64/671 and H10D64/675 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/675] Gate sidewall spacers -- Group H10D64/675 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D64/671 . Groups H10D64/671 and H10D64/675 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 64/679] comprising air gaps -- H10D 64/679 covers semiconductor devices whose electrodes incorporate one or more air gaps, providing a physical separation that forms a potential barrier within the device. The classification applies to inorganic semiconductor structures such as diodes or transistors that use an air gap as part of the electrode design to influence electrical characteristics.
[H10D 64/68] characterised by the insulator, e.g. by the gate insulator -- This subclass concerns inorganic semiconductor devices whose electrodes incorporate a potential-barrier and are defined by the presence of an insulating layer, such as a gate insulator, that separates the electrode from the semiconductor. It is used for devices where the insulator is the principal feature distinguishing the electrode structure.
[H10D 64/681] having a compositional variation, e.g. multilayered -- H10D 64/681 covers electrodes in inorganic semiconductor devices that incorporate compositional variations, such as multilayered structures, to create or modify potential barriers at the electrode interface. The classification applies to designs where different materials are stacked or graded to influence electrical performance.
[H10D 64/683] being parallel to the channel plane -- This subclass covers inorganic semiconductor devices whose electrodes are arranged so that their surfaces run parallel to the plane of the current-carrying channel. It applies to structures where the electrode orientation affects the potential-barrier properties, typically in field-effect or similar devices.
[H10D 64/685] being perpendicular to the channel plane -- H10D 64/685 covers inorganic semiconductor devices with potential-barrier electrodes that are arranged perpendicular to the channel plane. The classification is used for patents where the electrode orientation is orthogonal to the direction of current flow within the channel.
[H10D 64/687] having cavities, e.g. porous gate dielectrics having gasses therein -- H10D 64/687 classifies semiconductor devices whose electrodes contain cavities, such as porous gate dielectrics that retain gases. It falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices with potential-barrier electrodes.
[H10D 64/689] having ferroelectric layers -- H10D 64/689 covers inorganic semiconductor devices whose electrodes incorporate a ferroelectric layer, using the material's reversible polarization to modify barrier properties or store charge. Such structures are used in memory cells, switches and sensors that exploit the field-controlled behavior of the ferroelectric film.
[H10D 64/691] comprising metallic compounds, e.g. metal oxides or metal silicates   insulators comprising nitrogen  H10D64/693 -- The classification code H10D 64/691 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the EPO and USPTO, pertains to electrodes of inorganic electric semiconductor devices with potential barriers, specifically those that include metallic compounds such as metal oxides or silicates. This entry also covers insulators containing nitrogen, as detailed under H10D 64/693.
[H10D 64/693] the insulator comprising nitrogen, e.g. nitrides, oxynitrides or nitrogen-doped materials -- H10D 64/693 concerns inorganic electric semiconductor devices whose electrodes contain a nitrogen-based insulator, such as nitrides, oxynitrides or nitrogen-doped materials, employed to form potential barriers within the device. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of electricity, semiconductor devices and inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 8/00] Diodes -- In this group, when the manufacture or treatment of a device is determined to be novel and non-obvious, the device itself is also classified. Group H10D8/00 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D8/20 . Groups H10D8/00 and H10D8/20 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 80/00] Integrated devices -- Group H10D80/00 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W90/00 . Groups H10W90/00 and H10D80/00 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/01] Manufacture or treatment -- Groups H10D8/01 , H10D8/021 , H10D8/022 , H10D8/024 , H10D8/041 , H10D8/045 and H10D8/055 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D8/043 , H10D8/051 and H10D48/021 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 80/20] the at least one device being covered by groups -- H10D 80/20 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to integrated semiconductor devices where at least one device is categorized under specific subgroups, detailing particular types or aspects of these devices. This classification helps in organizing and searching for patents related to complex semiconductor structures with multiple components.
[H10D 8/021] of breakdown diodes -- The H10D 8/021 entry concerns breakdown diodes, devices that exploit controlled reverse-bias breakdown for voltage regulation or protection. It includes Zener diodes, avalanche diodes and similar inorganic semiconductor components designed to maintain a stable voltage or to safely dissipate excess energy.
[H10D 80/211] Resistors, capacitors or inductors covered by  H10D1/00 -- This subclass covers passive components such as resistors, capacitors and inductors that are incorporated into integrated inorganic semiconductor devices. It is classified under CPC H10D 80/211, a child of Electricity → Semiconductor devices → Inorganic electric semiconductor devices → Integrated devices.
[H10D 80/213] Resistors -- H10D 80/213 designates resistors, passive components that limit or regulate electric current by providing a defined resistance value and are used in a wide range of electronic circuits. The class falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices and integrated devices within the broader electricity and semiconductor device categories.
[H10D 80/215] Capacitors -- The H10D 80/215 class covers capacitors that are integrated within inorganic semiconductor devices, such as on-chip capacitors used for energy storage, filtering, or biasing in integrated circuits. It includes a variety of dielectric materials and configurations designed for semiconductor fabrication.
[H10D 8/022] of Zener diodes -- The H10D 8/022 subclass pertains to Zener diodes, a type of semiconductor diode that is designed to operate in its reverse-breakdown region to provide a stable reference voltage for regulation and protection. It includes the structure, material composition, manufacturing methods and applications of these voltage-stabilizing inorganic semiconductor devices.
[H10D 80/231] Diodes covered by  H10D8/00 -- H10D 80/231 classifies integrated inorganic semiconductor diode structures that fall under the broader H10D 8/00 group, covering diodes fabricated as part of integrated devices for electronic circuits.
[H10D 8/024] of Avalanche diodes -- The H10D 8/024 subclass covers avalanche diodes, which are semiconductor diodes designed to operate in the reverse-breakdown region where impact ionisation creates a controlled avalanche of charge carriers. These devices are used for voltage regulation, pulse generation and protective clamping in electronic circuits.
[H10D 80/251] FETs covered by  H10D30/00 , e.g. power FETs -- H10D 80/251 covers field-effect transistors that are classified under H10D30/00, primarily power FETs used in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices for controlling high currents or voltages.
[H10D 80/30] the at least one device being covered by groups -- This CPC subgroup concerns integrated inorganic semiconductor devices in which at least one functional element belongs to a defined group of devices, such as a stack of layers or multiple chips combined. It covers designs where separate semiconductor units are assembled into a single integrated component.
[H10D 8/041] of multilayer diodes -- H10D 8/041 covers multilayer diodes, which are semiconductor devices composed of several stacked p-type and n-type layers to achieve specific voltage-blocking or switching characteristics. The subclass includes the design, manufacturing methods, and material combinations used to form the multiple junctions within the diode.
[H10D 8/043] of planar diodes -- Group H10D8/043 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D8/051 and H10D48/021 . Group H10D8/043 is also impacted by reclassification into groups H10D8/01 , H10D8/021 - H10D8/024 , H10D8/041 , H10D8/045 and H10D8/055 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/045] of PN junction diodes -- The H10D 8/045 subclass concerns PN-junction diodes, covering structures, materials and operating principles of semiconductor diodes formed by joining p-type and n-type regions. It includes features such as barrier height, forward and reverse bias behavior, and variations in dopant profiles that affect switching and rectification performance.
[H10D 8/051] of Schottky diodes -- Group H10D8/051 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D48/021 . Group H10D8/051 is also impacted by reclassification into groups H10D1/01 , H10D1/025 , H10D1/045 - H10D1/048 , H10D8/01 , H10D8/021 - H10D8/024 , H10D8/041 , H10D8/043 , H10D8/045 , H10D8/053 , H10D8/055 and H10D48/021 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/053] of heterojunction diodes or of tunnel diodes -- Group H10D8/053 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D8/051 and H10D48/021 . Groups H10D8/051 , H10D48/021 and H10D8/053 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/055] of transit-time diodes, e.g. IMPATT or TRAPATT diodes -- This subclass covers transit-time diodes, such as IMPATT and TRAPATT devices, which operate by avalanche breakdown and a delayed carrier transit to generate microwave frequencies. These high-power semiconductor diodes are used in oscillators, amplifiers and radar transmitters.
[H10D 8/20] Breakdown diodes, e.g. avalanche diodes -- Group H10D8/20 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D8/00 . Groups H10D8/00 and H10D8/20 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/25] Zener diodes -- H10D 8/25 covers Zener diodes, a type of inorganic semiconductor diode that is intentionally operated in its reverse-breakdown region to provide a stable, reference voltage for regulation or protection circuits. The class includes their structure, fabrication and characteristic-based applications.
[H10D 8/30] Point-contact diodes -- Point-contact diodes are semiconductor devices in which a sharp metal tip makes a microscopic contact with an inorganic crystal, creating a rectifying junction. They belong to the diode subgroup of inorganic electric semiconductor devices and are used mainly for high-frequency detection, mixing and low-noise amplification.
[H10D 8/40] Transit-time diodes, e.g. IMPATT or TRAPATT diodes -- Transit-time diodes such as IMPATT or TRAPATT diodes are high-frequency semiconductor devices that generate microwave power by using the carrier transit time in an avalanche breakdown region. They fall under the inorganic electric semiconductor diode subclass within semiconductor devices and are classified as H10D 8/40 in the CPC system.
[H10D 84/00] Integrated devices formed in or on semiconductor substrates that comprise only semiconducting layers, e.g. on Si wafers or on GaAs-on-Si wafers -- In this group, when the manufacture or treatment of a device is determined to be novel and non-obvious, the device itself is also classified.
[H10D 84/01] Manufacture or treatment -- Group H10D84/01 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/0102 - H10D84/0105 , H10D84/0107 - H10D84/0109 , H10D84/0112 - H10D84/0121 , H10D84/0123 - H10D84/0195 , H10D84/0198 , H10D84/02 - H10D84/08 and H10D89/60 - H10D89/931 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/0102] of thyristors having built-in components, e.g. thyristor having built-in diode -- Groups H10D84/0102 and H10D84/0105 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/01 . Groups H10D84/01 , H10D84/0102 and H10D84/0105 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/0105] the built-in components being field-effect devices -- H10D 84/0105 designates integrated inorganic semiconductor devices in which field-effect components, such as transistors, are built directly into the semiconducting layers on substrates like silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. The classification applies when these field-effect structures are part of the monolithic device rather than added as separate parts.
[H10D 84/0107] Integrating at least one component covered by  H10D12/00  or  H10D30/00  with at least one component covered by  H10D8/00 ,  H10D10/00  or  H10D18/00 , e.g. integrating IGFETs with BJTs -- Groups H10D84/0107 and H10D84/0109 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/01 , H10D84/02 , H10D84/032 , H10D84/035 , H10D84/05 , H10D84/07 and H10D84/08 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/0109] the at least one component covered by  H10D12/00  or  H10D30/00  being a MOS device -- The class covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist only of semiconducting layers formed on a substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon, and it specifically includes any component that falls under H10D12/00 or H10D30/00 when that component is a MOS device.
[H10D 84/0112] Integrating together multiple components covered by  H10D8/00 ,  H10D10/00  or  H10D18/00 , e.g. integrating multiple BJTs -- Groups H10D84/0112 - H10D84/0121 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/01 , H10D84/02 , H10D84/032 , H10D84/035 , H10D84/05 , H10D84/07 and H10D84/08 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/0114] the components including vertical BJTs and lateral BJTs -- H10D 84/0114 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on a substrate that consist solely of semiconducting layers, such as Si or GaAs-on-Si wafers, and includes components that incorporate vertical and lateral bipolar junction transistors.
[H10D 84/0116] the components including integrated injection logic [I2L] -- The H10D 84/0116 classification covers integrated semiconductor devices built on silicon or similar substrates that contain only active semiconducting layers, and it specifically includes components that use integrated injection logic (I2L) circuits. This falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/0119] the components including complementary BJTs -- H10D 84/0119 covers integrated semiconductor devices fabricated on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon that consist only of semiconductor layers and incorporate complementary bipolar junction transistors as circuit components.
[H10D 84/0121] the complementary BJTs being vertical BJTs -- This subclass covers integrated vertical bipolar junction transistors that are complementary, meaning both N-type and P-type vertical BJTs are fabricated together on a single semiconductor substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon, using only semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/0123] Integrating together multiple components covered by  H10D12/00  or  H10D30/00 , e.g. integrating multiple IGBTs -- Groups H10D84/0123 - H10D84/0195 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/01 , H10D84/02 , H10D84/032 , H10D84/035 , H10D84/05 , H10D84/07 and H10D84/08 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/0126] the components including insulated gates, e.g. IGFETs -- The CPC subclass H10D 84/0126 covers components that incorporate insulated-gate structures, notably insulated-gate field-effect transistors (IGFETs). These parts are used in integrated semiconductor devices formed on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon and consist solely of semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/0128] Manufacturing their channels -- The class H10D 84/0128 covers processes for forming the conductive channels in fully semiconductor integrated devices built on Si, GaAs-on-Si or similar substrates. It includes steps such as patterning, doping, epitaxial growth or etching that define the channel geometry in devices where only semiconductor layers are present. The focus is on manufacturing the channel rather than the overall device layout.
[H10D 84/013] Manufacturing their source or drain regions, e.g. silicided source or drain regions -- The CPC subclass H10D 84/013 covers methods for forming the source and drain regions of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices, such as silicided source or drain areas on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It includes processes that create these doped regions to define the transistor terminals within the device.
[H10D 84/0133] Manufacturing common source or drain regions between multiple IGFETs -- This CPC subclass covers processes for forming a single source or drain region that serves two or more insulated-gate field-effect transistors on a semiconductor substrate, such as shared diffusions or implanted zones that connect multiple IGFETs within an integrated device.
[H10D 84/0135] Manufacturing their gate conductors -- The entry H10D 84/0135 covers processes used to form the gate conductors of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices, such as those built on silicon or gallium-arsenide substrates. It includes methods for depositing, patterning, doping or otherwise fabricating the gate electrodes that control the channel in transistors. This classification is applied to manufacturing steps that create the conductive gate structures on the semiconductor wafer.
[H10D 84/0137] the gate conductors being silicided -- This classification deals with integrated semiconductor devices, such as MOSFETs, whose gate electrodes are formed by silicidation, meaning the gate conductor is a metal silicide layer on the semiconductor substrate. It applies to devices built on silicon or silicon-based substrates where only semiconducting layers are present.
[H10D 84/014] the gate conductors having different materials or different implants -- H10D 84/014 covers gate conductors in integrated semiconductor devices that are formed from distinct materials or receive different implant procedures, such as varying dopant species or concentrations. The classification applies to devices built on substrates like silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon where only semiconducting layers are present, focusing on the heterogeneous composition of the gate electrodes.
[H10D 84/0142] the gate conductors having different shapes or dimensions -- This CPC subclass covers integrated semiconductor devices in which the gate electrodes are formed with differing geometries or sizes, such as varied widths, lengths, or cross-sectional profiles, to achieve specific electrical characteristics. It applies to devices built on Si, GaAs-on-Si, or other inorganic semiconductor substrates.
[H10D 84/0144] Manufacturing their gate insulating layers -- H10D 84/0144 is a CPC subclass that concerns the production of gate insulating layers in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices such as those built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It covers processes for forming, depositing, or treating the dielectric material that separates the gate electrode from the channel, including oxidation, chemical vapor deposition and other thin-film techniques.
[H10D 84/0147] Manufacturing their gate sidewall spacers -- This subclass concerns methods for producing the spacer structures that line the sides of a transistor gate in integrated semiconductor devices built on inorganic substrates such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers. It includes steps such as deposition, patterning and etching to create the sidewall spacers that define the gate geometry.
[H10D 84/0149] Manufacturing their interconnections or electrodes, e.g. source or drain electrodes -- The classification covers methods for fabricating the interconnection structures or electrodes, such as source and drain contacts, in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on substrates like silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon. These processes pertain to the formation of conductive paths that link active regions within the device.
[H10D 84/0151] Manufacturing their isolation regions -- Group H10D84/0151 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/02 , H10D84/032 , H10D84/035 , H10D84/05 , H10D84/07 and H10D84/08 . [H10D 84/0153] using gate cut processes -- Group H10D84/0153 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/0151 , H10D84/02 , H10D84/032 , H10D84/035 , H10D84/05 , H10D84/07 and H10D84/08 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/0156] Manufacturing their doped wells -- Group H10D84/0156 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/02 , H10D84/032 , H10D84/035 , H10D84/05 , H10D84/07 and H10D84/08 . Group H10D84/0156 is also impacted by reclassification into group H10D62/299 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/0158] the components including FinFETs -- H10D 84/0158 designates components that incorporate FinFET structures within integrated devices formed wholly from semiconducting layers on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It covers any device where the FinFET architecture is part of the active semiconductor material stack, without additional non-semiconductor layers.
[H10D 84/016] the components including vertical IGFETs -- H10D 84/016 covers integrated semiconductor components that consist solely of semiconducting layers and incorporate vertical insulated-gate field-effect transistors. The classification applies to devices fabricated on substrates such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers, where the IGFETs are oriented vertically within the stack.
[H10D 84/0163] the components including enhancement-mode IGFETs and depletion-mode IGFETs -- H10D 84/0163 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed exclusively on semiconductor substrates, such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, where the structure consists solely of semiconducting layers. The entry specifically includes components that contain both enhancement-mode and depletion-mode insulated-gate field-effect transistors (IGFETs). This classification falls under the electricity → semiconductor devices hierarchy.
[H10D 84/0165] the components including complementary IGFETs, e.g. CMOS devices -- The entry refers to integrated semiconductor components that incorporate complementary insulated-gate field-effect transistors, such as CMOS devices, on a single substrate. These components contain both n-type and p-type IGFETs and are used in mixed-signal and digital integrated circuits.
[H10D 84/0167] Manufacturing their channels -- H10D 84/0167 covers methods for forming the conductive channels of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on semiconductor substrates that contain only semiconducting layers, such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It includes techniques for channel definition, doping, etching or deposition used during device manufacturing.
[H10D 84/017] Manufacturing their source or drain regions, e.g. silicided source or drain regions -- The code H10D 84/017 covers processes for creating the source and drain regions of inorganic semiconductor devices, such as those on silicon or gallium-arsenide substrates. It includes steps that form or modify these regions, for example by depositing or forming silicide layers to improve electrical contact.
[H10D 84/0172] Manufacturing their gate conductors -- H10D 84/0172 covers processes for forming the gate conductors of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices, such as MOSFETs built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It includes methods of depositing, patterning, etching or otherwise creating the metallic or polysilicon gate electrodes that control the channel in these devices. This subclass focuses specifically on the manufacturing steps that produce the gate structures within the semiconductor substrate.
[H10D 84/0174] the gate conductors being silicided -- This CPC subclass covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices where the gate electrode is formed as a silicide, meaning the gate conductor has been silicided. It applies to structures built on semiconductor substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon and comprising only semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/0177] the gate conductors having different materials or different implants -- This subclass covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices in which the gate conductors are made from different materials or receive distinct dopant implants, such as multi-metal gates or dual-doped gate structures on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It resides in the H10D 84 family for inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/0179] the gate conductors having different shapes or dimensions -- H10D 84/0179 covers gate conductors in integrated semiconductor devices where the electrodes are formed with varied shapes or dimensions, such as differing widths, lengths, or profiles, to achieve specific electrical characteristics. The classification applies to inorganic electric semiconductor devices built on substrates like silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon that consist solely of semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/0181] Manufacturing their gate insulating layers -- Manufacturing methods for the gate insulating layers used in inorganic electric semiconductor devices, such as integrated circuits built on silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon substrates, are covered. The entry details processes for forming, depositing, and treating the dielectric material that separates the gate electrode from the underlying channel, ensuring proper isolation and performance.
[H10D 84/0184] Manufacturing their gate sidewall spacers -- In inorganic electric semiconductor devices, the entry covers processes for forming gate sidewall spacers on integrated circuits. It includes methods such as depositing a spacer material on the gate structure, patterning and anisotropic etching to leave a spacer that defines the channel region, and related steps for controlling dimensions and material properties.
[H10D 84/0186] Manufacturing their interconnections or electrodes, e.g. source or drain electrodes -- H10D 84/0186 covers manufacturing the interconnections or electrodes, such as source or drain contacts, in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconductor layers on substrates like silicon or GaAs-on-silicon.
[H10D 84/0188] Manufacturing their isolation regions -- H10D 84/0188 categorises processes for creating isolation regions within integrated semiconductor devices that contain only semiconducting layers, such as those fabricated on silicon wafers or GaAs-on-silicon substrates, enabling electrical separation of circuit elements.
[H10D 84/0191] Manufacturing their doped wells -- The H10D 84/0191 entry covers processes for forming doped wells in inorganic semiconductor integrated devices, such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It describes manufacturing steps that create the required doped regions within the semiconductor substrate to define transistor channels and other circuit elements.
[H10D 84/0193] the components including FinFETs -- H10D 84/0193 covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices formed on substrates such as Si or GaAs-on-Si where only semiconducting layers are present, including FinFET structures and their associated components.
[H10D 84/0195] the components including vertical IGFETs -- This subclass covers components that contain vertical insulated-gate field-effect transistors (vertical IGFETs) integrated on a semiconductor substrate. The devices consist solely of semiconducting layers, such as those built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, and are used where vertical current flow through the channel is required.

All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
