[H10D 84/101] Integrated devices comprising main components and built-in components, e.g. IGBT having built-in freewheel diode -- Group H10D84/101 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D30/64 . Groups H10D30/64 and H10D84/101 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/411] PN diodes having planar bodies -- PN diodes with planar bodies are semiconductor devices in which the p-type and n-type regions are formed in a flat, layer-by-layer structure rather than a junction created by crystal growth. This planar architecture simplifies manufacturing, improves uniformity, and is widely used in integrated circuits and power electronics.
[H10D 84/121] BJTs having built-in components -- Bipolar junction transistors that incorporate additional functional elements-such as resistors, diodes, capacitors or other semiconductor components-directly within the same semiconductor structure are covered by this subclass. The built-in components are formed on the same substrate as the BJT, allowing compact, monolithic devices that combine amplification with ancillary circuitry. This facilitates higher integration and reduced external interconnections in semiconductor assemblies.
[H10D 84/125] the built-in components being resistive elements, e.g. BJT having a built-in ballasting resistor -- The CPC subclass H10D 84/125 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon that incorporate built-in resistive components. Typical examples include bipolar transistors that contain an integral ballasting resistor, providing internal current limiting within the device.
[H10D 84/131] Thyristors having built-in components -- Thyristors that incorporate additional functional elements directly within the same semiconducting layers are covered by H10D 84/131. The class includes integrated devices built on silicon, gallium-arsenide or similar substrates where the thyristor structure contains built-in diodes, resistors, or other components, enabling compact monolithic power circuits.
[H10D 84/133] the built-in components being capacitors or resistors -- This subclass covers inorganic semiconductor integrated devices-such as those on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers-where the circuit includes monolithically formed passive components, specifically built-in capacitors or resistors, alongside the active semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/135] the built-in components being diodes -- The H10D 84/135 entry covers integrated semiconductor devices that incorporate diodes as built-in components within the chip. It applies to inorganic electric semiconductor devices formed on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, where the diode function is realized by a dedicated semiconducting layer integrated into the overall structure.
[H10D 84/136] in anti-parallel configurations, e.g. reverse current thyristor [RCT] -- The H10D 84/136 entry covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices formed on a single-material substrate, such as Si or GaAs-on-Si, that are built only from semiconducting layers and operate in an anti-parallel arrangement, exemplified by reverse-current thyristors. This classification is used for devices where two complementary elements are connected in opposite polarity to enable bidirectional current control.
[H10D 84/138] the built-in components being FETs -- This CPC subclass covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices whose internal active components are field-effect transistors, fabricated on semiconductor substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers and comprising only semiconductor layers. It is applied to inventions where the built-in components are FETs.
[H10D 84/141] VDMOS having built-in components -- The H10D 84/141 entry covers vertical double-diffused MOS (VDMOS) transistors that incorporate built-in components, such as resistors, diodes or capacitors, within the same semiconductor substrate. It addresses monolithically integrated devices formed on inorganic wafers where only semiconducting layers are present.
[H10D 84/143] the built-in components being PN junction diodes -- This subclass concerns integrated semiconductor chips that contain only built-in PN-junction diodes, formed on inorganic substrates such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers. The classification is used for devices where the diode function is integrated directly into the substrate without additional active structures.
[H10D 84/144] in antiparallel diode configurations -- H10D 84/144 pertains to integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on a single semiconductor substrate that consist solely of semiconductor layers and incorporate diodes connected in an antiparallel arrangement. Such configurations enable bidirectional conduction without external components and are used in circuits such as protection or switching on Si or GaAs-on-Si wafers.
[H10D 84/146] the built-in components being Schottky barrier diodes -- This CPC sub-class covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices fabricated on a substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon that incorporate built-in Schottky barrier diodes as functional components. It applies when the Schottky diode is formed as part of the integrated structure rather than as a separate external part.
[H10D 84/148] the built-in components being breakdown diodes, e.g. Zener diodes -- This subclass covers integrated semiconductor devices built on inorganic substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon in which the internal circuitry incorporates breakdown diodes, for example Zener diodes, typically used for voltage regulation or protection.
[H10D 84/151] LDMOS having built-in components -- LDMOS devices that incorporate built-in components such as resistors, capacitors or inductors directly on the semiconductor substrate are classified under H10D 84/151. These laterally diffused MOS transistors are formed on inorganic semiconductor wafers (e.g., silicon or GaAs-on-Si) and consist only of semiconductor layers, integrating auxiliary circuitry within the same chip.
[H10D 84/153] the built-in component being PN junction diodes -- H10D 84/153 classifies integrated inorganic semiconductor devices formed on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon that contain only semiconducting layers, where the built-in active component is a pn-junction diode. This includes monolithic chips whose functional element is the diode junction itself.
[H10D 84/154] in antiparallel diode configurations -- This subclass covers integrated semiconductor devices where diodes are connected in an antiparallel arrangement, meaning two diodes are oriented oppositely to conduct under opposite voltage polarities. It applies to inorganic electric semiconductor devices formed on substrates such as silicon or gallium arsenide.
[H10D 84/156] the built-in components being Schottky barrier diodes -- This CPC subclass covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on a silicon or GaAs-on-silicon substrate that contain built-in components realised as Schottky barrier diodes, with the device consisting only of semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/158] the built-in components being breakdown diodes, e.g. Zener diodes -- Integrated semiconductor devices that incorporate built-in breakdown diodes, such as Zener diodes, into the chip structure for functions like voltage regulation or protection. The classification applies to devices formed on silicon, gallium-arsenide or similar substrates where the breakdown element is part of the semiconductor layers.
[H10D 84/161] IGBT having built-in components -- Group H10D84/161 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D12/411 . Groups H10D12/411 and H10D84/161 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/201] characterised by the integration of only components covered by  H10D1/00  or  H10D8/00 , e.g. RLC circuits -- Group H10D84/201 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D86/85 . Groups H10D86/85 and H10D84/201 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/204] of combinations of diodes or capacitors or resistors -- The class H10D 84/204 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on inorganic substrates that consist only of semiconducting layers and combine two or more discrete elements such as diodes, capacitors or resistors. These monolithic structures are fabricated on wafers like silicon or gallium-arsenide on silicon, and the code is applied to inventions that merge the functions of the individual components into a single device.
[H10D 84/206] of combinations of capacitors and resistors -- Group H10D84/206 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D86/85 . Group H10D84/206 is also impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/209 and H10D84/212 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/209] of only resistors -- Group H10D84/209 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/206 and H10D86/85 . Groups H10D84/206 , H10D86/85 and H10D84/209 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/212] of only capacitors -- Group H10D84/212 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/206 and H10D86/85 . Groups H10D84/206 , H10D86/85 and H10D84/212 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/215] of only varactors -- This subclass covers integrated devices built on inorganic semiconductor substrates that consist only of semiconductor layers and contain exclusively varactor components. Varactors are voltage-controlled capacitors used for frequency tuning or impedance matching. The code applies to structures on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers where no other active devices are present.
[H10D 84/217] of only conductor-insulator-semiconductor capacitors -- Subclass H10D 84/217 covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist solely of conductor-insulator-semiconductor (CIS) capacitors. These structures are fabricated on silicon or similar substrates such as GaAs-on-Si and contain only the three layers-conductive electrode, insulating dielectric, and semiconductor-without any additional active components.
[H10D 8/422] PN diodes having the PN junctions in mesas -- PN diodes with the p-n junction fabricated on mesa-type structures, where the semiconductor material is etched into raised, isolated regions that define the active junction area. The mesa geometry provides better control of electric fields and reduces surface leakage, improving the diode's breakdown voltage and switching speed. This design is common in power and high-frequency semiconductor applications.
[H10D 84/221] of only diodes -- A subclass of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that contain only diode elements, fabricated on semiconductor substrates such as silicon wafers or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers. It excludes other active or passive components, focusing on monolithic diode arrays or circuits formed solely from semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/40] characterised by the integration of at least one component covered by groups -- Group H10D84/40 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/401 . Group H10D84/40 is also impacted by reclassification into group H10D84/80 . Groups H10D84/401 , H10D84/40 and H10D84/80 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/401] Combinations of FETs or IGBTs with BJTs -- Group H10D84/401 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D84/40 . Groups H10D84/401 and H10D84/40 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/403] Combinations of FETs or IGBTs with BJTs and with one or more of diodes, resistors or capacitors -- H10D 84/403 covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices formed on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon substrates that consist solely of semiconductor layers and combine field-effect transistors or insulated-gate bipolar transistors with bipolar junction transistors, optionally including diodes, resistors or capacitors in the same die.
[H10D 84/406] Combinations of FETs or IGBTs with vertical BJTs and with one or more of diodes, resistors or capacitors -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code H10D 84/406 pertains to integrated semiconductor devices that combine field-effect transistors (FETs) or insulated-gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs) with vertical bipolar junction transistors (BJTs), along with additional components such as diodes, resistors, or capacitors.
[H10D 84/409] Combinations of FETs or IGBTs with lateral BJTs and with one or more of diodes, resistors or capacitors -- This CPC subclass (H10D 84/409) covers integrated semiconductor devices that combine field-effect transistors or insulated-gate bipolar transistors with lateral bipolar junction transistors, and may also incorporate one or more diodes, resistors or capacitors, all formed on a single semiconductor substrate.
[H10D 84/60] characterised by the integration of at least one component covered by groups -- H10D 84/60 covers inorganic electric semiconductor devices that are integrated on a common substrate, such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, and that incorporate at least one component belonging to a specified group of semiconductor elements. The classification applies to devices formed solely from semiconducting layers without additional mechanical or optical structures.
[H10D 84/611] Combinations of BJTs and one or more of diodes, resistors or capacitors -- H10D 84/611 classifies integrated semiconductor devices that contain only active semiconducting layers on a wafer, such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si, where a bipolar junction transistor is combined with one or more passive components like diodes, resistors or capacitors. The code is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system for inventions that integrate these elements on a single chip.
[H10D 84/613] Combinations of vertical BJTs and one or more of diodes, resistors or capacitors -- This class covers integrated semiconductor devices built only from semiconductor layers that combine vertical bipolar junction transistors with one or more passive elements such as diodes, resistors or capacitors. The devices are formed on substrates like silicon or GaAs-on-silicon and are intended for use where multiple functions are integrated in a single chip.
[H10D 84/615] Combinations of vertical BJTs and one or more of resistors or capacitors -- Integrated devices on semiconductor substrates that combine vertical bipolar junction transistors with one or more discrete resistors or capacitors, forming compact mixed-function circuits on a single chip. The configuration enables close coupling of active and passive elements for improved performance and reduced interconnect length. This type of integration falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices in the CPC hierarchy.
[H10D 84/617] Combinations of vertical BJTs and only diodes -- The H10D 84/617 entry refers to integrated semiconductor structures that incorporate vertical bipolar junction transistors together with circuits that contain only diodes, all formed on a single inorganic semiconductor substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon. It covers designs where the active layers consist solely of semiconducting material without additional passive layers.
[H10D 84/619] Combinations of lateral BJTs and one or more of diodes, resistors or capacitors -- This CPC subclass covers integrated circuits fabricated on inorganic semiconductor substrates that combine lateral bipolar junction transistors with one or more diodes, resistors or capacitors on the same chip. It applies to devices formed only from semiconducting layers such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers. The code is H10D 84/619.
[H10D 84/63] Combinations of vertical and lateral BJTs -- This CPC subclass covers integrated semiconductor devices that incorporate both vertical and lateral bipolar junction transistors on a single inorganic substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon. The structures share a common semiconductor layer and exploit the differing electrical characteristics of the two transistor orientations within one chip. It is used for compact circuits that need both high current handling and high-frequency performance.
[H10D 84/641] Combinations of only vertical BJTs  vertical complementary BJTs  H10D84/673 -- The CPC subclass H10D 84/641 covers integrated circuits formed on inorganic semiconductor substrates that contain only vertical bipolar junction transistors, including complementary pairs. It applies to devices built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers where the structure consists exclusively of semiconducting layers without additional passive or active components.
[H10D 84/642] Combinations of non-inverted vertical BJTs of the same conductivity type having different characteristics, e.g. Darlington transistors -- The H10D 84/642 entry covers integrated semiconductor devices that combine two or more non-inverted vertical bipolar junction transistors of the same conductivity type into a single structure, such as Darlington configurations, where the transistors are formed on the same substrate and share only semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/643] Combinations of non-inverted vertical BJTs and inverted vertical BJTs -- The entry H10D 84/643 covers integrated semiconductor structures that combine non-inverted vertical bipolar junction transistors with inverted vertical bipolar junction transistors on a common substrate, typically silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It pertains to inorganic electric semiconductor devices in which only semiconducting layers are present, enabling mixed-BJT configurations within a single chip.
[H10D 84/645] Combinations of only lateral BJTs -- Group H10D84/645 is impacted by reclassification into group H10D84/67 . Groups H10D84/645 and H10D84/67 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/65] Integrated injection logic -- Integrated injection logic comprises semiconductor circuits that combine logic gates and current injection within a single inorganic semiconductor substrate, typically silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, enabling compact, high-speed, low-power digital operation.
[H10D 84/652] using vertical injector structures -- H10D 84/652 covers the use of vertical injector structures in inorganic electric semiconductor devices such as integrated circuits built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, where the device consists solely of semiconductor layers. The classification applies to designs that employ vertically oriented carrier injection to improve performance or enable specific functions within the integrated device.
[H10D 84/655] using field effect injector structures -- The subclass covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers that employ field-effect injector structures to introduce charge carriers. It includes designs where a field-effect transistor-like region is used to inject electrons or holes into active layers for amplification or light emission. The focus is on the device architecture rather than the specific material composition.
[H10D 84/658] integrated in combination with analog structures -- A mixed-signal integrated circuit in which purely semiconducting layers on a substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si are combined with analog components. The device integrates digital and analog structures on the same chip, enabling on-chip signal conditioning, filtering or amplification alongside purely semiconductor functions.
[H10D 84/67] Complementary BJTs -- Group H10D84/67 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/645 . Groups H10D84/645 and H10D84/67 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/673] Vertical complementary BJTs -- The H10D 84/673 class covers vertical complementary bipolar junction transistors formed as integrated devices on semiconductor substrates that consist solely of semiconducting layers, such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si wafers. These devices employ a vertical geometry where the collector and emitter are stacked, enabling complementary N- and P-type operation in a single chip.
[H10D 84/676] Combinations of only thyristors -- This subgroup covers integrated semiconductor devices in which only thyristor elements are combined on a single inorganic substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon, without inclusion of other active or passive components. The devices are built from semiconducting layers only and are used for power-switching or control functions.
[H10D 84/80] characterised by the integration of at least one component covered by groups -- Group H10D84/80 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/40 . Groups H10D84/40 and H10D84/80 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/811] Combinations of field-effect devices and one or more diodes, capacitors or resistors -- Group H10D84/811 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/813 and H10D84/817 . Groups H10D84/811 , H10D84/813 and H10D84/817 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/813] Combinations of field-effect devices and capacitor only -- Group H10D84/813 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/811 . Groups H10D84/811 and H10D84/813 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/817] Combinations of field-effect devices and resistors only -- Group H10D84/817 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/811 . Groups H10D84/811 and H10D84/817 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/82] of only field-effect components -- H10D 84/82 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on a single semiconducting layer where the only active elements are field-effect components such as MOSFETs, JFETs or MESFETs. It applies to devices built on silicon, GaAs-on-Si or similar substrates that contain no other active structures.
[H10D 84/83] of only insulated-gate FETs [IGFET] -- Group H10D84/83 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/8311 , H10D84/8312 , H10D84/83125 , H10D84/83135 , H10D84/83138 , H10D84/8314 , H10D84/8316 , H10D84/832 - H10D84/833 , H10D84/835 , H10D84/836 and H10D84/837 - H10D84/839 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

[H10D 84/833] comprising forksheet IGFETs -- H10D 84/833 covers integrated devices built on semiconductor substrates that consist solely of semiconducting layers and incorporate fork-sheet insulated-gate field-effect transistors. These structures are typically realized on silicon wafers or GaAs-on-silicon wafers and are used for high-performance electronic circuits.

[H10D 84/839] comprising VDMOS -- The H10D 84/839 subclass covers integrated devices built on semiconductor substrates that contain exclusively semiconductor layers, such as those formed on silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers, where the devices incorporate vertical double-diffused MOS (VDMOS) structures. It falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices within the broader electricity and semiconductor device categories.
[H10D 84/84] Combinations of enhancement-mode IGFETs and depletion-mode IGFETs -- Group H10D84/84 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/8311 , H10D84/8312 , H10D84/83125 , H10D84/83135 , H10D84/83138 , H10D84/8314 , H10D84/8316 , H10D84/835 , H10D84/836 and H10D84/837 - H10D84/839 .
