[H10D 84/852] comprising forksheet IGFETs -- The H10D 84/852 subclass refers to integrated devices formed on inorganic semiconductor substrates that contain only semiconductor layers and incorporate forksheet insulated-gate field-effect transistors (IGFETs) within the circuit.
[H10D 84/853] comprising FinFETs -- Group H10D84/853 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/8311 , H10D84/8312 , H10D84/83135 , H10D84/83138 , H10D84/8314 , H10D84/8316 , H10D84/835 , H10D84/836 , H10D84/837 - H10D84/839 and H10D84/851 - H10D84/852 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/854] comprising arrangements for preventing bipolar actions between the different IGFET regions, e.g. arrangements for latchup prevention -- H10D 84/854 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on substrates that contain only semiconducting layers, such as Si or GaAs-on-Si wafers, and includes circuit designs that prevent bipolar actions between different IGFET regions, for example latch-up suppression techniques.
[H10D 84/856] the complementary IGFETs having different architectures than each other, e.g. high-voltage and low-voltage CMOS -- Group H10D84/856 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/8311 , H10D84/8312 , H10D84/83135 , H10D84/83138 , H10D84/8314 , H10D84/8316 , H10D84/835 , H10D84/836 and H10D84/837 - H10D84/839 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 84/857] comprising an N-type well but not a P-type well -- This subclass covers integrated semiconductor structures built on a single semiconductor substrate that contain an N-type well but omit any P-type well. The devices are formed entirely from semiconducting layers, such as those on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, and are used for electronic functions.
[H10D 84/858] comprising a P-type well but not an N-type well -- This subclass covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon that consist solely of semiconducting layers and include a P-type well while omitting any N-type well. It is used to classify inventions where the device structure relies exclusively on a P-type region for functionality.
[H10D 84/859] comprising both N-type and P-type wells, e.g. twin-tub -- Integrated semiconductor devices formed on a substrate that contain only semiconducting layers and include both N-type and P-type wells, such as twin-tub structures, are classified under H10D 84/859. This falls within the broader groups for inorganic electric semiconductor devices and semiconductor devices in general.
[H10D 84/86] of Schottky-barrier gate FETs -- H10D 84/86 covers integrated semiconductor devices on inorganic substrates where the gate of a field-effect transistor is formed by a Schottky barrier, i.e., a metal-semiconductor junction that controls channel conduction. These Schottky-gate FETs are used for high-speed or high-frequency applications and are fabricated on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers.
[H10D 84/87] of PN-junction gate FETs -- H10D 84/87 covers field-effect transistors whose gate is formed by a pn-junction, known as PN-junction gate FETs, fabricated as integrated devices on inorganic semiconductor substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si wafers. These devices belong to the class of inorganic electric semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/891] characterised by the integration of only components covered by  H10D44/00 , e.g. integration of charge-coupled devices [CCD] or charge injection devices [CID -- H10D 84/891 covers inorganic semiconductor devices in which the integrated circuit contains only components defined in H10D 44/00, such as charge-coupled devices or charge-injection devices, fabricated on a single semiconducting substrate like silicon or GaAs-on-silicon. It applies to integrated structures formed solely from semiconducting layers without additional passive or active elements.
[H10D 84/895] comprising bucket-brigade charge-coupled devices -- H10D 84/895 covers integrated devices formed on semiconductor substrates that consist only of semiconducting layers, such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, and incorporate bucket-brigade charge-coupled devices. It falls within the inorganic electric semiconductor devices subclass of the electricity section.
[H10D 84/90] Masterslice integrated circuits -- The H10D 84/90 class defines masterslice integrated circuits, which are integrated semiconductor devices built on a single substrate that contains only active semiconductor layers such as silicon or gallium-arsenide on silicon, without additional passive or interconnect layers. These devices are fabricated directly on the wafer and function as a complete circuit in one piece.
[H10D 84/901] comprising bipolar technology -- H10D 84/901 covers integrated semiconductor devices fabricated on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon substrates that are made only of semiconducting layers and employ bipolar technology, such as bipolar junction transistors or bipolar integrated circuits, rather than field-effect or other device types.
[H10D 84/903] comprising field effect technology -- Integrated semiconductor devices formed on a single type of substrate, such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon, in which the circuitry includes field-effect components that modulate current flow by an applied electric field.
[H10D 84/905] A3B5 or A3B6 gate arrays -- H10D 84/905 covers integrated gate-array devices that employ group-III-V compounds of the A3B5 or A3B6 type as the active semiconductor layers, usually built on silicon or silicon-based substrates such as GaAs-on-Si. The structures consist solely of semiconductor layers and belong to the class of inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/907] CMOS gate arrays -- H10D 84/907 refers to CMOS gate arrays, which are integrated circuit structures built on a semiconductor substrate containing only semiconductor layers such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon. The arrays consist of pre-fabricated CMOS transistors arranged in a configurable pattern that can be customized during final metalization to implement specific logic functions. They are used for producing application-specific integrated circuits with moderate volume and rapid time-to-market.
[H10D 84/909] Microarchitecture -- The CPC subclass H10D 84/909, titled Microarchitecture, pertains to integrated semiconductor devices that consist only of semiconductor layers formed on a substrate such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers, describing the internal arrangement and structural layout of the circuitry. It is nested under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Inorganic Electric Semiconductor Devices.
[H10D 84/911] Basic cell P to N transistor counts -- The H10D 84/911 entry covers methods and data for counting or specifying the basic P-to-N transistor cells used in integrated semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconducting layers on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It relates to the design and layout of these P-N transistor structures within the overall circuit.
[H10D 84/912] 4-T CMOS basic cells -- The H10D 84/912 entry covers basic four-transistor (4-T) CMOS cells used as elementary building blocks in integrated circuits. These devices are formed on semiconductor substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si wafers and consist solely of semiconducting layers, without additional conductive or insulating films.
[H10D 84/914] 5-T CMOS basic cells -- The H10D 84/914 class covers five-transistor (5-T) CMOS basic cells, which are fundamental logic elements built from a network of five MOS transistors on a single semiconductor substrate. These cells serve as building blocks for integrated circuits and are used in designs where a compact, low-power implementation of standard logic functions is required.
[H10D 84/916] 6-T CMOS basic cells -- The H10D 84/916 entry covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on substrates that contain only semiconducting layers, such as silicon wafers or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers, and that implement basic six-transistor CMOS cells. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under electricity, semiconductor devices and inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/918] 7-T CMOS basic cells -- H10D 84/918 designates CMOS basic cells that contain seven transistors, intended for integration on purely semiconducting layers such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. The class covers the structure, fabrication and electrical behavior of these 7-T cells used in digital logic or memory circuits.
[H10D 84/921] 8-T CMOS basic cells -- The H10D 84/921 classifies eight-transistor CMOS basic cells, which are integrated circuit building blocks built only from semiconducting layers on silicon or comparable substrates. It pertains to inorganic electric semiconductor devices where the entire structure is formed on or in the semiconductor wafer.
[H10D 84/922] relative P to N transistor sizes -- This CPC subclass H10D 84/922 concerns the relative sizing of p-type and n-type transistors within integrated semiconductor devices that consist only of semiconductor layers, such as those fabricated on silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers. It addresses design considerations for matching or scaling the dimensions of complementary transistors in monolithic circuits.
[H10D 84/924] for current drive capability -- The subclass H10D 84/924 addresses the current-drive capability of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist only of semiconducting layers on a substrate such as Si or GaAs-on-Si. It covers design, analysis, or measurement methods concerning how much current the device can source or sink and related performance-enhancing features.
[H10D 84/925] for delay time adaptation -- H10D 84/925 covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that incorporate circuitry for adjusting signal delay times, such as on-chip delay lines, buffers or timing-control circuits used to synchronise or fine-tune the propagation speed of electrical signals within a monolithic device. This subclass pertains to the design of delay-time adaptation features built directly onto silicon, GaAs-on-Si or similar semiconductor substrates.
[H10D 84/927] for capacitive loading -- H10D 84/927 covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on semiconductor substrates, such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers, that consist solely of semiconducting layers and incorporate capacitive loading to modify their electrical characteristics. This subclass is used for devices where the capacitance is an intentional part of the circuit architecture.
[H10D 84/929] Isolations -- The H10D 84/929 class deals with isolation structures and methods used in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconductor layers, such as those built on silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers. It covers techniques for electrically separating components within the same substrate, including isolation trenches, guard rings, and dielectric isolation.
[H10D 84/931] FET isolation -- The CPC subclass H10D 84/931, titled "FET isolation", covers methods and structures for electrically isolating field-effect transistors in integrated devices that consist solely of semiconducting layers on a wafer, such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si substrates. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/933] LOCOS -- H10D 84/933 (LOCOS) refers to the local oxidation of silicon technique used to create insulating regions in integrated circuits formed on semiconductor substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon, where the device consists solely of semiconducting layers. It is classified under inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/935] Degree of specialisation for implementing specific functions -- H10D 84/935 covers integrated semiconductor devices built solely from semiconducting layers on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon, focusing on how specialized the device is for a particular function. It classifies the degree to which the structure, doping or layout is optimized for a dedicated task rather than generic operation.
[H10D 84/937] Implementation of digital circuits -- H10D 84/937 covers methods and structures for realizing digital logic functions within integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon substrates. The classification includes design, layout, and fabrication techniques that enable digital circuitry to operate directly on these semiconductor layers.
[H10D 84/938] Implementation of memory functions -- Implements memory functions within integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconducting layers on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, covering the design, architecture and fabrication of memory cells and related circuitry embedded in these monolithic structures.
[H10D 84/941] Implementation of analog circuits -- H10D 84/941 covers implementation of analog circuits in integrated devices formed on semiconductor substrates composed solely of semiconducting layers, such as silicon or gallium-arsenide on silicon wafers. It falls under the CPC hierarchy ELECTRICITY → SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES → INORGANIC ELECTRIC SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES.
[H10D 84/942] Resistors and capacitors -- H10D 84/942 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon substrates that consist only of semiconducting layers and incorporate on-chip resistors and capacitors.
[H10D 84/944] Hybrid analog or digital -- Covers integrated semiconductor devices fabricated on silicon or gallium arsenide-on-silicon substrates that consist solely of semiconducting layers and implement hybrid analog or digital functionality within a single chip.
[H10D 84/946] Embedded IO cells -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code H10D 84/946 pertains to embedded input-output cells in integrated semiconductor devices, specifically those formed on or within semiconductor substrates like silicon wafers. These cells are crucial for the communication between the device and external systems.
[H10D 84/948] Transmission gates -- Transmission gates are semiconductor switches formed on integrated circuit substrates that combine n-type and p-type MOSFETs to pass or block signals bidirectionally without applying a voltage offset. They are used in digital logic and analog multiplexing circuits to provide low-on-resistance and high isolation.
[H10D 84/949] Porous cells, i.e. pass-through elements -- The code H10D 84/949 covers porous cells, also called pass-through elements, which are integrated semiconductor structures that incorporate a porous layer enabling fluids, gases or light to traverse the device while preserving its electrical function. These elements are built on inorganic substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers and consist solely of semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/951] Technology used, i.e. design rules -- The CPC subclass H10D 84/951 pertains to inorganic integrated semiconductor devices built on Si or GaAs-on-Si substrates where the classification focuses on the technology employed, specifically the design rules governing layout and processing of the purely semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/953] Sub-micron technology -- H10D 84/953 covers integrated semiconductor devices built on substrates such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon where the structure consists solely of semiconducting layers and the feature sizes are in the sub-micron range. The classification is used for technologies that implement very small dimensions in inorganic semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/955] Twin-tub technology -- Twin-tub technology refers to an integrated semiconductor structure in which two adjacent, isolated regions (tubs) are formed on a single inorganic substrate such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si. Each tub contains its own active layers, allowing distinct devices or circuit functions to be realized on the same wafer while maintaining electrical isolation. The approach is used for compact, high-density integration of purely semiconducting layers.
[H10D 84/957] SOS or SOI technology -- The H10D 84/957 entry covers integrated semiconductor devices built on substrates that incorporate an insulating layer, such as silicon-on-insulator or silicon-on-sapphire, so that only semiconducting layers form the active circuitry. It applies to circuits fabricated on silicon wafers or on gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers where the insulating layer isolates the device from the bulk substrate.
[H10D 84/959] Connectability characteristics, i.e. diffusion and polysilicon geometries -- The H10D 84/959 subclass deals with the connectability characteristics of integrated semiconductor devices, specifically the geometry of diffusion regions and polysilicon patterns that form electrical connections. It applies to devices built on purely semiconducting layers such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers.
[H10D 84/961] Substrate and well contacts -- H10D 84/961 pertains to the design of electrical contacts that connect the substrate and the individual wells of monolithic semiconductor devices built on a single crystalline layer, such as Si or GaAs-on-Si wafers. The classification includes structures, materials and methods for forming these contacts to provide reliable electrical access to the active regions within the integrated device.
[H10D 84/962] Horizontal or vertical grid line density -- The H10D 84/962 subclass covers specifications of the density of horizontal or vertical grid lines in inorganic electric semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconductor layers on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It addresses how closely spaced interconnect lines are arranged, influencing device layout, electrical performance and manufacturing processes.
[H10D 84/964] Yield or reliability -- This CPC subclass H10D 84/964 deals with methods, measurements or improvements related to the manufacturing yield and reliability of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconductor layers on substrates such as silicon or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers.
[H10D 84/966] Gate electrode terminals or contacts -- The classification covers structures that provide the electrical connection to the gate of a field-effect transistor, including terminals, pads or contact patterns formed on the semiconductor substrate. It applies to integrated devices where the gate electrode is linked to external circuitry by such gate electrode terminals or contacts.
[H10D 84/968] Macro-architecture -- The H10D 84/968 macro-architecture class covers the overall structural design of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that are built on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon and consist solely of semiconducting layers. It addresses the layout of layers, interconnections and the three-dimensional arrangement that defines the device's functional architecture.
[H10D 84/971] Number of core or basic cells in the macro (RAM or ROM) -- The H10D 84/971 classification covers patents that specify the number of elementary or basic memory cells constituting a macro-scale RAM or ROM block within integrated inorganic semiconductor devices, such as those built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon substrates.
[H10D 84/972] Distribution functions, e.g. sea of gates -- H10D 84/972 covers the use of distribution functions such as a sea of gates in integrated semiconductor devices built only from semiconducting layers on substrates like silicon or GaAs-on-silicon. The classification applies to designs where many gate structures are arranged in a regular or statistical pattern to control carrier flow across the device.
[H10D 84/974] Layout specifications, i.e. inner core regions -- H10D 84/974 covers the layout specifications for the inner core regions of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on semiconductor substrates, such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It defines how the active semiconductor layers and core structures are arranged within the chip to achieve the desired electrical performance. This subclass pertains to the design of the central functional areas of monolithic semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/975] Wiring regions or routing -- H10D 84/975 concerns the layout of interconnects in fully inorganic integrated semiconductor devices. It covers the definition, design and implementation of wiring regions or routing that connect circuit elements on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers, including metal lines, vias and routing patterns within the chip.
[H10D 84/977] Avoiding clock-skew or clock-delays -- H10D 84/977 classifies integrated inorganic semiconductor devices-such as those built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers-where only semiconducting layers are present and the design includes measures to avoid or minimise clock-skew and clock-delays, ensuring more accurate timing in the circuit.
[H10D 84/979] Data lines, e.g. buses -- H10D 84/979 covers data lines such as buses that are integrated on inorganic semiconductor substrates containing only semiconducting layers, for example silicon wafers or gallium-arsenide-on-silicon wafers. It is classified under electricity, semiconductor devices, inorganic electric semiconductor devices, and pertains to integrated devices formed in or on these semiconductor substrates.
[H10D 84/981] Power supply lines -- H10D 84/981 covers power supply lines integrated on semiconductor substrates that consist solely of semiconducting layers, such as internal voltage rails or distribution networks formed on Si or GaAs-on-Si wafers. The subclass pertains to inorganic electric semiconductor devices where the supply interconnects are built into the chip rather than added as external conductors.
[H10D 84/983] Levels of metallisation -- H10D 84/983 classifies the arrangement and stacking of metal layers that create the interconnect structure in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices, such as those built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It addresses how multiple metallisation levels are formed, patterned and connected to achieve circuit functionality.
[H10D 84/985] Two levels of metal -- The entry covers integrated semiconductor devices built on Si or GaAs-on-Si substrates that contain only semiconducting layers and employ two separate metal levels for interconnections, such as contact and routing layers, within the device structure.
[H10D 84/987] Three levels of metal -- H10D 84/987 covers semiconductor integrated devices built on silicon or similar substrates that incorporate three distinct metal layers, typically used for contacts, interconnections and barrier functions within the device structure.
[H10D 84/988] Four or more levels of metal -- This subclass pertains to integrated semiconductor devices built on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon substrates that contain only semiconductor layers and employ four or more separate metal interconnect levels for routing. It is nested under electricity, semiconductor devices, and inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 84/991] Latch-up prevention -- H10D 84/991 covers techniques for preventing latch-up in integrated semiconductor devices that consist only of semiconducting layers on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-silicon. It includes design measures-guard rings, isolation structures, doping profiles and layout strategies-that suppress the parasitic thyristor effect which can cause uncontrolled high currents. The entry applies to any inorganic electric semiconductor device formed on a semiconductor wafer.
[H10D 84/992] Noise prevention, e.g. preventing crosstalk -- The subclass H10D 84/992 concerns methods for reducing electromagnetic interference in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that consist solely of semiconductor layers, such as those fabricated on silicon or GaAs-on-silicon wafers. It includes techniques for preventing crosstalk and other noise between circuit elements on the same chip.
[H10D 84/994] Radiation hardened circuits -- H10D 84/994 covers integrated circuits fabricated on inorganic semiconductor substrates that are designed to remain functional after exposure to ionising radiation, such as in space or nuclear environments. The circuits are formed solely from semiconducting layers on Si or GaAs-on-Si wafers and incorporate radiation-hardening techniques and designs.
[H10D 84/996] using combined field effect technology and bipolar technology -- H10D 84/996 covers integrated semiconductor devices that combine field-effect and bipolar functions on a single substrate, allowing both high-speed switching and amplification in one chip. The classification applies to inorganic semiconductor structures such as Si or GaAs-on-Si where only semiconducting layers are present.
[H10D 84/998] Input and output buffer/driver structures -- H10D 84/998 classifies integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built only from semiconducting layers on substrates such as silicon or GaAs-on-Si, and it specifically covers circuit structures that function as input and output buffers or drivers, managing signal levels and drive capability within the chip.
[H10D 8/50] PIN diodes -- H10D 8/50 classifies PIN diodes, a type of inorganic semiconductor diode consisting of a p-type layer, an intrinsic region, and an n-type layer. The intrinsic layer provides high resistance at low bias and low resistance when forward-biased, making PIN diodes useful for high-frequency switching, attenuation, and photodetection.
[H10D 8/60] Schottky-barrier diodes -- Schottky-barrier diodes are semiconductor devices that use a metal-semiconductor junction instead of a p-n junction, giving a low forward voltage drop and fast switching. They are employed in power rectification, RF detection, and voltage clamping. The classification H10D 8/60 covers these inorganic electric semiconductor diode structures.
[H10D 86/00] Integrated devices formed in or on insulating or conducting substrates, e.g. formed in silicon-on-insulator [SOI] substrates or on stainless steel or glass substrates -- In this group, when the manufacture or treatment of a device is determined to be novel and non-obvious, the device itself is also classified.
[H10D 86/01] Manufacture or treatment -- Groups H10D86/01 - H10D86/03 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/01 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 86/011] comprising FinFETs -- The code H10D 86/011 refers to inorganic semiconductor devices that are integrated on insulating or conductive substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass and that incorporate FinFET structures. It falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, inorganic electric semiconductor devices, and integrated devices formed on such substrates.
[H10D 86/021] of multiple TFTs -- This subclass covers integrated semiconductor structures that incorporate several thin-film transistors on a single insulating or conducting substrate, such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel or glass plates, where the TFTs are fabricated together and share interconnections.
[H10D 86/0212] comprising manufacture, treatment or coating of substrates -- H10D 86/0212 relates to the manufacture, treatment or coating of substrates used for inorganic electric semiconductor devices that are integrated on insulating or conductive bases such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass. The code covers methods for preparing the substrate surface, applying protective or functional layers, and other processes that affect device performance.
[H10D 86/0214] using temporary substrates -- This subclass covers processes that employ a sacrificial or temporary substrate to fabricate integrated inorganic semiconductor devices on insulating or conductive supports such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass. The temporary substrate supports the device during formation and is later removed or replaced.
[H10D 86/0221] comprising manufacture, treatment or patterning of TFT semiconductor bodies -- The H10D 86/0221 class covers processes for making thin-film-transistor (TFT) semiconductor bodies that are built on insulating or conductive substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel or glass. It includes manufacturing steps, post-fabrication treatments and patterning techniques specific to these TFT structures. This falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices within the broader electricity and semiconductor device hierarchy.
[H10D 86/0223] comprising crystallisation of amorphous, microcrystalline or polycrystalline semiconductor materials -- The CPC subclass H10D 86/0223 relates to semiconductor technology in which an amorphous, micro-crystalline or poly-crystalline semiconductor layer is deliberately crystallised within integrated devices that are built on insulating or conductive substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel or glass. It falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 86/0225] using crystallisation-promoting species, e.g. using a Ni catalyst -- This CPC subclass H10D 86/0225 covers inorganic electric semiconductor devices formed on insulating or conducting substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, steel or glass, where a crystallisation-promoting species, commonly a nickel catalyst, is used to induce crystal growth during fabrication.
[H10D 86/0227] using structural arrangements to control crystal growth, e.g. placement of grain filters -- This CPC subclass pertains to inorganic electric semiconductor devices in which crystal growth is regulated by structural features such as grain filters, patterned barriers or other arrangements that affect grain size and orientation. It applies to integrated devices formed on insulating or conducting substrates like silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass.
[H10D 86/0229] characterised by control of the annealing or irradiation parameters -- This subclass covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices fabricated on insulating or conducting substrates, such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel, or glass, where the defining feature is the control of annealing or irradiation parameters-temperature, time, atmosphere, dose or energy-to obtain specific material or device characteristics. It relates to processes that deliberately adjust heat-treatment or radiation steps during device formation.
[H10D 86/0231] using masks, e.g. half-tone masks -- This CPC subclass belongs to electricity → semiconductor devices → inorganic electric semiconductor devices and concerns integrated devices produced on insulating or conducting substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass. H10D 86/0231 specifically covers the use of masks, for example halftone masks, in the fabrication of these devices.
[H10D 86/0241] using liquid deposition, e.g. printing -- A subclass of inorganic electric semiconductor devices formed on insulating or conducting substrates, such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel or glass, where the device structures are created by liquid-deposition techniques including printing. It covers integrated devices produced by applying conductive or semiconductive inks, pastes or other liquid materials onto the substrate.
[H10D 86/0251] characterised by increasing the uniformity of device parameters -- H10D 86/0251 concerns integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on insulating or conducting substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass, where the focus is on methods that improve the uniformity of device parameters across the wafer or circuit. It covers techniques for controlling process variations to achieve consistent electrical performance in the final device.
[H10D 86/03] wherein the substrate comprises sapphire, e.g. silicon-on-sapphire [SOS] -- The CPC subclass H10D 86/03 covers integrated semiconductor devices fabricated on insulating substrates where the substrate is sapphire, such as silicon-on-sapphire structures. It is a narrow division of inorganic electric semiconductor devices dealing with devices formed on insulating or conducting bases.
[H10D 8/605] of the trench conductor-insulator-semiconductor barrier type, e.g. trench MOS barrier Schottky rectifiers [TMBS] -- H10D 8/605 covers semiconductor diodes that use a trench conductor-insulator-semiconductor barrier configuration, such as trench MOS-barrier Schottky rectifiers. The classification applies to devices where a trench etched into the semiconductor forms a barrier layer that controls carrier flow, combining MOS and Schottky principles for low-forward voltage drop and fast switching. It is a subcategory of inorganic electric semiconductor diodes.
[H10D 86/201] the substrates comprising an insulating layer on a semiconductor body, e.g. SOI  H10D86/40  take precedence -- H10D 86/201 covers semiconductor devices built on substrates that include an insulating layer over a semiconductor body, such as silicon-on-insulator (SOI) wafers, stainless-steel or glass platforms. The subclass addresses the material composition and structure of these layered substrates.
[H10D 86/215] comprising FinFETs -- H10D 86/215 covers integrated semiconductor devices fabricated on insulating or conducting substrates-such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel, or glass-that incorporate FinFET (fin-shaped field-effect transistor) structures, a geometry used to improve control and reduce leakage in modern micro-electronics.
[H10D 86/40] characterised by multiple TFTs -- H10D 86/40 classifies integrated semiconductor devices built on insulating or conducting substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass and containing multiple thin-film transistors. The code is used for inventions where several TFTs are incorporated on a single substrate to achieve circuit functions.
[H10D 86/411] characterised by materials, geometry or structure of the substrates -- H10D 86/411 classifies integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that are defined by the material, geometry or structure of the substrate on which they are built, such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel or glass substrates. It pertains to devices formed in or on insulating or conducting substrates within the broader category of electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 86/421] having a particular composition, shape or crystalline structure of the active layer -- This subclass covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on insulating or conductive substrates, such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel, or glass, where the active layer is defined by a specific chemical composition, physical shape, or crystalline structure, affecting the device's electrical properties.
[H10D 86/423] comprising semiconductor materials not belonging to the Group IV, e.g. InGaZnO -- An integrated semiconductor device built on an insulating or conductive substrate such as SOI, stainless steel or glass, in which the active material is a non-Group-IV compound. The entry covers devices that employ semiconductor alloys like indium-gallium-zinc-oxide (InGaZnO) rather than silicon, germanium or tin. It falls under the CPC class H10D 86/423, a subcategory of inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 86/425] having different crystal properties in different TFTs or within an individual TFT -- H10D 86/425 covers integrated semiconductor devices formed on insulating or conducting substrates-such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass-where the thin-film transistors display differing crystal properties either between separate TFTs or within a single TFT, encompassing variations in crystal orientation, grain size or phase used to tailor electrical performance.
[H10D 86/427] having different thicknesses of the semiconductor bodies in different TFTs -- H10D 86/427 covers integrated semiconductor devices built on insulating or conductive substrates where the thin-film transistors have semiconductor layers of varying thickness across the circuit. This variation is employed to tailor electrical characteristics such as drive current or threshold voltage within the same chip, commonly seen in SOI, glass or stainless-steel platforms.
[H10D 86/431] having different compositions, shapes, layouts or thicknesses of gate insulators in different TFTs -- This subclass covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on insulating or conducting substrates, such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass, where the gate insulator of individual thin-film transistors is not uniform, employing different compositions, shapes, layouts or thicknesses to tailor performance.
[H10D 86/441] Interconnections, e.g. scanning lines -- The code H10D 86/441 covers interconnections such as scanning lines in integrated inorganic semiconductor devices built on insulating or conducting substrates like SOI, steel, or glass. It relates to the layout and electrical connections within these devices.
[H10D 86/443] adapted for preventing breakage, peeling or short circuiting -- H10D 86/443 covers integrated semiconductor devices built on insulating or conductive substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass, which incorporate features to avoid mechanical breakage, substrate peeling or electrical short-circuiting. It lies within the CPC hierarchy of Electricity → Semiconductor devices → Inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 86/451] characterised by the compositions or shapes of the interlayer dielectrics -- H10D 86/451 is a CPC subclass for integrated semiconductor devices formed on insulating or conducting substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass, where the invention is defined by the material composition or physical shape of the interlayer dielectric layers used between circuit elements.
[H10D 86/471] having different architectures, e.g. having both top-gate and bottom-gate TFTs -- This CPC subgroup pertains to inorganic electric semiconductor devices, especially integrated devices built on insulating or conductive substrates, where the thin-film transistors are implemented with mixed architectures such as a combination of top-gate and bottom-gate configurations within the same circuit or module.
[H10D 86/481] integrated with passive devices, e.g. auxiliary capacitors -- The H10D 86/481 subclass covers integrated semiconductor devices that incorporate passive elements such as auxiliary capacitors, built directly on insulating or conducting substrates like silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass, where the passive components are formed as part of the same chip structure.
[H10D 86/60] wherein the TFTs are in active matrices -- The subclass H10D 86/60 covers inorganic electric semiconductor devices in which thin-film transistors are organized as active-matrix arrays, typically on insulating or conductive substrates such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless-steel or glass. It specifically addresses configurations where each pixel is driven by its own TFT in an integrated matrix.
[H10D 86/80] characterised by multiple passive components, e.g. resistors, capacitors or inductors -- This class covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices formed on insulating or conducting substrates-such as silicon-on-insulator, stainless steel or glass-that incorporate two or more passive components like resistors, capacitors or inductors. It sits within the hierarchy of electricity, semiconductor devices, and inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 86/85] characterised by only passive components -- Group H10D86/85 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10D84/201 , H10D84/206 , H10D84/209 and H10D84/212 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/70] Tunnel-effect diodes -- Tunnel-effect diodes are semiconductor devices that exploit quantum tunneling to allow charge carriers to traverse a potential barrier, enabling very fast switching and operation at low voltages. They fall under inorganic electric semiconductor devices within the broader categories of semiconductors and electricity, and are classified in the CPC as H10D 8/70.
[H10D 87/00] Integrated devices comprising both bulk components and either SOI or SOS components on the same substrate -- Group H10D87/00 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/01 . Groups H10D84/01 and H10D87/00 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/75] Tunnel-effect PN diodes, e.g. Esaki diodes -- H10D 8/75 classifies tunnel-effect PN diodes, such as Esaki diodes, which are inorganic semiconductor diodes that exploit quantum-mechanical tunneling across a narrow p-n junction to achieve negative resistance and fast switching. The code falls under the hierarchy Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Inorganic Electric Semiconductor Devices → Diodes.
[H10D 8/755] Resonant tunneling diodes [RTD] -- Resonant tunneling diodes are quantum-mechanical semiconductor devices that exploit tunneling through a double-barrier structure to produce negative differential resistance, enabling high-frequency oscillators and fast switching. They fall under CPC H10D 8/755 within the inorganic electric semiconductor diode subclass.
[H10D 8/80] PNPN diodes, e.g. Shockley diodes or break-over diodes -- PNPN diodes, often called Shockley or break-over diodes, are four-layer semiconductor devices with a p-n-p-n structure that display a distinct break-over voltage and negative resistance region. When the applied voltage exceeds this break-over point, the device switches abruptly from a high-impedance to a low-impedance state, making it useful for triggering, pulse generation and switching applications.
[H10D 88/00] Three-dimensional [3D] integrated devices -- H10D 88/00 covers three-dimensional integrated semiconductor devices that stack multiple active layers vertically to achieve higher functionality and density. These 3D structures combine inorganic semiconductor materials and are used in advanced electronics, memory and photonic applications.
[H10D 88/01] Manufacture or treatment -- Group H10D88/01 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10D84/01 , H10D84/02 , H10D84/032 , H10D84/035 , H10D84/05 , H10D84/07 and H10D84/08 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 88/101] comprising components on opposite major surfaces of semiconductor substrates -- Group H10D88/101 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/01 . Groups H10D84/01 and H10D88/101 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 8/812] Charge-trapping diodes -- Charge-trapping diodes are semiconductor diodes that use a charge-trapping layer or region to store carriers, enabling functions such as memory storage, over-voltage protection, or threshold adjustment. The classification covers designs where trapped charge influences the diode's electrical characteristics, a subset of inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 8/825] Diodes having bulk potential barriers, e.g. Camel diodes, planar doped barrier diodes or graded bandgap diodes -- H10D 8/825 covers diodes whose rectifying action is created by a bulk potential barrier rather than a p-n junction, examples being Camel diodes, planar doped barrier diodes and graded-bandgap diodes. These devices use a spatially varying doping or material composition to form a built-in electric field that controls carrier flow.
[H10D 89/00] Aspects of integrated devices not covered by groups -- H10D 89/00 covers miscellaneous aspects of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that are not specifically addressed in other groups, such as unconventional device structures, novel interconnections, special packaging or processing steps, and other unique features of integrated circuits.
[H10D 89/10] Integrated device layouts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Integrated device layouts, e.g. top-view representations of integrated circuits using planar geometrical shapes.
[H10D 89/105] adapted for thermal considerations -- H10D 89/105 in the Cooperative Patent Classification addresses aspects of integrated semiconductor devices specifically modified or designed to manage and optimize thermal performance, ensuring efficient heat dissipation and maintaining operational temperatures within safe limits.
[H10D 89/211] Design considerations for internal polarisation  integrated injection logic  H10D84/65 -- H10D 89/211 addresses the design of internal polarisation mechanisms in integrated injection-logic circuits, covering how device architecture, biasing schemes and material choices are engineered to create and control the built-in electric fields that enable IIL operation in inorganic semiconductor devices.
[H10D 89/213] in field-effect devices -- H10D 89/213 covers field-effect semiconductor devices that are not classified elsewhere, such as MOSFETs, JFETs, and related structures used in integrated circuits. The entry pertains to inorganic semiconductor devices where an electric field controls charge carrier flow, encompassing variations in channel design, gate insulation, and device geometries. It addresses specialized aspects of these field-effect components within broader semiconductor device classifications.
[H10D 89/215] comprising arrangements for charge pumping or biasing substrates -- This CPC subclass covers inorganic electric semiconductor devices that incorporate arrangements for charge pumping or biasing the substrate, used to adjust threshold voltages, improve performance, or provide body-bias control in integrated circuits. It is placed in H10D 89/215 for aspects of integrated devices not covered by other groups.
[H10D 89/217] comprising arrangements for charge injection in static induction transistor logic [SITL] devices -- H10D 89/217 covers semiconductor integrated circuits that include specific charge-injection structures used in static induction transistor logic (SITL) devices, describing arrangements that supply carriers to the transistor channels in these circuits. It falls under inorganic electric semiconductor devices.
[H10D 89/311] in bipolar devices -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers aspects of integrated inorganic semiconductor devices that operate as bipolar components, encompassing structures and functions of bipolar transistors and related circuits not addressed by more specific groups.
[H10D 89/60] Integrated devices comprising arrangements for electrical or thermal protection, e.g. protection circuits against electrostatic discharge [ESD] -- Groups H10D89/60 - H10D89/931 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10D84/01 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10D 89/601] for devices having insulated gate electrodes, e.g. for IGFETs or IGBTs -- The class H10D 89/601 covers semiconductor devices that employ insulated gate electrodes, such as insulated-gate field-effect transistors (IGFETs) and insulated-gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs). These components are used in power-electronic circuits for switching and controlling high-voltage or high-current loads, and are classified under integrated semiconductor devices not addressed by other groups.
[H10D 89/611] using diodes as protective elements -- H10D 89/611 relates to the use of diodes incorporated into integrated inorganic semiconductor devices for protective functions such as guarding against over-voltage, reverse-bias, or transient spikes. The classification falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, inorganic electric semiconductor devices, aspects of integrated devices not covered by other groups.
[H10D 89/711] using bipolar transistors as protective elements -- H10D 89/711 covers the use of bipolar transistors within integrated semiconductor devices to provide protection functions, such as over-current, voltage, or temperature safeguarding, that are not classified in more specific groups. The entry pertains to inorganic electric semiconductor devices under the broader electricity and semiconductor categories.
[H10D 89/713] including a PNP transistor and a NPN transistor, wherein each of said transistors has its base region coupled to the collector region of the other transistor, e.g. silicon controlled rectifier [SCR] devices -- H10D 89/713 covers integrated structures that combine a PNP and an NPN transistor such that each transistor's base is connected to the collector of the other, a configuration typical of silicon-controlled rectifier devices.
[H10D 89/811] using FETs as protective elements -- The H10D 89/811 subclass concerns semiconductor devices that incorporate field-effect transistors as protective elements, such as over-voltage, over-current or electrostatic-discharge clamps integrated in a circuit. It covers designs and methods where FETs are used to limit fault currents or clamp voltages, falling outside the other groups for integrated devices.
[H10D 89/813] specially adapted to provide an electrical current path other than the field-effect induced current path -- H10D 89/813 covers inorganic semiconductor integrated devices that provide an electrical current path independent of the field-effect channel, such as structures where current is supplied through built-in resistive or conductive paths, contacts or diffusion regions rather than a gate-controlled channel.
[H10D 89/814] involving a parasitic bipolar transistor triggered by the electrical biasing of the gate electrode of the FET, e.g. gate coupled transistors -- A parasitic bipolar transistor that is activated by the electrical bias applied to the gate of a field-effect transistor, such as gate-coupled transistor configurations, falls under CPC H10D 89/814, which addresses integrated inorganic semiconductor device aspects not covered elsewhere.
[H10D 89/815] involving a parasitic bipolar transistor triggered by the local electrical biasing of the layer acting as base region of said parasitic bipolar transistor -- A description of H10D 89/815 covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices where a parasitic bipolar transistor is unintentionally turned on by the local electrical bias applied to the layer that functions as its base region, addressing effects not captured in other groups.
[H10D 89/817] FETs in a Darlington configuration -- This subclass refers to circuits that combine two field-effect transistors in a Darlington configuration, where the output of one transistor controls the gate of a second to achieve high current gain. It includes designs where both devices are FETs rather than bipolar transistors, focusing on the electrical behaviour and integration of such paired devices.
[H10D 89/819] Bias arrangements for gate electrodes of FETs, e.g. RC networks or voltage partitioning circuits  FETs in a Darlington configuration  H10D89/817 -- This subclass covers bias arrangements for the gate electrodes of field-effect transistors, including resistor-capacitor networks, voltage-partitioning circuits, and configurations where a FET operates in a Darlington arrangement.
[H10D 89/911] using passive elements as protective elements -- This subgroup covers inorganic semiconductor devices in which passive components such as resistors, capacitors or inductors are incorporated to serve protective functions like over-voltage, electro-static discharge or surge suppression within the integrated circuit. It addresses protection schemes that are not covered by the other more specific groups in the H10D hierarchy.
[H10D 89/921] characterised by the configuration of the interconnections connecting the protective arrangements, e.g. ESD buses -- This subgroup covers integrated inorganic semiconductor devices where the layout of the interconnections that link protective circuits, such as electrostatic-discharge (ESD) buses, defines the invention. It addresses the specific arrangement of these connections rather than the protective functions themselves.
[H10D 89/931] characterised by the dispositions of the protective arrangements -- H10D 89/931 covers inorganic semiconductor integrated devices whose defining feature is the specific layout or positioning of protective structures such as guard rings, field plates or isolation zones that shield active regions from electrical stress. It includes any device where the arrangement of these protective elements is the primary distinguishing characteristic, rather than the device function or material composition.
[H10D 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- H10D 99/00 is a residual group for inorganic electric semiconductor devices that are not covered by any other more specific subgroup within subclass H10D. It is used for novel or uncommon device types, materials, structures or functions that fall under the broader field of inorganic semiconductor technology but do not fit predefined categories such as diodes, transistors, or thyristors. This catch-all group ensures such inventions can still be classified within the CPC hierarchy.
[H10F] INORGANIC SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES SENSITIVE TO INFRARED RADIATION, LIGHT, ELECTROMAGNETIC RADIATION OF SHORTER WAVELENGTH OR CORPUSCULAR RADIATION -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Inorganic semiconductor devices that are sensitive to infrared radiation, visible light, ultraviolet radiation, x-ray radiation, gamma radiation or corpuscular radiation, and which are specially adapted for either the conversion of the radiation energy into electrical energy or for the control of electrical energy by such radiation.  This includes the following kinds of devices:  This place also covers processes and apparatus specially adapted for the manufacture or treatment of such devices.  In this subclass, infrared radiation includes far infrared radiation having wavelengths between about 700 nm and about 1 mm.  In this subclass, the periodic system used is the I to VIII Group system indicated in the Periodic Table under Note (3) of section C .  **Glossary:** - chip: a piece of a wafer or a substrate that has been processed to contain devices therein or thereon. The expression "diced chip" refers to the result of dicing a wafer or a substrate into a plurality of chips, whereas "undiced chip" refers to a chip before dicing or with no dicing. - device: an electric circuit element (e.g. diode, transistor, LED, etc.); (depending on the context) can also refer to an integrated device (e.g. CMOS-IC, DRAM device, etc.). A device may be in the form of a bare or packaged chip. - dopant: the atoms or compounds added to a material during doping - doping: the intentional addition of a small quantity of atoms or compounds into a material to achieve a desired characteristic, e.g. to produce an n-type or p-type material - individual: refers to: an electric circuit element not being an integrated device; or a component of an integrated device. Examples of individual devices include: diodes, transistors, photovoltaic cells, Josephson-junction devices, light-emitting diodes [LED], organic LEDs or a single LED component within an integrated device. - integrated device: a device consisting of a plurality of semiconductor or other solid-state electric circuit elements formed in or on a common substrate - integrated circuit: an integrated device where all the electric circuit elements (e.g. diodes, transistors, LEDs, etc.) are formed in or on a common substrate, including interconnections between the elements - component: an electric circuit element (e.g. diode, transistor, LED, etc.) that is one of a plurality of elements formed in or on a common substrate, e.g. in an integrated device - wafer: this can be one of the following: (a) a slice of semiconductor or electric solid-state active material. For example: a slice of silicon; a slice of a semiconducting compound, e.g. gallium nitride [GaN]; a slice of lithium tantalate [LiTaO 3 ] for superconductor applications. (b) a multilayered laminate, having at least one layer of semiconductor or electric solid-state active material, the layer being meant to be processed into devices. For example: silicon-on-insulator [SOI]; silicon-on-glass [SOG]; silicon-on-sapphire [SOS]; a composite wafer comprising silicon carbide [SiC] on polycrystalline silicon [Si] support; a layer of semiconducting nanowires on glass. A wafer is typically processed by (e.g.) deposition, etching, doping or diffusion, and is then typically diced into chips. - body: the region of semiconductor (resp. solid-state) material(s) within which, or at the surface of which, the physical effects that are characteristic of the device occur, and any bordering semiconductor (resp. solid-state) material(s) that are contiguous with this region. Examples: in a field-effect transistor [FET], the physical effects occur in the channel region between the source and the drain. The semiconductor body includes the channel region, the source and drain regions, and any contiguous semiconductor material; in a light-emitting diode [LED], the physical effects occur at a junction of active semiconductor layers. The semiconductor body includes these active semiconductor layers and any contiguous semiconductor layers, such as buffer layers, possibly a growth substrate, etc., that are between the cathode and anode electrodes; in a thermoelectric device, the solid-state body includes all solid-state materials in the path of current between the electrodes. - electrode: a conductive region in or on the semiconductor body or solid-state body of a device (and other than the body itself) which exerts an electrical influence on the body, irrespective of whether or not an external electrical connection is made thereto. The term covers metallic regions which exert electrical influence on the body through an insulating region (e.g. in intentional non-parasitic capacitive coupling), or inductive coupling arrangements. In a capacitive coupling arrangement, the dielectric region is regarded as part of the electrode. The overall conductive wiring may comprise multiple portions. In such a case, only the wiring portions that exert an electrical influence on the body are considered portions of the electrode. Examples: conductive layer(s) in direct physical contact with the body; conductive region(s) exerting an inductive coupling onto the body; a multilayer structure which exerts influence on the body through an insulating region, e.g. in intentional non-parasitic capacitive coupling. - interconnection: a conductive arrangement for conducting electric current from an electrode of a circuit element to another part of the circuit. Examples include metal wirings. - container: a solid construction in which (one or more) devices are placed, or which is formed around the devices, for forming packaged devices. A container requires a partial or total enclosure and it may also comprise a filling. - encapsulation: an enclosure consisting of (one or more) layers, e.g. comprising organic polymers, which at least partially enclose the (one or more) devices, thereby protecting them. An encapsulation is often used to hermetically seal devices. - field-effect: refers to semiconductor technology wherein a voltage applied to a gate electrode creates an electric field that allows for control of current near the interface of the gate and the body, e.g. to create an inversion channel between the source and drain of a MOSFET - package: the collection of all elements, which are external to the chip, that protect the chip or connect it to another object. Package therefore covers encapsulations, containers, package substrates, interposers, heatsinks or the like. Package does not include objects at a higher system level, like circuit boards and beyond, e.g. a housing in which the circuit board is enclosed. - unipolar: refers to semiconductor technology that primarily involves one type only of charge carrier, i.e. it involves either holes or electrons but not both - bipolar: refers to semiconductor technology that involves multi-carrier-type operation, i.e. which simultaneously uses both electrons and holes as charge carriers - MIS: metal-insulator-semiconductor - MOS: metal-oxide-semiconductor - FET: field-effect transistor - MISFET: metal-insulator-semiconductor field-effect transistor - TFT: thin-film transistor - active material: material within which the physical effects that are characteristic of the device occur - coating: one or more layers which are formed essentially conformally on and directly associated with at least a portion of the semiconductor device, e.g. having passivating or optical effects - Group IV material: material comprising only Group IV elements, except for dopants or other impurities - Group III-V material: material comprising only Group III and Group V elements, except for dopants or other impurities - Group II-VI material: material comprising only Group II and Group VI elements, except for dopants or other impurities - Group I-III-VI material: material comprising only Group I, Group III and Group VI elements, except for dopants or other impurities - heterojunction: interface between two different materials, the difference lying in the crystal structure and/or the composition (example: p-type amorphous silicon/n-type crystalline silicon) - homojunction: interface between materials having the same composition, the same crystal structure and different dopants or dopant concentrations - hybrid: refers to an image sensor having two substrates that are connected together, wherein the sensing elements are in one substrate and the driving circuitry is in the other substrate - intrinsic region or layer: semiconductor region or layer that is undoped or not intentionally doped such that electron and hole densities are approximately equal - junction box: enclosure attached to a photovoltaic module for electrically connecting modules together - photovoltaic cell, solar cell: photovoltaic device that converts electromagnetic radiation (e.g. light) into direct current electricity for the purpose of providing electrical energy (not for light detection purposes). It is often, but not necessarily, part of a photovoltaic module. - photovoltaic module: assembly of multiple photovoltaic cells that are electrically connected together - PIN, p-i-n: junction having a p-type region/intrinsic region/n-type region structure, wherein the intrinsic layer is the light-absorbing layer. Contrast with SHJ photovoltaic cells, wherein the intrinsic layer is not the light-absorbing layer. - PN, p-n: junction having a p-type region/n-type region structure - Silicon heterojunction [SHJ] photovoltaic cell: heterostructure comprising two silicon materials having different crystalline structure and including a very thin intrinsic interlayer therebetween, which is not the light-absorbing layer - Schottky barrier: metal/semiconductor barrier that is non-ohmic and rectifying - multiple-junction photovoltaic cell, tandem photovoltaic cell: photovoltaic cell comprising multiple photovoltaic subcells formed on one another to form a single integrated structure between a single pair of anode/cathode electrodes. Typically, each subcell has a different spectral sensitivity than each other. Tunnel junctions between subcells usually ensure the electrical connection and the current flow between the subcells. - up-conversion: process wherein two or more lower energy photons are converted to a single high energy photon to be absorbed by the photovoltaic cell. Up conversion layers are electrically isolated from the photovoltaic cell and are usually between the back reflector and the photovoltaic cell.
[H10F 10/00] Photovoltaics -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual inorganic photovoltaic devices that convert electromagnetic radiation (e.g. light) into direct current electricity for the purpose of providing electrical energy (not for light detection purposes). Such devices are often, but not necessarily, part of a photovoltaic module.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Electrolytic light-sensitive devices, e.g. dye-sensitized photovoltaic cells -> H01G9/20
[H10F 10/10] having potential barriers -- The H10F 10/10 subclass covers inorganic semiconductor photovoltaic devices that employ potential barriers, such as p-n or heterojunctions, to separate and collect charge carriers. It includes devices sensitive to infrared, visible, ultraviolet or other short-wavelength radiation, describing how the barrier structure influences their operation.
[H10F 10/11] Photovoltaic cells having point contact potential barriers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells wherein electrodes make ohmic contact to the active region at small-area "points."  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate a photovoltaic cell (32) having thin, active InGaAs layer (38) formed on a bulk, inactive substrate (36). Localised point p+ and n+ regions (40) facilitate extraction of electrons and holes from the active layer through ohmic contacts.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Photovoltaic cells having Schottky potential barriers -> H10F10/18
[H10F 10/12] Photovoltaic cells having only metal-insulator-semiconductor [MIS] potential barriers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells wherein the potential barrier consists of a metal-insulator-semiconductor (MIS) structure.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a photovoltaic cell having a MIS structure comprising metallic second electrodes, a thin insulator layer, and a semiconductor (208).
[H10F 10/13] Photovoltaic cells having absorbing layers comprising graded bandgaps -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells wherein the absorbing region or layer has a varying bandgap.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a bandgap diagram, wherein the bandgap energy Eg of a CIGSS light absorbing layer (30) is varying in region B, which is caused by adjusting the concentration of gallium or sulfur.
[H10F 10/14] Photovoltaic cells having only PN homojunction potential barriers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells wherein the potential barrier is a PN junction wherein the p-type and n-type regions are made out of one single material (i.e. having the same composition and the same crystal structure).  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a photovoltaic cell having a PN homojunction wherein the p-type layer and the n-type layer are made of the same material and have different dopants.
[H10F 10/142] comprising multiple PN homojunctions, e.g. tandem cells -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells having multiple subcells between a single set of anode/cathode electrodes, wherein the subcells each have PN homojunctions.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a tandem photovoltaic cell (10) having three subcells (20, 40 and 60) that are connected in series between a pair of anode/cathode electrodes (top metal contact [14] and bottom metal contact [68]). Subcell (20) comprises GaInP regions, subcell (40) comprises GaInAs regions, and subcell (60) comprises Ge regions.
[H10F 10/1425] Inverted metamorphic multi-junction [IMM] photovoltaic cells -- Inverted metamorphic multi-junction photovoltaic cells are semiconductor devices that stack several p-n junctions with progressively larger bandgaps, grown in a reverse order to accommodate lattice-mismatch and capture a broader spectrum of light, thereby boosting solar-energy conversion efficiency.
[H10F 10/144] comprising only Group III-V materials, e.g. GaAs,AlGaAs, or InP photovoltaic cells -- The CPC subclass H10F 10/144 groups photovoltaic devices that are made solely from Group III-V compound semiconductors such as gallium arsenide, aluminum gallium arsenide, or indium phosphide. These cells respond to infrared, visible or shorter-wavelength radiation and are classified under inorganic semiconductor devices within the electricity section of the CPC.
[H10F 10/146] Back-junction photovoltaic cells, e.g. having interdigitated base-emitter regions on the back side -- H10F 10/146 refers to back-junction photovoltaic cells, in which the p-n junction is formed on the rear side of the semiconductor and may include interdigitated base-emitter regions. This design lets light enter the front surface unimpeded, enhancing carrier collection and cell efficiency. The entry is placed under inorganic semiconductor devices sensitive to infrared, light, and other short-wavelength radiation.
[H10F 10/148] Double-emitter photovoltaic cells, e.g. bifacial photovoltaic cells -- H10F 10/148 covers photovoltaic cells that incorporate two light-absorbing emitters, typically on opposite faces, allowing generation of electricity from illumination on either side. Known as bifacial cells, they improve energy yield compared to single-sided devices, often using silicon or other inorganic semiconductors and are employed in modules where rear-side light is reflected or diffuse.
[H10F 10/16] Photovoltaic cells having only PN heterojunction potential barriers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells wherein the junction is a PN structure involving two different materials (i.e. having different composition or crystal structures).
[H10F 10/161] comprising multiple PN heterojunctions, e.g. tandem cells -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells having multiple subcells between a single set of anode/cathode electrodes, wherein the subcells each have PN heterojunctions.
[H10F 10/162] comprising only Group II-VI materials, e.g. CdS/CdTe photovoltaic cells -- H10F 10/162 covers photovoltaic devices that employ only group II-VI semiconductor compounds, such as cadmium sulfide or cadmium telluride cells, as the light-absorbing material. It falls under inorganic semiconductor devices sensitive to infrared, visible or shorter-wavelength radiation within the broader electricity and semiconductor device categories.
[H10F 10/163] comprising only Group III-V materials, e.g. GaAs/AlGaAs or InP/GaInAs photovoltaic cells -- This subgroup covers photovoltaic devices that use only group III-V compound semiconductors, such as gallium arsenide/aluminium gallium arsenide or indium phosphide/gallium indium arsenide junctions. These cells convert light into electricity and are classified under H10F 10/163 in the CPC.
[H10F 10/164] comprising heterojunctions with Group IV materials, e.g. ITO/Si or GaAs/SiGe photovoltaic cells -- The CPC subclass H10F 10/164 covers photovoltaic cells that incorporate heterojunctions formed with Group-IV materials, for example indium-tin-oxide on silicon or gallium-arsenide on silicon-germanium structures. These devices belong to inorganic semiconductor devices designed to convert light into electricity.
[H10F 10/165] the heterojunctions being Group IV-IV heterojunctions, e.g. Si/Ge, SiGe/Si or Si/SiC photovoltaic cells -- H10F 10/165 pertains to inorganic semiconductor photovoltaic devices that employ Group IV-IV heterojunctions, such as silicon-germanium, silicon-germanium on silicon, or silicon on silicon-carbide. The classification covers heterojunction solar cells where the junction is formed between two Group IV materials, enabling conversion of light, infrared or short-wavelength radiation into electricity.
[H10F 10/166] the Group IV-IV heterojunctions being heterojunctions of crystalline and amorphous materials, e.g. silicon heterojunction [SHJ] photovoltaic cells -- **Definition:** This place covers:  PN heterojunction photovoltaic cells wherein the heterojunction barrier material consists of a heterojunction between crystalline and amorphous materials.  The PN heterojunction may comprise a thin intrinsic buffer layer between the p-type and n-type layers, provided that the intrinsic layer is not the light-absorbing layer.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates a SHJ photovoltaic cell comprising a heterojunction between n-type crystalline silicon layer (1) and amorphous silicon layers (2 and 3).
[H10F 10/167] comprising Group I-III-VI materials, e.g. CdS/CuInSe -- **Definition:** This place covers:  PN heterojunction photovoltaic cells wherein the heterojunction barrier includes at least one Group I-III-VI material, e.g. cadmium sulfide/copper indium selenide [CdS/CuInSe 2 ].
[H10F 10/169] comprising Cu 2 X/CdX heterojunctions, wherein X is a Group VI element, e.g. Cu 2 O/CdO PN heterojunction photovoltaic cells -- The CPC subclass H10F 10/169 relates to photovoltaic devices that employ a heterojunction formed between copper-based compounds Cu2X and cadmium-based compounds CdX, where X is a Group VI element such as oxygen, sulfur, selenium or tellurium. These cells exploit the PN junction between the two inorganic semiconductors for light-to-electric conversion.
[H10F 10/17] Photovoltaic cells having only PIN junction potential barriers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Photovoltaic cells wherein the potential barrier consists of a PIN structure, wherein the intrinsic layer is the major light-absorbing layer. For example, amorphous silicon PIN photovoltaic cells or perovskite photovoltaic cells having only inorganic active materials.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[H10F 10/172] comprising multiple PIN junctions, e.g. tandem cells -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Individual photovoltaic cells having multiple subcells, wherein the subcells each have PIN junction barriers.
[H10F 10/174] comprising monocrystalline or polycrystalline materials -- H10F 10/174 covers photovoltaic devices that employ inorganic semiconductor chips made from monocrystalline or polycrystalline material, such as crystalline silicon solar cells, for converting light into electricity. It lies within the hierarchy Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Inorganic Semiconductor Devices Sensitive to Infrared, Light, or Short-Wavelength Radiation → Photovoltaics.
[H10F 10/18] Photovoltaic cells having only Schottky potential barriers -- H10F 10/18 refers to photovoltaic cells that generate electric power solely through Schottky potential barriers, lacking traditional p-n junctions. These inorganic semiconductor devices convert light, including infrared and shorter-wavelength radiation, directly into electricity and are used in solar energy applications.
[H10F 10/19] Photovoltaic cells having multiple potential barriers of different types, e.g. tandem cells having both PN and PIN junctions -- The subclass H10F 10/19 pertains to photovoltaic cells that incorporate several different potential-barrier structures in one device, such as tandem cells that combine a PN junction with a PIN junction to raise conversion efficiency. It sits within the inorganic semiconductor devices sensitive to radiation, specifically under photovoltaics.
[H10F 19/00] Integrated devices, or assemblies of multiple devices, comprising at least one photovoltaic cell covered by group -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Integrated devices, or assemblies of multiple devices, comprising at least one photovoltaic cell, e.g. photovoltaic arrays, modules or panels. This includes configurations, dispositions or shapes of the photovoltaic cells in the integrated devices or assemblies.
[H10F 19/10] comprising photovoltaic cells in arrays in a single semiconductor substrate, the photovoltaic cells having vertical junctions or V-groove junctions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Photovoltaic cells formed in a bulk semiconductor substrate either having a plurality of vertical junctions or having junctions that are at least partly in V-shaped grooves.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.  Figure 1 illustrates photovoltaic cells (204) formed on substrate (202), the photovoltaic cells (204) having vertical PN junctions.  2.  Figure 2 illustrates photovoltaic cell (66) comprising layers GaAlAs layers (72, 74 and 76), and photovoltaic cell (60) comprising Si intrinsic layers between GaP n-type and p-type layers. Both cells are both formed on substrate (62). The subcells of (66) are separated by V-shaped grooves that have been etched into the GaAlAs layers (72, 74 and 76). The subcells of (60) are separated by V-shaped grooves that have been etched into the Si layer.
[H10F 19/20] comprising photovoltaic cells in arrays in or on a single semiconductor substrate, the photovoltaic cells having planar junctions -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Photovoltaic cells in arrays in or on a single semiconductor substrate, the cells having planar junctions. Often, the cells each comprise a portion of the semiconductor substrate and a layer that is thereon, using the portion of the semiconductor substrate for its electrical properties.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1a.  1b.  Figures 1a and 1b illustrate an array of photovoltaic cells (620), each having a PN junction between n-type layer (605) and p-type layer (603). Trenches are etched between adjacent cells, and SiO 2 isolation regions (614) isolate the cells within the substrate (601). [H10F 19/30] comprising thin-film photovoltaic cells -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Integrated devices or assemblies including inorganic thin-film photovoltaic cells, e.g. single thin film a-Si, CIS or CdTe photovoltaic cells.  The group H10F19/30 itself covers an assembly of multiple photovoltaic cells, each formed on its own substrate and interconnected. The groups indented under H10F19/30 cover integrated devices. [H10F 19/31] having multiple laterally adjacent thin-film photovoltaic cells deposited on the same substrate -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Integrated devices including multiple inorganic thin-film photovoltaic cells deposited on the same substrate. Typically, the substrate is insulating and the cells do not use the substrate for its electrical properties.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:  The Figure illustrates integrated thin film photovoltaic cells formed of surface electrode SL, semiconductor layer L and rear surface electrode RSL connected in series through contact line (7).
[H10F 19/33] Patterning processes to connect the photovoltaic cells, e.g. laser cutting of conductive or active layers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Specific patterning methods (like laser trimming, chemical etching) which aims at forming a module from a plurality of (interconnected) adjacent thin film photovoltaic cells from initially continuous thin films.
[H10F 19/35] Structures for the connecting of adjacent photovoltaic cells, e.g. interconnections or insulating spacers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Structures related to the interconnecting of adjacent thin film photovoltaic cells. Examples include interconnections and insulating spacers that prevent short-circuits between cells.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:  1.
