It includes processes that use a focused laser beam to selectively etch or form structures on organic thin-film devices.
[H10K 71/164] using vacuum deposition -- The H10K 71/164 subclass covers the manufacture of organic electric solid-state devices by means of vacuum deposition, such as depositing organic semiconductor layers for LEDs, solar cells or transistors. It addresses processes that form the active organic films under vacuum conditions.
[H10K 71/166] using selective deposition, e.g. using a mask -- The subclass H10K 71/166 covers methods for fabricating organic solid-state devices in which the active material is deposited only in selected areas, typically by employing a mask or other selective-deposition technique. It pertains to manufacturing or treatment processes that create patterned organic layers during device production.
[H10K 71/18] using non-liquid printing techniques, e.g. thermal transfer printing from a donor sheet -- This CPC subclass H10K 71/18 concerns the manufacture or treatment of organic electric solid-state devices using non-liquid printing techniques such as thermal transfer printing from a donor sheet. It is located in the hierarchy electricity - semiconductor devices - organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 71/191] characterised by provisions for the orientation or alignment of the layer to be deposited -- The H10K 71/191 subclass covers manufacturing or treatment steps for organic solid-state semiconductor devices in which the deposited active layer is deliberately oriented or aligned, such as by aligning layers, fields, substrate patterning or surface-treatment techniques. It focuses on process features that control the molecular or crystalline direction of the layer to improve device performance.
[H10K 71/20] Changing the shape of the active layer in the devices, e.g. patterning -- The H10K 71/20 subclass addresses manufacturing steps that modify the geometry of the organic active layer in solid-state devices, such as organic light-emitting diodes or photovoltaic cells. It includes techniques for shaping, patterning or otherwise structuring the active layer to achieve desired electrical or optical performance, for example through photolithography, embossing or selective deposition. This falls under the broader category of organic semiconductor device fabrication.
[H10K 71/211] by selective transformation of an existing layer -- This CPC subclass (H10K 71/211) covers manufacturing or treatment steps for organic electric solid-state devices that involve selectively transforming an existing layer, such as doping, annealing, patterning or chemically modifying a pre-deposited film to alter its electrical properties. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 71/221] by lift-off techniques -- H10K 71/221 covers manufacturing and treatment steps for organic electric solid-state devices that employ lift-off techniques, where a patterned sacrificial layer is removed to define conductive or active regions without etching the underlying material. This subclass lies within electricity → semiconductor devices → organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 71/231] by etching of existing layers -- The CPC subclass H10K 71/231 covers manufacturing or treatment steps for organic electric solid-state devices that involve etching of existing layers. It includes processes that selectively remove material from previously deposited organic films to define patterns, structures or contacts in devices such as organic LEDs, solar cells and thin-film transistors. This subclass falls under the hierarchy Electricity → Semiconductor devices → Organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 71/233] by photolithographic etching -- H10K 71/233 covers the manufacturing step in which organic electric solid-state devices are patterned by photolithographic etching. The method uses light-sensitive masks to selectively remove material and define conductive, semiconductive, or insulating regions on organic layers, enabling the creation of circuits such as organic transistors or light-emitting diodes.
[H10K 71/236] using printing techniques, e.g. applying the etch liquid using an ink jet printer -- H10K 71/236 covers the use of printing methods, such as applying an etch liquid with an ink-jet printer, in the manufacture or treatment of organic solid-state electric devices, including organic semiconductor components.
[H10K 71/30] Doping active layers, e.g. electron transporting layers -- H10K 71/30 covers processes for doping the active layers of organic solid-state electronic devices, such as electron-transporting layers, to modify their electrical characteristics. It includes methods of introducing dopants into these layers during manufacture or post-treatment to improve charge mobility, conductivity, or stability of the device.
[H10K 71/311] Purifying organic semiconductor materials -- H10K 71/311 covers processes for removing impurities from organic semiconductor compounds used in solid-state electronic devices such as organic light-emitting diodes and organic photovoltaic cells. The classification includes chemical, physical or combined purification techniques applied during material preparation.
[H10K 71/40] Thermal treatment, e.g. annealing in the presence of a solvent vapour -- It covers thermal processing steps for organic semiconductor structures, such as annealing in a solvent-vapor atmosphere to modify layer morphology, crystallinity or interface properties and thereby improve device performance. The methods apply to organic electric solid-state devices including OLEDs, organic transistors and similar components.
[H10K 71/421] using coherent electromagnetic radiation, e.g. laser annealing -- The code covers processes that apply coherent electromagnetic radiation, such as laser annealing, to treat or manufacture organic solid-state semiconductor devices. It includes using laser light to modify the structure, crystallinity or electrical properties of organic layers in devices like organic LEDs, solar cells or transistors. This falls under the CPC group for semiconductor devices made from organic materials.
[H10K 71/441] in the presence of solvent vapors, e.g. solvent vapour annealing -- H10K 71/441 covers processes for fabricating or treating organic solid-state electronic devices in which the material is exposed to solvent vapors, such as solvent-vapor annealing, to modify morphology, crystallinity or performance. The technique is used to improve charge transport or stability of organic semiconductors.
[H10K 71/50] Forming devices by joining two substrates together, e.g. lamination techniques -- H10K 71/50 relates to manufacturing organic solid-state electronic devices by joining two substrate layers, typically using lamination or similar bonding techniques to create integrated structures.
[H10K 71/60] Forming conductive regions or layers, e.g. electrodes -- This CPC subclass H10K 71/60 covers processes for creating conductive regions or layers, such as electrodes, in organic solid-state devices like organic LEDs or solar cells. It includes methods of deposition, patterning, doping or printing that form the electrical contacts needed for device operation. The classification falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, organic electric solid-state devices, manufacture or treatment.
[H10K 71/611] using printing deposition, e.g. ink jet printing -- This CPC subclass covers the manufacture or treatment of organic electric solid-state devices by depositing organic materials through printing processes such as ink jet printing, including methods that form functional layers or patterns of organic semiconductors via printed deposition.
[H10K 71/621] Providing a shape to conductive layers, e.g. patterning or selective deposition -- This subgroup concerns processes that give a defined geometry to the conductive layers of organic solid-state electronic components, including techniques such as photolithographic patterning, ink-jet or screen printing, and selective material deposition to form electrodes or interconnects during device fabrication. It applies to organic LEDs, organic photovoltaics and similar devices.
[H10K 71/70] Testing, e.g. accelerated lifetime tests -- H10K 71/70 covers testing methods for organic solid-state semiconductor devices, such as accelerated lifetime and reliability tests, used to assess performance and durability during manufacturing or treatment. These tests help determine device stability under electrical stress.
[H10K 71/80] using temporary substrates -- This subclass covers methods for fabricating organic solid-state electronic components in which a temporary carrier substrate is employed during processing, such as for handling, patterning or transferring the active layers before the final device is mounted on its permanent substrate.
[H10K 71/811] Controlling the atmosphere during processing  H10K71/40  takes precedence -- H10K 71/811 falls under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Organic Electric Solid-State Devices and covers methods for controlling the processing atmosphere of such devices, such as inert or reactive gas environments during fabrication or treatment. It is a manufacturing-or-treatment subclass, but it is subordinate to the broader H10K 71/40.
[H10K 71/821] Patterning of a layer by embossing, e.g. stamping to form trenches in an insulating layer -- H10K 71/821 covers techniques for forming patterns in insulating layers of organic solid-state semiconductor devices by embossing or stamping, such as creating trenches or relief structures. The method is employed during the manufacture or treatment of organic electric components to define circuit features without photolithography.
[H10K 71/831] Aging -- H10K 71/831 pertains to the ageing of organic solid-state electric devices, covering manufacturing or treatment steps that assess or accelerate performance degradation over time, including methods for monitoring changes in electrical characteristics, stability testing, and techniques to improve device longevity.
[H10K 71/841] Applying alternating current [AC] during manufacturing or treatment -- The code H10K 71/841 covers processes that use alternating current during the manufacture or treatment of organic electric solid-state devices such as organic semiconductors and light-emitting diodes. It includes applying AC to influence material formation, doping, annealing, or performance-enhancing steps in the production line.
[H10K 71/851] Division of substrate -- This CPC subclass H10K 71/851 covers methods for dividing a semiconductor substrate into separate pieces, typically after growth or processing, used in organic solid-state devices such as OLEDs. It addresses cutting, sawing, dicing, or other separation techniques that prepare individual chips for further assembly.
[H10K 71/861] Repairing -- The CPC subclass H10K 71/861 covers processes for repairing organic electric solid-state devices, such as organic light-emitting diodes and organic photovoltaic cells. It includes techniques for restoring functionality, replacing damaged layers, and reconditioning the materials that constitute these semiconductor components. This falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and the manufacture or treatment of organic solid-state devices.
[H10K 77/00] Constructional details of devices covered by this subclass and not covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  This place covers details that are generic or generally applicable to all device types of H10K , e.g. transparent or flexible substrates.
[H10K 77/10] Substrates, e.g. flexible substrates -- H10K 77/10 relates to the constructional features of substrates used in organic solid-state devices, including flexible materials such as plastic or polymer films that support thin-film organic semiconductor layers. It covers design and manufacturing aspects of these substrates that are not addressed by more specific groups in the subclass.
[H10K 77/111] Flexible substrates -- H10K 77/111 lists the constructional details of flexible substrates employed in organic electric solid-state devices such as OLEDs or organic solar cells, covering material types, layer structures, and mechanical configurations that are not addressed by other groups in the subclass. This subclass falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → organic electric solid-state devices.

[H10K 85/113] Heteroaromatic compounds comprising sulfur or selene, e.g. polythiophene -- This subclass concerns organic semiconductor materials whose heteroaromatic rings include sulfur or selenium atoms, exemplified by polythiophene and related polymers, which serve as active layers or electrodes in solid-state electronic devices. [H10K 85/146] poly N-vinylcarbazol; Derivatives thereof -- The entry H10K 85/146 covers the organic polymer poly N-vinylcarbazole and its derivatives when used as the active material in the body or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices, such as organic light-emitting diodes or organic transistors. [H10K 85/151] Copolymers -- Copolymers used as the active organic material or as electrode constituents in solid-state semiconductor devices, such as organic light-emitting diodes, photovoltaic cells and field-effect transistors, are classified under H10K 85/151. The subclass includes any copolymer composition engineered to improve charge transport, conductivity, optical properties or stability of the device.
[H10K 85/154] Ladder-type polymers -- Ladder-type polymers are conjugated organic macromolecules with a rigid, fused-ring backbone that forms a ladder-like structure, providing high thermal stability and charge-transport properties. They are used as active layers or electrodes in organic solid-state devices such as field-effect transistors and light-emitting diodes.
[H10K 85/20] Carbon compounds, e.g. carbon nanotubes or fullerenes -- The H10K 85/20 subclass covers carbon-based compounds such as carbon nanotubes and fullerenes that serve as organic materials in the body or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices. These materials are employed to improve conductivity, mechanical strength, or functional properties of organic electric components.
[H10K 85/211] Fullerenes, e.g. C 60 -- H10K 85/211 pertains to the use of fullerenes such as C60 as organic materials in the active layers or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices. The classification includes devices where fullerenes provide charge-transport or light-absorbing functions in organic electronic components.
[H10K 85/215] comprising substituents, e.g. PCBM -- H10K 85/215 covers organic solid-state semiconductor materials that incorporate substituent groups, for example fullerene derivatives such as PCBM, used in the active layer or electrodes of electronic devices. These materials are designed to modify electronic properties, improve charge transport, or enhance stability in devices like organic photovoltaics and light-emitting diodes.
[H10K 85/221] Carbon nanotubes -- The CPC subclass H10K 85/221 covers carbon nanotubes used as organic materials in the active layers or electrodes of semiconductor devices. It includes single-walled and multi-walled nanotubes employed for charge transport, sensing, or flexible electronics within solid-state circuits.
[H10K 85/225] comprising substituents -- H10K 85/225 pertains to organic solid-state devices in which the active organic material contains substituents attached to the core molecular structure. These substituents are employed to tailor electrical, optical or mechanical properties of the semiconductor layer used in devices such as organic LEDs, solar cells or transistors. This subclass falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 85/30] Coordination compounds -- H10K 85/30 pertains to coordination compounds used as organic active materials in solid-state electronic devices such as organic light-emitting diodes, transistors and photovoltaic cells. The subclass includes metal-ligand complexes that function as conductors, semiconductors or charge-transport layers within the device electrodes or body.
[H10K 85/311] Phthalocyanine -- H10K 85/311 relates to the use of phthalocyanine compounds as organic semiconductor materials in the active layer or electrodes of solid-state electronic devices, including organic light-emitting diodes, photovoltaic cells and sensors.
[H10K 85/321] Metal complexes comprising a group IIIA element, e.g. Tris (8-hydroxyquinoline) gallium [Gaq3] -- The entry H10K 85/321 covers metal-complex organic solid-state devices that incorporate a Group IIIA element, such as tris-(8-hydroxyquinoline) gallium (Gaq₃). It includes semiconductor or electroluminescent structures where the metal complex functions as the active material in the body or electrodes of the device.
[H10K 85/322] comprising boron -- H10K 85/322 covers organic solid-state devices whose body or electrodes incorporate boron. It applies to semiconductor components such as organic light-emitting diodes, photovoltaic cells and transistors that use boron-based organic materials.
[H10K 85/324] comprising aluminium, e.g. Alq3 -- H10K 85/324 covers organic solid-state devices that incorporate aluminium compounds such as tris(8-hydroxyquinolinato)aluminium (Alq3) as the emissive or charge-transport material. It is a subcategory of organic electric solid-state devices within semiconductor devices.
[H10K 85/326] comprising gallium -- H10K 85/326 includes organic solid-state semiconductor devices that incorporate gallium in the organic material forming the device body or electrodes, such as organic transistors, diodes or optoelectronic components where gallium is part of the active organic layer.
[H10K 85/331] Metal complexes comprising an iron-series metal, e.g. Fe, Co, Ni -- The subclass covers organic solid-state devices that employ metal-complex materials containing iron-group metals such as iron, cobalt or nickel as active or electrode components. These complexes are used to modify electrical, optical or catalytic properties within the device structure.
[H10K 85/341] Transition metal complexes, e.g. Ru(II)polypyridine complexes  H10K85/331  takes precedence -- H10K 85/341 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (EPO/USPTO) pertains to organic materials used in semiconductor devices, specifically focusing on transition metal complexes such as Ru(II)polypyridine complexes. This classification is a subset of organic electric solid-state devices and takes precedence over similar entries when applicable.
[H10K 85/342] comprising iridium -- The H10K 85/342 subclass pertains to organic solid-state devices in which iridium is present in the active material or electrode, such as iridium-doped organic light-emitting diodes or photovoltaic cells. It distinguishes these iridium-containing designs from other organic semiconductor configurations.
[H10K 85/344] comprising ruthenium -- The code H10K 85/344 classifies organic solid-state devices that incorporate ruthenium in the device body or as part of an electrode material. It sits within the CPC hierarchy electricity, semiconductor devices, organic electric solid-state devices. Patents using ruthenium-based organic compounds for such devices are placed in this entry.
[H10K 85/346] comprising platinum -- This CPC subclass (H10K 85/346) covers organic solid-state semiconductor devices in which the organic material used in the body or electrodes contains platinum. It falls under the hierarchy electricity → semiconductor devices → organic electric solid-state devices, specifying platinum as a component of the organic layer.
[H10K 85/348] comprising osmium -- The H10K 85/348 subclass covers solid-state semiconductor devices whose organic active layers or electrodes incorporate osmium. It concerns organic electric solid-state components, such as organic light-emitting diodes or photovoltaic cells, where osmium is used as part of the material composition. This entry falls under the hierarchy of electricity → semiconductor devices → organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 85/351] Metal complexes comprising lanthanides or actinides, e.g. comprising europium -- The entry H10K 85/351 covers metal complexes that contain lanthanide or actinide elements such as europium and are used as organic materials in the bodies or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices. These complexes modify electrical, optical or magnetic properties of organic electronic components.
[H10K 85/361] Polynuclear complexes, i.e. complexes comprising two or more metal centers -- H10K 85/361 covers polynuclear complexes, meaning compounds that contain two or more metal centers, when they are employed as organic materials in the body or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices. The classification applies to such metal-centered organics used in electricity-related technologies.
[H10K 85/371] Metal complexes comprising a group IB metal element, e.g. comprising copper, gold or silver -- The subclass covers organic solid-state devices whose active organic material contains metal complexes of a group IB element such as copper, gold or silver. These complexes may be incorporated into the body of the device or into its electrodes, influencing electrical performance.
[H10K 85/381] Metal complexes comprising a group IIB metal element, e.g. comprising cadmium, mercury or zinc -- The H10K 85/381 subclass covers metal complexes that contain a group IIB element such as cadmium, mercury or zinc and are used as organic materials in the body or electrodes of semiconductor devices. These complexes are employed in organic solid-state components, where they contribute to electrical conductivity, charge transport or optoelectronic functionality within devices classified under electricity-> semiconductor devices-> organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 85/40] Organosilicon compounds, e.g. TIPS pentacene -- Organosilicon compounds such as TIPS pentacene are used as active semiconducting layers or electrode-contact materials in organic solid-state devices. Their silicon-based substituents improve solubility, film-forming ability, and charge-carrier mobility, enhancing device performance and stability. The classification covers any organic electric device that employs these organosilicon materials.
[H10K 85/50] Organic perovskites -- H10K 85/50 covers organic perovskite compounds used as the active material or electrode in semiconductor devices. These hybrid organic-inorganic crystals are employed in solid-state components such as solar cells, LEDs and photodetectors for their tunable optoelectronic properties. The entry falls within the organic electric solid-state devices subclass of semiconductor devices.
[H10K 85/60] Organic compounds having low molecular weight -- H10K 85/60 covers low-molecular-weight organic compounds employed as active materials in organic solid-state devices such as organic light-emitting diodes, organic solar cells and thin-film transistors. These substances serve as the body or electrode components of the device and typically include small molecules rather than polymers. The classification is part of the CPC hierarchy under electricity, semiconductor devices, organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 85/611] Charge transfer complexes -- H10K 85/611 covers organic charge-transfer complexes employed as the active material or electrode component of solid-state semiconductor devices. These are donor-acceptor molecular assemblies that facilitate charge transport and are used in organic LEDs, solar cells and transistors. The subclass addresses materials that are part of the device body or electrodes.
[H10K 85/615] Polycyclic condensed aromatic hydrocarbons, e.g. anthracene -- The H10K 85/615 subclass covers the use of polycyclic condensed aromatic hydrocarbons such as anthracene as organic materials in the active layers or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices. These compounds serve as charge-transport or light-emitting components in organic electronic applications.
[H10K 85/621] Aromatic anhydride or imide compounds, e.g. perylene tetra-carboxylic dianhydride or perylene tetracarboxylic di-imide -- H10K 85/621 covers aromatic anhydride or imide compounds, for example perylene tetra-carboxylic dianhydride and perylene tetracarboxylic di-imide, used as organic materials in the active layers or electrodes of organic solid-state semiconductor devices.
[H10K 85/622] containing four rings, e.g. pyrene -- Organic semiconductor materials that comprise a fused four-ring structure, exemplified by pyrene, used as the active layer or electrode component in solid-state devices such as organic light-emitting diodes, solar cells or transistors. This entry groups compounds whose molecular architecture contains exactly four aromatic rings, providing specific guidance for devices that rely on such polycyclic structures.
[H10K 85/623] containing five rings, e.g. pentacene -- This subclass covers organic semiconductor materials that contain five fused aromatic rings, such as pentacene, used as the active layer or electrode material in organic solid-state electronic devices.
[H10K 85/624] containing six or more rings -- The subclass covers organic semiconductor materials whose molecular structure contains six or more rings, including fused or linked polycyclic systems used as the active layer or electrode in organic solid-state devices such as OLEDs, organic photovoltaic cells and transistors. Examples include perylene, quinacridone and similar multi-ring organic dyes employed for charge transport or light emission.
[H10K 85/625] containing at least one aromatic ring having 7 or more carbon atoms, e.g. azulene -- This subclass of the CPC for electric solid-state semiconductor devices concerns organic materials that incorporate at least one aromatic ring of seven or more carbon atoms, for example azulene, used as the body or electrodes.
[H10K 85/626] containing more than one polycyclic condensed aromatic rings, e.g. bis-anthracene -- The code H10K 85/626 covers organic solid-state devices whose active organic material contains two or more fused aromatic rings, such as bis-anthracene, providing enhanced charge transport or optical properties for applications in displays, photovoltaics, or sensors.
[H10K 85/631] Amine compounds having at least two aryl rest on at least one amine-nitrogen atom, e.g. triphenylamine -- The subclass H10K 85/631 concerns organic solid-state devices and defines amine compounds that carry at least two aryl groups attached to a nitrogen atom, such as triphenylamine. These molecules are used as functional materials in the active layers or electrodes of semiconductor devices.
[H10K 85/633] comprising polycyclic condensed aromatic hydrocarbons as substituents on the nitrogen atom -- This subclass concerns organic solid-state semiconductor devices that employ nitrogen atoms substituted with polycyclic condensed aromatic hydrocarbons, a configuration used to modify electrical, optical or stability properties of organic LEDs, solar cells and related components.
[H10K 85/636] comprising heteroaromatic hydrocarbons as substituents on the nitrogen atom -- This subclass pertains to solid-state semiconductor devices that employ organic materials in which a heteroaromatic hydrocarbon group is bonded to a nitrogen atom, serving as part of the active layer or electrode. It is categorized under CPC H10K 85/636 within the broader groups for electricity, semiconductor devices, and organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 85/649] Aromatic compounds comprising a hetero atom -- This subclass includes aromatic compounds that contain at least one hetero atom and are employed as organic materials in the active layer or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices, such as organic LEDs, photovoltaic cells or field-effect transistors. The hetero-atom-substituted aromatics are used to modify electronic properties, energy levels or charge-transport behavior.
[H10K 85/652] Cyanine dyes -- H10K 85/652 pertains to the use of cyanine dyes as organic materials in the active layer or electrodes of solid-state semiconductor devices. The subclass addresses devices in which these dyes serve as charge-transport or light-absorbing components, particularly in organic photovoltaics, sensors and other electronic applications.
[H10K 85/653] comprising only oxygen as heteroatom -- H10K 85/653 covers organic solid-state electric devices whose active material contains oxygen as the sole heteroatom. It applies to semiconducting polymers, small-molecule organics or other carbon-based compounds used in the body or electrodes of the device, without nitrogen, sulfur or other heteroatoms. The subclass falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and organic electric solid-state devices.
[H10K 85/654] comprising only nitrogen as heteroatom  H10K85/652  takes precedence -- H10K85/654 covers organic solid-state semiconductor materials in which nitrogen is the only heteroatom present in the molecular structure, used as the active layer or electrodes of devices in this subclass. The entry is subordinate to H10K85/652, which takes precedence over it.
[H10K 85/655] comprising only sulfur as heteroatom -- The H10K 85/655 subclass of the CPC groups organic solid-state electronic materials whose molecular structure contains sulfur as the sole heteroatom. These materials are employed in the active layers or electrodes of semiconductor devices such as organic transistors, memory elements or photovoltaic cells. The classification excludes compounds with other heteroatoms like nitrogen or oxygen.
[H10K 85/656] comprising two or more different heteroatoms per ring  H10K85/652  takes precedence -- H10K 85/656 relates to organic solid-state semiconductor devices whose active material includes ring structures that contain two or more different heteroatoms. The subclass falls under organic electric solid-state devices within the semiconductor hierarchy. It is superseded by the broader H10K 85/652, which takes precedence in classification.
[H10K 85/6565] Oxadiazole compounds -- H10K 85/6565 covers oxadiazole compounds employed as organic materials in solid-state semiconductor devices. These heterocyclic molecules, featuring an oxadiazole ring, are used in electrodes or active layers of devices such as OLEDs, organic photovoltaics and sensors to provide charge-transport, luminescence or stability. The subclass groups any body-integrated or device-integrated applications of these compounds.
[H10K 85/657] Polycyclic condensed heteroaromatic hydrocarbons -- The subclass H10K 85/657 includes organic solid-state devices that employ polycyclic condensed heteroaromatic hydrocarbons as the body material or electrode component. These fused-ring compounds contain heteroatoms (e.g., N, O, S) within the aromatic system and are used to provide conductive or semiconducting functions in devices such as organic LEDs, solar cells, and transistors.
[H10K 85/6572] comprising only nitrogen in the heteroaromatic polycondensed ring system, e.g. phenanthroline or carbazole -- The entry H10K 85/6572 designates organic solid-state devices whose heteroaromatic polycondensed ring system contains only nitrogen atoms, such as phenanthroline or carbazole, used as the active material or electrode. It belongs to the subclass for organic materials employed in the body or electrodes of semiconductor devices.
[H10K 85/6574] comprising only oxygen in the heteroaromatic polycondensed ring system, e.g. cumarine dyes -- The subclass H10K 85/6574 covers organic solid-state semiconductor devices whose active material includes heteroaromatic polycondensed rings that contain only oxygen as the heteroatom, exemplified by coumarin-type dyes used in the body or electrodes of the device.
[H10K 85/6576] comprising only sulfur in the heteroaromatic polycondensed ring system, e.g. benzothiophene -- The CPC subclass H10K 85/6576 covers organic solid-state semiconductor devices in which the heteroaromatic polycondensed ring system contains only sulfur, such as benzothiophene, used as the material of the device body or electrodes.
[H10K 85/658] Organoboranes -- Organoboranes are organic boron-containing compounds employed as active or auxiliary materials in organic solid-state devices. They function as charge-transport layers, dopants or emitters in organic LEDs, photovoltaics and transistors, improving carrier mobility and device stability. The CPC subclass H10K 85/658 covers these materials when used in the body or electrodes of such devices.
[H10K 85/701] Langmuir Blodgett films -- H10K 85/701 covers Langmuir Blodgett films, which are molecularly thin organic layers transferred onto a substrate to form the active material or electrode of solid-state semiconductor devices. The class pertains to organic electric solid-state devices that employ such films as the functional medium within the device architecture.
[H10K 85/731] Liquid crystalline materials -- Liquid crystalline materials used as active or supporting layers in organic solid-state electronic devices, such as organic light-emitting diodes, photovoltaic cells, transistors, or sensors, are covered by CPC subclass H10K 85/731. The entry encompasses compounds and mixtures that form liquid-crystal phases and contribute to charge transport, light emission, or electro-optical modulation within these semiconductor structures.
[H10K 85/761] Biomolecules or bio-macromolecules, e.g. proteins, chlorophyl, lipids or enzymes -- The H10K 85/761 subclass covers the use of biomolecules or bio-macromolecules-such as proteins, chlorophyll, lipids or enzymes-as functional components in organic solid-state semiconductor devices, including body-integrated electrodes. It addresses how these natural substances are employed to create or improve electronic properties in the device structure.
[H10K 85/791] Starburst compounds -- Starburst compounds are branched organic molecules employed as active or electrode materials in solid-state semiconductor devices such as organic LEDs, photovoltaic cells and transistors. Their multi-arm architecture provides high charge-carrier mobility and tunable optoelectronic properties, making them suitable for the body or electrodes of devices covered by subclass H10K.
[H10K 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- H10K 99/00 is a residual subgroup within the CPC subclass for organic electric solid-state devices, covering inventions that pertain to semiconductor devices but do not fit any of the specific groups defined in that subclass. It serves as a catch-all for miscellaneous organic-material technologies not otherwise classified.
[H10N] ELECTRIC SOLID-STATE DEVICES NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Discrete and integrated solid-state devices not otherwise provided for in the IPC and details and fabrication thereof.  Assemblies of multiple devices comprising at least one solid-state device covered by this subclass not otherwise provided for in the IPC.  This includes the following kinds of devices:  **Glossary:** - chip: a piece of a wafer or a substrate that has been processed to contain devices therein or thereon. The expression "diced chip" refers to the result of dicing a wafer or a substrate into a plurality of chips, whereas "undiced chip" refers to a chip before dicing or with no dicing. - device: an electric circuit element (e.g. diode, transistor, LED, etc.); (depending on the context) can also refer to an integrated device (e.g. CMOS-IC, DRAM device, etc.). A device may be in the form of a bare or packaged chip. - dopant: the atoms or compounds added to a material during doping - doping: the intentional addition of a small quantity of atoms or compounds into a material to achieve a desired characteristic, e.g. to produce an n-type or p-type material - individual: refers to: an electric circuit element not being an integrated device; or a component of an integrated device. Examples of individual devices include: diodes, transistors, photovoltaic cells, Josephson-junction devices, light-emitting diodes [LED], organic LEDs or a single LED component within an integrated device. - integrated device: a device consisting of a plurality of semiconductor or other solid-state electric circuit elements formed in or on a common substrate - integrated circuit: an integrated device where all the electric circuit elements (e.g. diodes, transistors, LEDs, etc.) are formed in or on a common substrate, including interconnections between the elements - component: an electric circuit element (e.g. diode, transistor, LED, etc.) that is one of a plurality of elements formed in or on a common substrate, e.g. in an integrated device - wafer: it can be one of the following: (a) a slice of semiconductor or electric solid-state active material. For example: a slice of silicon; a slice of a semiconducting compound, e.g. gallium nitride [GaN]; a slice of lithium tantalate [LiTaO 3 ] for superconductor applications. (b) A multilayered laminate, having at least one layer of semiconductor or electric solid-state active material, the layer being meant to be processed into devices. For example: silicon-on-insulator [SOI]; silicon-on-glass [SOG]; silicon-on-sapphire [SOS]; a composite wafer comprising silicon carbide [SiC] on polycrystalline silicon [Si] support; a layer of semiconducting nanowires on glass. A wafer is typically processed by (e.g.) deposition, etching, doping or diffusion, and is then typically diced into chips. - body: the region of semiconductor (resp. solid-state) material(s) within which, or at the surface of which, the physical effects that are characteristic of the device occur, and any bordering semiconductor (resp. solid-state) material(s) that are contiguous with this region. Examples: in a field-effect transistor [FET], the physical effects occur in the channel region between the source and the drain. The semiconductor body includes the channel region, the source and drain regions, and any contiguous semiconductor material; in a light-emitting diode [LED], the physical effects occur at a junction of active semiconductor layers. The semiconductor body includes these active semiconductor layers and any contiguous semiconductor layers, such as buffer layers, possibly a growth substrate, etc., that are between the cathode and anode electrodes; in a thermoelectric device, the solid-state body includes all solid-state materials in the path of current between the electrodes. - electrode: a conductive region in or on the semiconductor body or solid-state body of a device (and other than the body itself) which exerts an electrical influence on the body, irrespective of whether or not an external electrical connection is made thereto. The term covers metallic regions which exert electrical influence on the body through an insulating region (e.g. in intentional non-parasitic capacitive coupling), or inductive coupling arrangements. In a capacitive coupling arrangement, the dielectric region is regarded as part of the electrode. The overall conductive wiring may comprise multiple portions. In such a case, only the wiring portions that exert an electrical influence on the body are considered portions of the electrode. Examples: conductive layer(s) in direct physical contact with the body; conductive region(s) exerting an inductive coupling onto the body; a multilayer structure which exerts influence on the body through an insulating region, e.g. in intentional non-parasitic capacitive coupling. - interconnection: a conductive arrangement for conducting electric current from an electrode of a circuit element to another part of the circuit. Examples include metal wirings. - container: a solid construction in which (one or more) devices are placed, or which is formed around the devices, for forming packaged devices. A container requires a partial or total enclosure and it may also comprise a filling. - encapsulation: an enclosure consisting of (one or more) layers, e.g. comprising organic polymers, which at least partially enclose the (one or more) devices, thereby protecting them. An encapsulation is often used to hermetically seal devices. - field-effect: refers to semiconductor technology wherein a voltage applied to a gate electrode creates an electric field that allows for control of current near the interface of the gate and the body, e.g. to create an inversion channel between the source and drain of a MOSFET - package: the collection of all elements, which are external to the chip, that protect the chip or connect it to another object. Package therefore covers encapsulations, containers, package substrates, interposers, heatsinks or the like. Package does not include objects at a higher system level, like circuit boards and beyond, e.g. a housing in which the circuit board is enclosed. - unipolar: refers to semiconductor technology that primarily involves one type only of charge carrier, i.e. it involves either holes or electrons but not both - bipolar: refers to semiconductor technology that involves multi-carrier-type operation, i.e. which simultaneously uses both electrons and holes as charge carriers - MIS: metal-insulator-semiconductor - MOS: metal-oxide-semiconductor - FET: field-effect transistor - MISFET: metal-insulator-semiconductor field-effect transistor - TFT: thin-film transistor - active material: material within which the physical effects that are characteristic of the device occur
[H10N 10/00] Thermoelectric or thermomagnetic devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Thermoelectric devices being based on:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Integrated devices or assemblies comprising thermoelectric or thermomagnetic elements -> H10N19/00  **Glossary:** - thermocouple: temperature measuring device consisting of two conductors of different materials joined at the end - thermopile: a thermopile is an electronic device that converts thermal energy into electrical energy. It is composed of several thermocouples connected usually in series or less commonly in parallel.
[H10N 10/01] Manufacture or treatment -- H10N 10/01 covers processes for manufacturing or treating thermoelectric and thermomagnetic solid-state devices, a subgroup of electric solid-state devices in the semiconductor devices branch of the CPC electricity section. It includes methods such as material deposition, doping, annealing, assembly and surface modification aimed at creating or improving devices that convert heat to electricity or vice-versa.
[H10N 10/10] operating with only the Peltier or Seebeck effects -- The H10N 10/10 subclass covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic devices that generate or pump heat solely by the Peltier effect (direct cooling when current passes) or the Seebeck effect (voltage produced by a temperature gradient). Such devices exclude any additional mechanisms, focusing exclusively on these reversible thermoelectric phenomena for power generation or temperature control.
[H10N 10/13] characterised by the heat-exchanging means at the junction -- The subclass H10N 10/13 includes thermoelectric or thermomagnetic devices whose heat-exchanging means are positioned at the junction of the active elements, allowing direct heat transfer across the junction for power generation or cooling.
[H10N 10/17] characterised by the structure or configuration of the cell or thermocouple forming the device -- The H10N 10/17 subclass covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic devices where the defining feature is the particular structure or arrangement of the cell or thermocouple that makes up the device, distinguishing it from other thermoelectric configurations. This classification falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, and electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 10/80] Constructional details -- H10N 10/80 covers the constructional details of thermoelectric or thermomagnetic devices, including their mechanical design, encapsulation, mounting methods, heat-sink arrangements, electrical contacts and packaging configurations that enable conversion between heat gradients and electrical energy.
[H10N 10/81] Structural details of the junction -- The H10N 10/81 subclass covers the structural characteristics of the junctions within thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices, such as the geometry, materials, and layer configurations that form the heat-to-electricity conversion region. It focuses on how the junction is built rather than its operation or external connections.
[H10N 10/813] the junction being separable, e.g. using a spring -- H10N 10/813 covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic devices where the junction between the p-type and n-type elements is made separable, for example by using a spring that allows the junctions to be detached for replacement or adjustment.
[H10N 10/817] the junction being non-separable, e.g. being cemented, sintered or soldered -- H10N 10/817 covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic devices whose constituent materials are joined by a permanent, non-separable interface such as cementing, sintering or soldering, rather than a removable connection. This classification is used for solid-state devices where the junction is integral to the structure, affecting heat-to-electric conversion performance.
[H10N 10/82] Interconnections -- The H10N 10/82 class covers the design and arrangement of electrical interconnections in thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices, including contacts, lead frames, bonding methods and other conductive paths that join the active semiconductor elements to external circuitry or packaging. It addresses how these connections are formed, secured and integrated to ensure efficient heat-to-electricity conversion and reliable operation.
[H10N 10/85] Thermoelectric active materials -- H10N 10/85 defines thermoelectric active materials, the semiconductor compounds, alloys, doped oxides and nanostructured substances that generate electric voltage from a temperature gradient or produce cooling when current passes. The entry covers materials engineered for high Seebeck coefficient, low thermal conductivity and suitable electrical conductivity, used in solid-state generators, Peltier coolers and temperature-sensor elements.
[H10N 10/851] comprising inorganic compositions -- It covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices that employ inorganic material compositions as active elements or substrates, focusing on inorganic compounds used to convert heat to electricity or vice versa.
[H10N 10/852] comprising tellurium, selenium or sulfur -- H10N 10/852 covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices that incorporate tellurium, selenium or sulfur as active materials. It sits in the CPC hierarchy under electricity, semiconductor devices, electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for. The code is used by the EPO and USPTO to classify patents relating to these specific thermoelectric materials.
[H10N 10/853] comprising arsenic, antimony or bismuth -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Thermoelectric active materials comprising tellurium, selenium or sulfur -> H10N10/852
[H10N 10/854] comprising only metals -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Thermoelectric active materials comprising tellurium, selenium or sulfur -> H10N10/852 - Thermoelectric active materials comprising arsenic, antimony or bismuth -> H10N10/853
[H10N 10/855] comprising compounds containing boron, carbon, oxygen or nitrogen -- A thermoelectric or thermomagnetic device that incorporates solid-state components using compounds of boron, carbon, oxygen or nitrogen is classified under CPC H10N 10/855. This entry falls within electricity > semiconductor devices > electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for, and covers devices whose operation relies on the thermoelectric or thermomagnetic properties of such compounds.
[H10N 10/8552] the compounds being superconducting -- This subclass concerns compounds that exhibit superconductivity, i.e., zero electrical resistance and expulsion of magnetic fields, when used in thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices. It covers the composition, preparation, and properties of such superconducting materials for integration in semiconductor or energy-conversion applications.
[H10N 10/8556] comprising compounds containing germanium or silicon -- The CPC subclass H10N 10/8556 covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices that incorporate compounds made from germanium or silicon, such as semiconductor thermocouples and related heat-to-electricity converters. It falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 10/856] comprising organic compositions -- H10N 10/856 covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices that contain or use organic compositions, such as polymeric or molecular semiconductors, as active or supporting materials for converting heat to electricity or the reverse.
[H10N 10/857] comprising compositions changing continuously or discontinuously inside the material -- This subclass covers thermoelectric or thermomagnetic solid-state devices that contain material systems whose composition varies either gradually or abruptly within the component during operation or manufacturing, thereby affecting the device's properties. The change may result from diffusion, phase transition, segregation, or other processes that alter the material's stoichiometry or structure. It applies to devices in which such internal compositional modulation is an essential part of their functional performance.
[H10N 15/00] Thermoelectric devices without a junction of dissimilar materials -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices directly converting thermal energy to electrical or magnetic quantities, or vice versa, using thermoelectric or thermomagnetic effects of solid-state bulk materials. This main group also covers the related active materials and fabrication of such devices.  These include devices based on:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Integrated devices or assemblies comprising thermoelectric or thermomagnetic elements -> H10N19/00
[H10N 15/10] Thermoelectric devices using thermal change of the dielectric constant, e.g. working above and below the Curie point -- H10N 15/10 covers solid-state thermoelectric converters that generate electricity by exploiting the temperature-dependent change in a material's dielectric constant, such as devices operating around the Curie temperature. These are classified under electricity, semiconductor devices, electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for, thermoelectric devices without a junction of dissimilar materials.
[H10N 15/15] Thermoelectric active materials -- The H10N 15/15 subclass covers solid-state thermoelectric active materials used in devices that convert heat to electricity or vice-versa without relying on a junction of dissimilar materials. It includes bulk thermoelectric compounds, alloys and doped semiconductors that generate a temperature-driven voltage in a homogeneous structure.
[H10N 15/20] Thermomagnetic devices using thermal change of the magnetic permeability, e.g. working above and below the Curie point -- The subclass H10N 15/20 covers thermomagnetic devices that exploit the temperature-dependent change in magnetic permeability, typically operating by moving across the Curie temperature to switch magnetic properties for energy conversion or control. These devices convert heat variations into magnetic state changes for functions such as power generation or temperature sensing.
[H10N 19/00] Integrated devices, or assemblies of multiple devices, comprising at least one thermoelectric or thermomagnetic element covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Integrated devices comprising thermoelectric and/or thermomagnetic components individually covered by groups H10N10/00 or H10N15/00 , e.g. integrating Seebeck, Peltier, or pyroelectric components either with components of the same kind, (e.g. thermocouple arrays), or with components of a different kind (e.g. semiconductor diodes, transistors).  Assemblies of multiple devices comprising thermoelectric and/or thermomagnetic devices individually covered by groups H10N10/00 or H10N15/00 that are not provided for elsewhere in the IPC.  **Glossary:** - thermocouple: temperature measuring device consisting of two conductors of different materials joined at the end - thermopile: a thermopile is an electronic device that converts thermal energy into electrical energy. It is composed of several thermocouples connected usually in series or less commonly in parallel.
[H10N 19/101] Multiple thermocouples connected in a cascade arrangement -- H10N 19/101 pertains to integrated solid-state devices that incorporate several thermocouples linked in a cascade, so the output of one thermocouple drives the next to boost voltage or temperature difference in thermoelectric or thermomagnetic applications.
[H10N 30/00] Piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Piezoelectric or electrostrictive (PE) devices based on:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Integrated devices or assemblies comprising PE and/or MS elements -> H10N39/00  **Glossary:** - stacked or multilayer(ed) structure: PE parts, e.g. PE layers, and electrodes alternating in one, i.e. stacking direction
[H10N 30/01] Manufacture or treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for manufacturing a material, product or device which exhibits or changes an electrostatic polarisation when subjected to mechanical stress or which exhibits a mechanical deformation, e.g. tending to produce a deflection, when subjected to electric stress.
[H10N 30/02] Forming enclosures or casings -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for forming enclosures or casings with encapsulants.  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this place:
[H10N 30/03] Assembling devices that include piezoelectric or electrostrictive parts -- This classification covers assembling devices that incorporate pieelectric or electrostrictive components, such as sensors, actuators and transducers, detailing methods of mounting, bonding, and packaging for integration into larger electronic systems.
[H10N 30/04] Treatments to modify a piezoelectric or electrostrictive property, e.g. polarisation characteristics, vibration characteristics or mode tuning -- H10N 30/04 covers methods for altering the functional characteristics of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices, such as adjusting polarization, vibration behavior or tuning resonant modes. It includes processes like poling, annealing, stress application or electrical conditioning to improve or change device performance.
[H10N 30/045] by polarising -- H10N 30/045 covers solid-state devices that use electrical polarisation to produce or detect mechanical strain, including piezoelectric, electrostrictive and magnetostrictive components where the effect is achieved by polarising the material.
[H10N 30/05] Manufacture of multilayered piezoelectric or electrostrictive devices, or parts thereof, e.g. by stacking piezoelectric bodies and electrodes -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for manufacturing devices comprising multiple piezoelectric or electrostrictive [PE] parts that alternate with electrodes in a stacking direction, wherein the PE parts may comprise, for example, single PE layers, multilayered PE stacks or bulk PE bodies.
[H10N 30/053] by integrally sintering piezoelectric or electrostrictive bodies and electrodes -- H10N 30/053 covers solid-state devices that are made by integrally sintering piezoelectric or electrostrictive bodies together with their electrodes, forming a monolithic structure. This method combines the active material and electrode in a single sintering step, improving mechanical stability and reducing manufacturing complexity.
[H10N 30/057] by stacking bulk piezoelectric or electrostrictive bodies and electrodes -- The H10N 30/057 subclass covers solid-state devices in which multiple bulk piezoelectric or electrostrictive bodies are stacked together with interleaved electrodes, forming layered structures that generate or respond to electrical signals under mechanical stress. This arrangement is used to enhance output, sensitivity, or control in piezoelectric and electrostrictive components.
[H10N 30/06] Forming electrodes or interconnections, e.g. leads or terminals -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Generic processes or apparatus, for forming electrodes, leads or terminal arrangements for piezoelectric or electrostrictive devices or parts thereof.  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this place:
[H10N 30/063] Forming interconnections, e.g. connection electrodes of multilayered piezoelectric or electrostrictive parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for manufacturing connection electrodes of multilayered piezoelectric or electrostrictive [PE] parts, including lead-in or terminal arrangements.
[H10N 30/067] Forming single-layered electrodes of multilayered piezoelectric or electrostrictive parts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for manufacturing internal electrodes of multilayered piezoelectric or electrostrictive [PE] parts.
[H10N 30/07] Forming of piezoelectric or electrostrictive parts or bodies on an electrical element or another base -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for applying piezoelectric or electrostrictive [PE] parts or bodies onto an electrical element or another base.
