[H10N 30/071] Mounting of piezoelectric or electrostrictive parts together with semiconductor elements, or other circuit elements, on a common substrate -- H10N 30/071 concerns methods and structures for mounting piezoelectric or electrostrictive parts together with semiconductor or other circuit elements on a common substrate, enabling integrated devices that combine sensing, actuation, or energy-conversion functions with electronic circuitry.
[H10N 30/072] by laminating or bonding of piezoelectric or electrostrictive bodies -- H10N 30/072 pertains to solid-state piezoelectric or electrostrictive devices that are formed by laminating or bonding the active bodies together. The classification includes techniques for joining layers of piezoelectric or electrostrictive materials to create composite structures used in sensors, actuators, or energy-harvesting components.
[H10N 30/073] by fusion of metals or by adhesives -- H10N 30/073 covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that are assembled by fusing metal components together or by using adhesive bonding. The code pertains to solid-state electric devices where the active element is joined through metallurgical or adhesive processes rather than mechanical mounting. It falls under the hierarchy ELECTRICITY - SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES - ELECTRIC SOLID-STATE DEVICES NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR.
[H10N 30/074] by depositing piezoelectric or electrostrictive layers, e.g. aerosol or screen printing -- H10N 30/074 covers the manufacturing of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices by depositing functional layers onto a substrate, such as using aerosol-based or screen-printing techniques. The classification falls under the CPC section for electric solid-state devices, specifically in the subclass for semiconductor devices employing piezoelectric or electrostrictive materials. It defines layer-deposition processes as a means to create these active devices.
[H10N 30/076] by vapour phase deposition -- H10N 30/076 covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that are fabricated by vapor-phase deposition techniques such as chemical or physical vapor deposition. It falls under the electricity subclass for semiconductor devices and the category of electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 30/077] by liquid phase deposition -- H10N 30/077 covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices that are formed using liquid-phase deposition techniques, such as sol-gel or chemical bath processes, to create the functional thin-film or bulk material.
[H10N 30/078] by sol-gel deposition -- H10N 30/078 pertains to solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive components that are produced by sol-gel deposition. The code covers the formation of functional thin-film layers using sol-gel processes for devices such as sensors, actuators or transducers. It is part of the hierarchy electricity - semiconductor devices - electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 30/079] using intermediate layers, e.g. for growth control -- H10N 30/079 relates to piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices and specifically covers the use of intermediate layers, such as buffer or seed layers, to control crystal growth and material properties during fabrication.
[H10N 30/08] Shaping or machining of piezoelectric or electrostrictive bodies -- H10N 30/08 covers processes for shaping, cutting, grinding, or otherwise machining bodies made of piezoelectric or electrostrictive materials. It includes techniques to form these functional ceramics or crystals for use in sensors, actuators, and other devices.
[H10N 30/081] by coating or depositing using masks, e.g. lift-off -- H10N 30/081 covers manufacturing of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices by applying coatings or deposited layers through mask techniques such as lift-off. The process uses patterned masks to define where material is added or removed, enabling precise formation of functional thin-film structures.
[H10N 30/082] by etching, e.g. lithography -- H10N 30/082 pertains to the manufacturing of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices by means of etching techniques. Methods such as lithographic patterning are specifically covered.
[H10N 30/084] by moulding or extrusion -- H10N 30/084 covers the manufacture of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices using moulding or extrusion techniques. It includes processes where the active material is shaped by casting, injection moulding, extrusion or similar forming methods to create the functional element of the device.
[H10N 30/085] by machining -- H10N 30/085 - by machining covers the production of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices in which the active material is shaped using machining operations such as grinding, turning, milling or drilling, rather than forming or deposition techniques.
[H10N 30/086] by polishing or grinding -- This subgroup covers methods of finishing or shaping piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices by polishing or grinding, including surface planarisation, material removal and related steps to achieve required dimensions or surface quality.
[H10N 30/088] by cutting or dicing -- The H10N 30/088 subclass covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices that are formed by cutting or dicing the functional material into the desired shape or geometry, rather than by deposition or molding. This method of shaping the active element is used to create specific mechanical or electrical properties in the finished component.
[H10N 30/089] by punching -- H10N 30/089 covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive components that are fabricated by punching, meaning the active material is mechanically separated or shaped with a punch to form the final device.
[H10N 30/09] Forming piezoelectric or electrostrictive materials -- H10N 30/09 covers methods for producing piezoelectric or electrostrictive materials, such as crystal growth, deposition, sintering, annealing and other techniques that create functional solid-state components. The classification includes preparation of bulk compounds, thin films and composites used in sensors, actuators and transducers.
[H10N 30/092] Forming composite materials -- H10N 30/092 relates to the formation of composite materials that integrate piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state components. It covers processes for preparing, shaping or assembling such composites for use in electronic or electromechanical devices.
[H10N 30/093] Forming inorganic materials -- H10N 30/093 covers methods for producing inorganic piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive materials used in solid-state devices. It includes processes such as crystal growth, sintering, thin-film deposition and other techniques that form the active inorganic layers of these devices.
[H10N 30/095] by melting -- The CPC subclass H10N 30/095 covers methods for producing piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices by melting the functional material. It includes manufacturing steps in which the active ceramic or crystal is melted, shaped, and then solidified to form the device structure. This entry falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, and electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 30/097] by sintering -- The CPC subclass H10N 30/097 covers solid-state devices that exploit piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive effects and are produced by sintering, i.e., the high-temperature consolidation of powdered material into a dense, functional component. This applies to various semiconductor-type actuators, sensors and transducers fabricated via that powder-processing technique.
[H10N 30/098] Forming organic materials -- The code H10N 30/098 covers techniques for forming organic materials that are incorporated into piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices, including processes such as deposition, patterning or synthesis of organic layers used in these semiconductor components.
[H10N 30/101] with electrical and mechanical input and output, e.g. having combined actuator and sensor parts -- The H10N 30/101 subclass covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that accept both electrical and mechanical signals and produce both types of output, typically integrating actuator and sensor functions in a single component. This includes devices such as combined piezoelectric actuators with sensing capability.
[H10N 30/20] with electrical input and mechanical output, e.g. functioning as actuators or vibrators -- H10N 30/20 pertains to piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that convert an electrical input into a mechanical output, such as actuators and vibrators.
[H10N 30/202] using longitudinal or thickness displacement combined with bending, shear or torsion displacement -- This subclass covers solid-state devices - typically piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive - in which a longitudinal or thickness strain is combined with bending, shear or torsional deformation to produce or sense a coupled mechanical response. Such combined-mode operation is used in advanced actuators, sensors and transducers that require multi-axis motion or enhanced coupling.
[H10N 30/2023] having polygonal or rectangular shape -- H10N 30/2023 covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that are formed with a polygonal or rectangular shape. It is classified under electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for, within the broader semiconductor devices and electricity categories.
[H10N 30/2027] having cylindrical or annular shape -- This subclass includes piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that are manufactured in a cylindrical or annular form, such as rods, tubes or rings, used as solid-state transducers, actuators or sensors.
[H10N 30/204] using bending displacement, e.g. unimorph, bimorph or multimorph cantilever or membrane benders -- This CPC subclass pertains to piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices that operate by bending-displacement mechanisms such as unimorph, bimorph or multimorph cantilevers or membrane benders, converting mechanical strain into electrical signals or vice-versa. It covers designs that use these flexural structures to achieve actuation or sensing functions within the broader H10N 30/20 family.
[H10N 30/2041] Beam type -- H10N 30/2041 - Beam type - refers to solid-state devices such as piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive components that employ a beam-shaped element as the active transducer. The classification covers the structure, mounting and operation of beam configurations used to generate or sense mechanical vibrations from electrical signals.
[H10N 30/2042] Cantilevers, i.e. having one fixed end -- H10N 30/2042 covers cantilever structures that have one end fixed while the other end is free to move, used in solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices. The classification applies to semiconductor-based components where the cantilever serves as a transducer, actuator or sensor element.
[H10N 30/2043] connected at their free ends, e.g. parallelogram type -- H10N 30/2043 refers to solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices in which the individual active elements are joined together at their free ends, for example in a parallelogram configuration. Such connections allow the combined structure to produce or sense mechanical deformation or magnetic response while maintaining electrical continuity.
[H10N 30/2044] having multiple segments mechanically connected in series, e.g. zig-zag type -- The entry covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices in which several active segments are mechanically joined in series, for example in a zig-zag stack, to increase displacement or voltage output while distributing stress across the series-connected elements.
[H10N 30/2045] adapted for in-plane bending displacement -- A device that uses piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive effects and is specifically configured to generate or detect bending motion within the plane of the element, rather than out-of-plane displacement. Such in-plane bending adaptations are employed in actuators, sensors or transducers where lateral flexure is required for precise control or measurement.
[H10N 30/2046] adapted for multi-directional bending displacement -- The H10N 30/2046 subclass covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive components that are specifically designed to produce or respond to bending movements in more than one direction. These devices are adapted for multi-directional displacement, enabling complex actuation or sensing functions in applications such as adaptive optics, micro-robotics or multi-axis vibration control.
[H10N 30/2047] Membrane type -- This CPC subclass (H10N 30/2047) pertains to membrane-type electric solid-state devices that exploit piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive effects. Such devices are thin-film or layered structures used as transducers, sensors or actuators, and are classified under semiconductor devices within the broader electricity field.
[H10N 30/2048] having non-planar shape -- H10N 30/2048 covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices that are configured in a non-planar shape. The class falls under electricity, semiconductor devices and electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 30/206] using only longitudinal or thickness displacement, e.g. d 33  or d 31  type devices -- This CPC subclass covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices that generate or sense signals by using only longitudinal or thickness displacement, such as d33 or d31 configurations. It applies to devices where the movement is parallel to the polarization axis or normal to the plane, without shear or other deformation modes.
[H10N 30/208] using shear or torsion displacement, e.g. d 15  type devices -- The H10N 30/208 subclass covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices that generate or sense motion by applying shear or torsional displacement, such as the d15 mode where the strain occurs parallel to the applied electric field. It is used for devices that convert shear strain into electrical signals or vice-versa.
[H10N 30/30] with mechanical input and electrical output, e.g. functioning as generators or sensors -- The H10N 30/30 subclass covers solid-state devices that generate an electrical output from a mechanical input, typically using piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive effects. It includes generators, sensors and similar transducers that convert mechanical stress into electrical signals.
[H10N 30/302] Sensors -- The H10N 30/302 subclass covers sensors that exploit piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive effects, such as pressure, acceleration, ultrasonic or magnetic-field detectors, within electric solid-state devices and semiconductor technology. It includes devices that convert mechanical or magnetic changes into electrical signals.
[H10N 30/304] Beam type -- The "Beam type" subclass (H10N 30/304) covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices in which a beam-like element is used as the active transducer. Typical examples include cantilever or doubly-clamped beams that convert electrical signals into mechanical vibrations or vice versa for sensors, actuators, resonators and frequency-filter applications.
[H10N 30/306] Cantilevers -- Cantilevers are beam-style solid-state components that deflect when subjected to electric, electrostrictive, piezoelectric or magnetostrictive stimuli, enabling precise displacement in micro-electromechanical sensors, actuators and resonators. The classification covers their design, materials and performance characteristics within semiconductor and solid-state device technology.
[H10N 30/308] Membrane type -- In the Cooperative Patent Classification, H10N 30/308 designates membrane-type devices within the subclass of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices. It covers structures where a thin membrane or diaphragm serves as the active element that deforms under an electric or magnetic field to produce or sense mechanical motion. These are used in sensors, actuators, and energy-harvesting components.
[H10N 30/40] with electrical input and electrical output, e.g. functioning as transformers -- H10N 30/40 classifies solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that receive an electrical input and deliver an electrical output, essentially operating as transformers that convert electrical energy through strain-based mechanisms.
[H10N 30/50] having a stacked or multilayer structure -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices comprising multiple piezoelectric or electrostrictive [PE] parts that alternate with electrodes in a stacking direction, wherein the PE parts may comprise, for example, single PE layers, multilayered PE stacks or bulk PE bodies.
[H10N 30/501] having a non-rectangular cross-section in a plane parallel to the stacking direction, e.g. polygonal or trapezoidal in side view -- H10N 30/501 classifies solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices whose cross-section, viewed in a plane parallel to the stacking direction, is not rectangular but polygonal, trapezoidal or otherwise irregular. This subclass refines the geometry of the active layer for such devices.
[H10N 30/503] having a non-rectangular cross-section in a plane orthogonal to the stacking direction, e.g. polygonal or circular in top view -- H10N 30/503 classifies piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices whose cross-section, taken in a plane perpendicular to the stacking direction, is non-rectangular, such as polygonal or circular shapes when viewed from above. This sub-category refines the broader group of electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 30/505] the cross-section being annular -- This CPC subcategory covers annular cross-section designs for piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices such as resonators, sensors or actuators where the active material forms a ring-shaped cross-section. It is part of the broader group for electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for.
[H10N 30/506] having a cylindrical shape and having stacking in the radial direction, e.g. coaxial or spiral type rolls -- This subclass concerns solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that are formed as cylinders in which the active layers are stacked radially, for example in coaxial or spiral roll configurations. It addresses the geometry and stacking orientation rather than the specific material composition.
[H10N 30/508] adapted for alleviating internal stress, e.g. cracking control layers -- This subgroup covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive components that incorporate structures or layers specifically designed to relieve internal mechanical stress, for example cracking-control coatings or stress-relief layers. These adaptations aim to improve reliability and performance of the device by mitigating stress-induced damage.
[H10N 30/60] having a coaxial cable structure -- This subcategory covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that incorporate a coaxial cable geometry, where an inner conductor, a dielectric layer and an outer conductive sheath are arranged concentrically to transmit signals or power. The structure provides electromagnetic shielding and controlled impedance, improving signal integrity and allowing compact integration in devices.
[H10N 30/702] based on piezoelectric or electrostrictive fibres -- H10N 30/702 concerns solid-state devices that employ piezoelectric or electrostrictive fibres, such as fibre-based transducers, sensors, actuators or energy-harvesting elements, where the active material is in the form of individual or bundled fibres, often incorporated into composites or woven structures.
[H10N 30/704] based on piezoelectric or electrostrictive films or coatings -- H10N 30/704 covers solid-state devices that employ thin piezoelectric or electrostrictive films or coatings as the functional element. These may include sensors, actuators, transducers or other components where the film's deformation under an electric field generates mechanical motion or vice-versa. The classification is applied to devices in which the active material is a deposited film rather than a bulk crystal.
[H10N 30/706] characterised by the underlying bases, e.g. substrates -- H10N 30/706 covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that are defined by the material or structure of the underlying base, such as a specific substrate. The classification applies to devices where the substrate-crystal, ceramic, composite or similar-determines the functional characteristics of the element.
[H10N 30/708] Intermediate layers, e.g. barrier, adhesion or growth control buffer layers -- H10N 30/708 covers the use of intermediate layers-such as barrier, adhesion or growth-control buffer layers-within piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices, focusing on their function to protect, bind or manage material growth between active components.
[H10N 30/80] Constructional details -- H10N 30/80 relates to the structural and assembly aspects of piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices, covering features such as housing, mounting, sealing, interconnections, and other mechanical or material details that define the device's physical construction. This subgroup refines broader categories of solid-state electric devices by focusing on how the components are integrated and protected within the overall device architecture.
[H10N 30/802] Circuitry or processes for operating piezoelectric or electrostrictive devices not otherwise provided for, e.g. drive circuits -- H10N 30/802 covers circuitry and control methods specifically designed to operate piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices when no other provision applies. It includes drive circuits, signal-conditioning networks and related process steps used to excite, bias or modulate such devices.
[H10N 30/804] for piezoelectric transformers -- H10N 30/804 is a CPC subgroup within the electricity class that deals with solid-state semiconductor devices, specifically piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive components. It is limited to piezoelectric transformers, i.e., devices that convert voltage or frequency using the piezoelectric effect.
[H10N 30/85] Piezoelectric or electrostrictive active materials -- H10N 30/85 concerns materials that exhibit piezoelectric or electrostrictive activity, i.e., substances that generate electric charge under mechanical stress or deform in response to an electric field. The subclass includes ceramics, polymers, composites and other solid-state media used in devices that convert mechanical and electrical energy.
[H10N 30/852] Composite materials, e.g. having 1-3 or 2-2 type connectivity -- H10N 30/852 covers composite materials employed in piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices, specifically those with defined connectivity patterns such as 1-3 or 2-2, where active and passive phases are arranged in a regular lattice to tailor mechanical and electrical response.
[H10N 30/853] Ceramic compositions -- H10N 30/853 covers ceramic compositions used in solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices. The classification falls under electricity > semiconductor devices > electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for > piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices. It addresses the material makeup of ceramics that provide the functional properties for these transducer applications.
[H10N 30/8536] Alkaline earth metal based oxides, e.g. barium titanates -- H10N 30/8536 refers to solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that employ alkaline-earth-metal oxides such as barium titanate. These materials are used in sensors, actuators and frequency-control components because of their strong electromechanical coupling.
[H10N 30/8542] Alkali metal based oxides, e.g. lithium, sodium or potassium niobates -- H10N 30/8542 classifies solid-state devices that use alkali-metal-based oxide compounds such as lithium, sodium or potassium niobates as piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive materials. These materials are employed in semiconductor devices that convert electrical energy to mechanical deformation or detect mechanical stress through an electric response.
[H10N 30/8548] Lead-based oxides -- The H10N 30/8548 subclass comprises lead-based oxide compounds employed as functional materials in piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices. These oxides are used for their high dielectric constant and electromechanical coupling, enabling actuation, sensing or energy-conversion functions in semiconductor and electronic components.
[H10N 30/8554] Lead-zirconium titanate [PZT] based -- H10N 30/8554 covers solid-state devices that use lead-zirconium titanate (PZT) as a piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive material. It pertains to semiconductor components that convert electrical energy to mechanical motion or vice-versa using PZT's ferroelectric properties. The entry falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, electric solid-state devices not otherwise provided for, and specifically piezoelectric or related devices.
[H10N 30/8561] Bismuth-based oxides -- H10N 30/8561 covers bismuth-based oxide materials used in piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices. [H10N 30/857] Macromolecular compositions -- H10N 30/857 covers macromolecular (polymeric) compositions employed in piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices, including polymer blends, copolymers, composites and doped polymers that exhibit these properties, as well as methods of preparing and configuring such materials for use in the devices.
[H10N 30/87] Electrodes or interconnections, e.g. leads or terminals -- The H10N 30/87 code refers to electrodes or interconnections in piezoelectric devices. This includes leads or terminals - components that facilitate connections. It is a subset of electric solid-state devices.
[H10N 30/871] Single-layered electrodes of multilayer piezoelectric or electrostrictive devices, e.g. internal electrodes -- H10N 30/871 covers single-layer electrodes used inside multilayer piezoelectric or electrostrictive components, such as internal electrodes that are formed as a single conductive layer within the stack. It applies to devices where the electrode is a single layer rather than laminated or composite.
[H10N 30/872] Interconnections, e.g. connection electrodes of multilayer piezoelectric or electrostrictive devices -- H10N 30/872 covers the design and arrangement of conductive connections, such as connection electrodes or interconnect structures, within multilayer piezoelectric or electrostrictive devices. It includes methods for forming, patterning or bonding electrodes that electrically link the stacked layers of such solid-state components.
[H10N 30/874] embedded within piezoelectric or electrostrictive material, e.g. via connections -- H10N 30/874 covers solid-state devices in which functional elements such as electrodes, sensors or circuitry are placed directly inside a piezoelectric or electrostrictive material, typically by embedding them during fabrication or through internal connections. This enables integrated actuation or sensing within the material itself.
[H10N 30/875] Further connection or lead arrangements, e.g. flexible wiring boards, terminal pins -- This subclass relates to the design of extra electrical interconnections for piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices, covering features such as flexible wiring boards, lead frames, terminal pins and other lead-arrangement elements that connect the active element to external circuitry.
[H10N 30/877] Conductive materials -- The code H10N 30/877 covers conductive materials used in piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices, such as electrodes, conductive coatings, interconnects and layers that permit electric-field interaction with the active material. It includes metals, alloys, polymer-based conductors and composites integrated into these devices.
[H10N 30/878] the principal material being non-metallic, e.g. oxide or carbon based -- This CPC subclass covers piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices whose active material is a non-metallic substance, such as oxides or carbon-based compounds. The classification applies to solid-state components that convert electrical signals into mechanical motion or vice versa using these non-metallic functional materials.
[H10N 30/88] Mounts -- H10N 30/88 covers mounts for piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive solid-state devices, including fixtures, supports, couplings and housings that secure the element to a structure or interface. The classification includes mechanical arrangements that provide alignment, vibration isolation or thermal management for such devices.
[H10N 30/883] Additional insulation means preventing electrical, physical or chemical damage, e.g. protective coatings -- The CPC subclass H10N 30/883 covers additional insulation measures applied to solid-state semiconductor devices to guard against electrical, mechanical or chemical harm. It includes protective coatings, encapsulation layers, barrier films and other dielectric or protective structures that prevent degradation or failure of the device.
[H10N 30/886] Additional mechanical prestressing means, e.g. springs -- H10N 30/886 covers solid-state piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive devices that incorporate mechanical prestressing elements, such as integrated springs, to apply a bias stress. The prestressing improves linearity, sensitivity, bandwidth or operating range of the device and is considered an additional mechanical means within the electric solid-state device category.

[H10N 35/01] Manufacture or treatment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Processes or apparatus for manufacturing a material, product or device, which exhibits or changes a magnetisation when subjected to mechanical stress or which exhibits a mechanical deformation when subjected to a magnetic field.
[H10N 35/101] with mechanical input and electrical output, e.g. generators, sensors -- H10N 35/101 covers solid-state magnetostrictive devices that receive mechanical energy and produce an electrical output. It includes generators, transducers and sensors that exploit the magnetostrictive effect to convert motion into voltage or current.
[H10N 35/80] Constructional details -- H10N 35/80 defines the constructional details of magnetostrictive devices, a type of solid-state semiconductor component that changes dimensions in response to a magnetic field. The entry covers structural features, housing, mounting and integration aspects of such devices within electrical systems. It falls under the hierarchy ELECTRICITY - SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES - ELECTRIC SOLID-STATE DEVICES NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR - Magnetostrictive devices.
[H10N 35/85] Magnetostrictive active materials -- Magnetostrictive active materials are substances that alter their shape or dimensions under a magnetic field and produce magnetic fields when mechanically stressed. They are employed in sensors, actuators, transducers and other devices that require precise motion or energy conversion. This CPC subgroup H10N 35/85 covers patents concerning the composition, processing, and performance of such magnetostrictive compounds.
[H10N 39/00] Integrated devices, or assemblies of multiple devices, comprising at least one piezoelectric, electrostrictive or magnetostrictive element covered by groups -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[H10N 50/00] Galvanomagnetic or similar magnetic-effect devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Galvanomagnetic devices, wherein an external magnetic field or a spin-polarised current directly influence properties of an electric current, e.g. amplitude, direction, or electronic spin, through a solid-state body.  These include devices based on:  This group covers galvanomagnetic devices which are pertinent to several technical fields or which are not limited to a particular application, i.e. galvanomagnetic devices in general. Aspects such as their structure, active materials and fabrication are classified here.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Ordinary Hall-effect devices -> H10N52/00 - Integrated devices or assemblies comprising galvanomagnetic or Hall-effect elements -> H10N59/00
[H10N 50/01] Manufacture or treatment -- H10N 50/01 covers processes for manufacturing or treating solid-state semiconductor devices that exploit galvanomagnetic or related magnetic effects, such as Hall-effect sensors and magnetoresistive components, including steps like material deposition, doping, annealing, patterning and final assembly.
[H10N 50/10] Magnetoresistive devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices based on magnetoresistance effects, in particular tunnel magnetoresistance (TMR) or giant magnetoresistance (GMR), e.g. magnetic tunnel junctions, spin valves. These devices can be controlled by an external magnetic field or a spin-polarised current, e.g. spin-transfer torque (STT).
[H10N 50/20] Spin-polarised current-controlled devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices operating based on a spin-polarised current, such as for spin current generator, spin logic or spin memory elements.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Magnetoresistive devices -> H10N50/10
[H10N 50/80] Constructional details -- H10N 50/80 covers the constructional details of solid-state semiconductor devices that use galvanomagnetic or related magnetic-effect phenomena, such as Hall-effect or magnetoresistive components. It addresses the physical layout, layer structures, contact arrangements and other design features that influence the magnetic response of these devices.
[H10N 50/85] Materials of the active region -- H10N 50/85 covers the selection, composition and preparation of the semiconductor or other functional materials that form the active region of galvanomagnetic or related magnetic-effect solid-state devices, such as Hall, magnetoresistive or spin-tronic components, where the material's electrical and magnetic properties are critical to device performance.
[H10N 52/00] Hall-effect devices -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices based on:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Integrated devices or assemblies comprising galvanomagnetic or Hall-effect elements -> H10N59/00
[H10N 52/01] Manufacture or treatment -- The H10N 52/01 subclass deals with the manufacturing and post-manufacturing treatment of Hall-effect devices, which are semiconductor components that generate a voltage when subjected to a magnetic field. It includes processes such as crystal growth, doping, deposition, annealing, patterning, and packaging steps specifically tailored to produce or modify Hall-effect sensors and related solid-state devices.
[H10N 52/101] Semiconductor Hall-effect devices -- Hall-effect devices are semiconductor components that generate a voltage proportional to the magnetic field intersecting a current-carrying conductor, enabling magnetic field sensing, current measurement, and position detection. They are fabricated from materials such as silicon or gallium arsenide and are widely used in automotive, industrial, and consumer electronics for contactless detection and control.
[H10N 52/80] Constructional details -- The H10N 52/80 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers constructional details of Hall-effect devices, including the geometry, materials, contacts, substrate, encapsulation and packaging features that define the physical structure and assembly of such semiconductor magnetic-field sensors.
[H10N 52/85] Materials of the active region -- H10N 52/85 covers the selection, preparation and properties of semiconductor or other functional materials that form the active region of Hall-effect devices, including dopant types, alloy compositions, crystalline structures and layer-growth techniques used to optimise magnetic-field sensing performance. It addresses material choices that directly affect device sensitivity, temperature stability and linearity.
