[H10P 14/3446] Transition metal elements; Rare earth elements -- The CPC entry H10P 14/3446 pertains to processes for forming semiconductor materials, such as layers or pillars, that specifically involve transition-metal or rare-earth elements. It covers methods of depositing, alloying, doping or otherwise treating these elements within the manufacturing of semiconductor devices. This falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and generic manufacturing processes.
[H10P 14/3448] Delta-doping -- In semiconductor fabrication, delta-doping is the creation of an ultra-thin, highly doped impurity sheet within a crystal lattice, usually formed during epitaxial growth to locally modify carrier concentration and electronic properties. This technique is employed in devices such as high-electron-mobility transistors, quantum wells and lasers to enhance performance.
[H10P 14/3451] Structure -- This entry deals with the structural configuration of semiconductor materials created as layers, pillars or similar shapes during fabrication, covering aspects such as thickness, pattern, stacking and spatial arrangement that define the final device architecture.
[H10P 14/3452] Microstructure -- H10P 14/3452 covers aspects of the microstructure of semiconductor materials formed during processes such as deposition of layers, pillars or other geometric features. It relates to the internal structural characteristics that influence electrical performance, including grain size, crystal orientation and defects. The classification is used for patents describing how microstructural properties are achieved or controlled.
[H10P 14/3454] Amorphous -- The CPC subclass H10P 14/3454 covers processes and apparatus for forming amorphous semiconductor materials, such as depositing or shaping amorphous layers, films or pillars used in electronic devices. It falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, generic manufacturing or treatment methods, specifically the formation of materials in layered or pillar structures.
[H10P 14/3456] Polycrystalline -- This CPC subclass deals with methods and apparatus for forming polycrystalline material, such as layers or pillars, used in semiconductor devices. It covers processes that produce polycrystalline structures during device manufacturing, including deposition, crystallization, and patterning steps.
[H10P 14/3458] Monocrystalline -- H10P 14/3458 concerns processes and equipment for producing monocrystalline semiconductor material, such as single-crystal layers, wafers or pillar structures. It covers techniques that grow or shape a continuous crystal lattice, including epitaxial deposition, Czochralski pulling or other crystal-growth methods used in semiconductor device manufacturing.
[H10P 14/3461] Nanoparticles -- The CPC subclass H10P 14/3461 deals with the formation of semiconductor materials using nanoparticles, covering processes that create layers, pillars or other structures composed of nanoscale particles for electronic devices. It includes methods of depositing, patterning or treating nanoparticle assemblies within semiconductor fabrication.
[H10P 14/3462] Nanowires -- H10P 14/3462 relates to processes and apparatus for forming semiconductor nanowires, including their growth, deposition and patterning into layers, pillars or arrays for use in electronic, optoelectronic and sensing applications.
[H10P 14/3464] Nanotubes -- H10P 14/3464 covers technologies for producing semiconductor nanotubes and related tubular nanostructures, including methods of growth, deposition and patterning on substrates. It concerns the formation of material in tubular shapes for use in electronic or optoelectronic devices.
[H10P 14/3466] Crystal orientation -- H10P 14/3466 relates to techniques for controlling the crystallographic orientation of semiconductor layers or structures during their formation, such as by epitaxial growth, deposition, or patterning. The classification includes processes that align grains, orient wafers, or set the directional properties of crystal lattices to achieve desired electrical characteristics.
[H10P 14/36] characterised by treatments done before the formation of the materials -- H10P 14/36 covers semiconductor device manufacturing where the process includes pre-formation treatments of the material, such as surface cleaning, doping or etching applied before layers or pillars are formed. It falls under generic processes for semiconductor devices within the electricity section.
[H10P 14/3602] In-situ cleaning -- The entry H10P 14/3602 covers cleaning actions carried out directly on semiconductor structures while they remain in place during fabrication, such as removing contaminants from freshly formed layers or pillars without removing the wafer or device from the process environment. It belongs to the CPC subgroup for generic processes used to manufacture or treat semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/38] characterised by treatments done after the formation of the materials -- H10P 14/38 covers post-formation treatments applied to semiconductor materials shaped as layers, pillars or similar structures, such as annealing, doping, surface modification or cleaning performed after the material has been formed. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Processes or Apparatus for the Manufacture or Treatment of Devices Covered by Class, specifically dealing with treatments carried out after material formation.
[H10P 14/3802] Crystallisation or recrystallisation of non-monocrystalline semiconductor materials, e.g. regrowth -- This CPC subclass covers methods for crystallising or recrystallising semiconductor materials that are not monocrystalline, such as polycrystalline or amorphous films, including regrowth techniques used to form layers, pillars or other structures. It falls under generic processes for manufacturing semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/3804] using crystallisation-inhibiting elements -- H10P 14/3804 relates to semiconductor manufacturing processes that incorporate crystallisation-inhibiting elements during the formation of material structures such as layers or pillars, aiming to control crystal growth and improve device properties. This subclass falls under generic processes for making or treating semiconductor devices and focuses on using additives that suppress unwanted crystallisation.
[H10P 14/3806] using crystallisation-enhancing elements -- The CPC entry H10P 14/3806 covers the use of crystallisation-enhancing elements during the formation of semiconductor materials, such as layered or pillar structures. It falls within generic processes for manufacturing or treating semiconductor devices, specifically addressing how added elements promote crystal growth in these formations.
[H10P 14/3808] using laser beams -- The subclass H10P 14/3808 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps where laser beams are used to form material structures such as layers, pillars, or other shaped regions. It includes laser-driven deposition, ablation, annealing, or patterning techniques applied to create or modify device layers.
[H10P 14/381] Beam shaping, e.g. using a mask -- H10P 14/381 relates to methods for shaping a radiation beam, for example with a mask or similar patterning element, during the formation of semiconductor materials such as layered or pillar structures.
[H10P 14/3812] Shape of mask -- The CPC entry H10P 14/3812 covers the geometry of masks employed in semiconductor manufacturing, particularly during the formation of layered or pillar-like material structures. It pertains to generic processes or apparatuses used to treat devices within the broader electricity-semiconductor-device classification.
[H10P 14/3814] Continuous wave laser beam -- H10P 14/3814 covers continuous-wave laser beams employed in the formation of semiconductor materials, such as depositing or patterning layers and pillars during device manufacturing. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes for making or treating devices, focusing on material formation techniques.
[H10P 14/3816] Pulsed laser beam -- The H10P 14/3816 entry covers the use of pulsed laser beams to form semiconductor materials, such as creating thin layers, pillars, or other shaped structures during device fabrication. It pertains to generic processes or apparatuses that treat or manufacture semiconductor devices by depositing or modifying material with short, high-energy laser pulses. This classification falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and manufacturing processes.
[H10P 14/3818] using particle beams -- This CPC subclass covers methods for forming semiconductor materials or structures by directing particle beams, such as ion, electron, or plasma beams, onto substrates to deposit, pattern, or modify layers, pillars, or other features during device manufacture.
[H10P 14/382] Scanning of a beam -- H10P 14/382 covers techniques for scanning a beam-such as electron, ion, laser or photon beams-across a substrate during the formation of semiconductor materials, including layers, pillars or other structures, to pattern, modify or treat the device.
[H10P 14/3822] Controlling the interface between substrate and epitaxial layer, e.g. by ion implantation followed by annealing -- H10P 14/3822 concerns techniques for managing the interface between a substrate and an epitaxial layer in semiconductor manufacturing, typically by ion implantation followed by thermal annealing to modify composition or stress. The methods aim to improve layer quality, adhesion or electrical properties.
[H10P 14/3824] Intermixing, interdiffusion or disordering of III-V heterostructures, e.g. IILD -- H10P 14/3824 relates to processes that intentionally cause intermixing, interdiffusion or disordering of III-V semiconductor heterostructures, for example by ion implantation induced layer disordering (IILD). The methods are used to modify band-gap profiles or strain in layered devices such as lasers and detectors.
[H10P 14/40] of conductive or resistive materials -- This subclass deals with processes that form conductive or resistive material structures-such as layers, pillars or other shapes-used in semiconductor devices. It includes deposition, patterning, doping or related treatments that create conductive pathways or resistive elements, defining the material's electrical properties and geometry during manufacture.
[H10P 14/412] Deposition of metallic or metal-silicide materials -- H10P 14/412 covers methods for depositing metallic or metal-silicide films onto semiconductor substrates, such as forming thin layers or pillars used in device fabrication. [H10P 14/6302] Non-deposition formation processes -- Non-deposition formation processes create semiconductor structures such as layers, pillars or other shapes without using deposition techniques; they include methods like bulk crystal growth, epitaxial growth, molding, stamping or laser-induced structuring to shape the material directly for device fabrication.

The entry falls under generic processes for manufacturing semiconductor devices, focusing on material formation in the gas-phase stage.
[H10P 14/6329] using physical ablation of a target, e.g. physical vapour deposition or pulsed laser deposition -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10P 14/6329 pertains to the formation of materials in semiconductor device manufacturing through physical ablation techniques such as physical vapor deposition or pulsed laser deposition. This process involves removing material from a target and depositing it onto a substrate to form layers or structures.
[H10P 14/6332] using thermal evaporation  formation of epitaxial layers by a deposition process  H10P14/6349 -- This CPC subclass H10P 14/6332 concerns the use of thermal evaporation to create epitaxial semiconductor layers by a deposition process. It falls under the broader category of generic processes for manufacturing or treating semiconductor devices, specifically those involving the formation of materials in layer or pillar form.
[H10P 14/6334] using decomposition or reaction of gaseous or vapour phase compounds, i.e. chemical vapour deposition  deposition by physical ablation of a target  H10P14/6329 -- H10P 14/6334 classifies methods that form semiconductor layers or pillars by breaking down or reacting gaseous or vapor-phase compounds, such as chemical vapor deposition, or by depositing material through physical ablation of a target. The entry falls under generic processes for manufacturing devices covered by the electricity and semiconductor devices classes.
[H10P 14/6336] in the presence of a plasma [PECVD] -- This CPC subclass deals with producing semiconductor layers or structures by depositing material from a gaseous precursor while a plasma is present, i.e., plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition. It covers methods where the plasma assists the chemical reactions that create the film or pillar on a substrate.
[H10P 14/6338] the reactions being activated by other means than plasma or thermal, e.g. photo-CVD -- H10P 14/6338 covers semiconductor material formation methods where the chemical reactions are driven by non-thermal activation such as light, rather than plasma or heat, for example photochemical chemical vapor deposition used to create layers or pillars. This falls under generic processes for manufacturing devices in the electricity-semiconductor hierarchy.
[H10P 14/6339] deposition by cyclic CVD, e.g. ALD, ALE or pulsed CVD -- H10P 14/6339 relates to the formation of semiconductor layers or structures by cyclic chemical-vapor deposition techniques, such as atomic-layer deposition, atomic-layer etching or pulsed CVD, where precursor gases are introduced and removed in repeated cycles to build up material thickness with atomic-scale control.
[H10P 14/6342] Liquid deposition, e.g. spin-coating, sol-gel techniques or spray coating -- H10P 14/6342 covers liquid deposition techniques used to form thin layers or structures on semiconductor devices, including spin-coating, sol-gel processes, and spray coating. These methods are employed to create uniform films or pillars during device fabrication.
[H10P 14/6344] using Langmuir-Blodgett techniques -- This CPC subgroup covers semiconductor device manufacturing steps that employ Langmuir-Blodgett techniques to deposit or arrange thin films, monolayers or multilayers on a substrate. The methods involve transferring material from a compressed liquid-air interface to create controlled layers or pillars for device structures.
[H10P 14/6346] using printing, e.g. ink-jet printing -- This subclass covers methods of forming semiconductor layers or structures by depositing material with printing techniques, such as ink-jet printing, to pattern or build functional films, pillars or other shapes on a substrate. It falls under CPC H10P for generic manufacturing processes of semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/6348] Liquid ALD -- A liquid atomic-layer-deposition (ALD) process that uses liquid-phase precursors to deposit thin, conformal layers or pillars on semiconductor devices. It enables precise, sequential surface reactions for material formation in integrated-circuit fabrication, fitting within the broader CPC group for semiconductor device manufacturing and layer-formation methods.
[H10P 14/6349] Deposition of epitaxial materials -- H10P 14/6349 covers processes for depositing epitaxial materials on semiconductor substrates, including techniques such as chemical vapor deposition, molecular-beam epitaxy and related methods that create crystalline layers or pillars for device manufacturing.
[H10P 14/65] characterised by treatments performed before or after the formation of the materials -- H10P 14/65 covers semiconductor manufacturing methods where the material is treated either prior to or after it is shaped into layers, pillars or similar structures. The entry includes processes such as surface cleaning, doping, annealing, etching or other chemical-physical modifications that are applied before the material is formed or after the formation step to improve device performance or reliability.
[H10P 14/6502] of treatments performed before formation of the materials -- H10P 14/6502 covers treatments applied to semiconductor devices before the formation of material layers or structures such as pillars. This subcategory falls under generic processes for manufacturing or treating semiconductor devices within the electricity field, focusing on pre-formation processing steps.
[H10P 14/6504] In-situ cleaning -- H10P 14/6504 covers in-situ cleaning methods applied during the formation of semiconductor materials such as layers or pillars, without removing the substrate from the production environment. It is part of the CPC hierarchy for electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic processes or apparatus for manufacturing or treating those devices.
[H10P 14/6506] Formation of intermediate materials -- The code H10P 14/6506 relates to methods for creating intermediate semiconductor materials during device fabrication. It includes processes that form layers, pillars or other shaped bodies that serve as temporary or functional structures within the manufacturing sequence. These techniques are part of generic processes for semiconductor device production under the electricity and semiconductor devices sections of the CPC.
[H10P 14/6508] by exposure to a liquid -- This subclass concerns semiconductor material formation methods in which a solid substrate is treated by exposure to a liquid that causes deposition, growth or alteration of a layer or pillar, for example chemical bath deposition, liquid-phase epitaxy or solution-based coating. It is part of the CPC hierarchy covering generic processes for manufacturing semiconductor devices under the electricity and semiconductor devices sections.
[H10P 14/6509] by exposure to electromagnetic radiation, e.g. UV light -- H10P 14/6509 covers processes for forming semiconductor materials, such as layers or pillars, by exposing them to electromagnetic radiation like ultraviolet light. It falls under generic manufacturing or treatment methods for devices in the electricity-semiconductor devices hierarchy. The entry specifically addresses radiation-induced material formation techniques.
[H10P 14/6512] by exposure to a gas or vapour -- H10P 14/6512 covers processes for forming semiconductor layers or pillars by exposing a substrate to a gas or vapour, such as chemical-vapor-deposition, plasma-enhanced deposition, or other gas-phase reactions that create or modify the material structure. This falls under the CPC hierarchy for electricity → semiconductor devices → generic manufacturing or treatment processes.
[H10P 14/6514] by exposure to a plasma -- This subclass covers semiconductor-device manufacture steps in which a material is formed or shaped by exposure to a plasma, including plasma-assisted deposition, etching or surface modification for creating layers, pillars or similar structures. It falls under CPC H10P 14/65, which relates to generic processes for forming materials in semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/6516] of treatments performed after formation of the materials -- The subclass H10P 14/6516 covers post-formation treatments applied to semiconductor materials such as layers or pillars, including annealing, doping, surface modification, cleaning or other processes that alter the material after it has been formed. It is part of the CPC group for generic processes used in the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/6518] by introduction of substances into an already-existing insulating layer -- This subclass covers semiconductor fabrication steps that create new material structures-such as layers, pillars, or similar features-by adding substances into a pre-existing insulating layer. It is used for processes that modify the insulating film rather than forming it from scratch, typically in the manufacturing or treatment of semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/6519] the substance being oxygen -- H10P 14/6519 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps where oxygen is the material being deposited or incorporated, such as forming oxide layers or oxygen-containing pillars within devices. It falls under generic processes for creating layered or pillar structures in semiconductor devices, part of the electricity and semiconductor device sections of the CPC.
[H10P 14/6522] introduced into a nitride material, e.g. changing SiN to SiON -- The code H10P 14/6522 covers processes for introducing additional elements into nitride semiconductor materials, such as converting silicon nitride (SiN) into silicon oxynitride (SiON). It relates to manufacturing steps that modify the composition of nitride layers to achieve desired electrical or optical properties. This falls under generic methods for producing semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/6524] the substance being nitrogen -- H10P 14/6524 pertains to processes for forming semiconductor materials, specifically focusing on the use of nitrogen as a key component in the formation of layers or pillars. This classification is part of the broader category dealing with generic manufacturing and treatment processes for semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/6526] introduced into an oxide material, e.g. changing SiO to SiON -- H10P 14/6526 covers methods for incorporating additional elements into an oxide semiconductor, for example converting silicon dioxide into silicon oxynitride, typically during layer or pillar formation. These techniques are used in the manufacturing of semiconductor devices to adjust electrical or optical properties.
[H10P 14/6528] In-situ cleaning after layer formation, e.g. removing process residues -- H10P 14/6528 covers methods for cleaning a semiconductor structure in place after a material layer has been formed, such as removing residues from the deposition or patterning steps. The techniques are applied during the same processing sequence to prepare the surface for subsequent manufacturing stages.
[H10P 14/6529] by exposure to a gas or vapour -- H10P 14/6529 covers methods of forming semiconductor structures, such as layers or pillars, by treating the material with a gas or vapour. The exposure step is used to deposit, modify or etch the semiconductor surface during device manufacturing.
[H10P 14/6532] by exposure to a plasma -- A method for forming semiconductor material structures, such as layers or pillars, by exposing the material to a plasma, which modifies or deposits the desired composition during the manufacturing process. This falls under generic processes for treating devices covered by the class.
[H10P 14/6534] by exposure to a liquid -- This CPC subclass covers methods for forming semiconductor device materials, such as layers or pillars, by exposing the material to a liquid, including techniques like chemical-bath deposition or wet etching. It lies within generic processes for manufacturing semiconductor devices under the electricity and semiconductor devices sections.
[H10P 14/6536] by exposure to radiation, e.g. visible light -- The subclass H10P 14/6536 covers processes that form semiconductor materials, such as layers or pillars, by exposing the material to radiation, for example visible light, as part of the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/6538] by exposure to UV light -- H10P 14/6538 covers processes for forming semiconductor device structures, such as layers or pillars, by exposure to ultraviolet light. It is situated in the CPC hierarchy under electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic manufacturing or treatment processes for such devices.
[H10P 14/6539] by exposure to corpuscular radiation, e.g. exposure to electrons, alpha-particles, protons or ions -- H10P 14/6539 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that form layers or pillars by exposing the material to corpuscular radiation such as electrons, alpha-particles, protons or ions, using the radiation to deposit, implant or modify the material structure during device fabrication.
[H10P 14/6542] by using coherent radiation, e.g. using a laser -- The H10P 14/6542 subclass covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that employ coherent radiation, such as laser beams, to shape or modify materials. It includes processes that form layers, pillars or other structures by directing laser energy onto the semiconductor substrate. This method is applied in the production of electronic devices and integrated circuits.
[H10P 14/6544] to change the morphology of the insulating materials, e.g. transformation of an amorphous layer into a crystalline layer -- This CPC subclass covers processes that modify the morphology of insulating materials in semiconductor devices, for example converting an amorphous layer into a crystalline layer, to improve or tailor properties during device fabrication. It is nested under electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic manufacturing or treatment methods.
[H10P 14/6546] to change the surface groups of the insulating materials -- The code H10P 14/6546 covers processes that modify the chemical groups on the surface of insulating materials used in semiconductor components such as dielectric layers or pillars. Such surface-group alteration is employed to adjust adhesion, dielectric properties, or compatibility during device fabrication.
[H10P 14/6548] by forming intermediate materials, e.g. capping layers or diffusion barriers -- The H10P 14/6548 subclass covers processes that create intermediate semiconductor materials, such as capping layers or diffusion-barrier layers, during device fabrication. It addresses the formation of these layers-often in patterned or stacked forms-to protect or isolate functional regions within electronic components. This includes methods for depositing, shaping, or treating such intermediate structures.
[H10P 14/66] characterised by the type of materials -- H10P 14/66 relates to semiconductor device manufacturing steps that are defined by the specific type of material employed, such as particular semiconductor, dielectric, metallic or composite substances used to form layers, pillars or other structures. This sub-group falls under generic processes for the formation of materials in the CPC hierarchy for electricity-related semiconductor devices.
[H10P 14/662] Laminate layers, e.g. stacks of alternating high-k metal oxides  adhesion layers or buffer layers  H10P14/6508 ,  H10P14/6548 -- H10P 14/662 pertains to the formation of laminate layers in semiconductor devices, specifically involving stacks of alternating high-k metal oxides with adhesion or buffer layers, as detailed under related codes H10P14/6508 and H10P14/6548.
[H10P 14/665] Porous materials -- H10P 14/665 covers porous materials used in semiconductor device fabrication, such as layered or pillar-shaped structures, and relates to processes or apparatuses that form these materials during manufacturing or treatment steps. It falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic manufacturing processes.
[H10P 14/668] the materials being characterised by the deposition precursor materials -- H10P 14/668 covers the characterization of materials used as deposition precursors in the formation of semiconductor layers or structures. The subclass falls under generic processes for manufacturing semiconductor devices, focusing on material formation.

[H10P 32/1404] using predeposition followed by drive-in of impurities into the semiconductor surface, e.g. predeposition from a gaseous phase -- The entry H10P 32/1404 covers semiconductor doping methods that first apply a predeposited layer of impurity onto the wafer surface and then diffuse (drive-in) the dopants deeper into the material. It includes processes using a gaseous phase for the predeposition step and subsequent thermal diffusion to achieve the desired concentration profile.

It falls under generic processes for semiconductor devices within the electricity and semiconductor devices hierarchy.

It falls under the broader classes of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic manufacturing or treatment processes for those devices.

It falls under the broader category of semiconductor device manufacturing processes.

[H10P 50/694] characterised by their behaviour during the process, e.g. soluble masks or redeposited masks -- H10P 50/694 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that remove layers, structures or materials where the removal method is defined by its behaviour during processing, such as using soluble masks that dissolve or redeposited masks that are stripped away. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices, specifically the removal sub-group.
[H10P 50/695] characterised by the process involved to create the mask, e.g. lift-off masks or sidewalls or to modify the mask -- H10P 50/695 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that involve forming or altering the mask used to define features, such as creating lift-off masks, sidewall masks, or other mask modifications. The classification is applied to processes that remove layers, structures or materials by means of these specially prepared masks.
[H10P 50/696] Process specially adapted to improve the resolution of the mask -- H10P 50/696 relates to processes specially adapted to improve the resolution of photomasks used in semiconductor fabrication, typically by refining pattern definition during removal of layers, structures or materials, thereby enabling finer features and higher pattern fidelity in lithographic steps.
[H10P 50/71] using masks for conductive or resistive materials -- The subclass covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that employ a mask to define where conductive or resistive material is retained or removed during processing. It includes the use of photolithographic, shadow or other types of masks to protect selected areas while etching, stripping or otherwise treating the material, enabling precise patterning of electrical features.
[H10P 50/73] using masks for insulating materials -- H10P 50/73 covers semiconductor manufacturing methods that employ masks to protect selected areas while insulating materials are removed, such as during etching or stripping steps. The subclass includes techniques for applying and using masks to define patterns in insulating layers during device fabrication.

Group H10P52/00 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10P50/00 . Group H10P52/00 is also impacted by reclassification into groups H10P52/20 , H10P52/40 , H10P54/00 - H10P54/94 and H10P95/60 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

[H10P 52/202] of semiconductor materials -- Group H10P52/202 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10P52/402 . Groups H10P52/402 and H10P52/202 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 52/203] of conductive or resistive materials -- H10P 52/203 relates to grinding, lapping or polishing operations performed on semiconductor wafers, substrates or device parts where conductive or resistive materials are used, such as metal-based slurries or resistive polishing pads. The code falls under generic processes for treating semiconductor devices in the CPC system.

[H10P 52/402] of semiconductor materials -- Group H10P52/402 is impacted by reclassification into group H10P52/202 . Groups H10P52/402 and H10P52/202 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 52/403] of conductive or resistive materials -- This subclass deals with grinding, lapping or polishing operations applied to semiconductor wafers, substrates or device parts that are made of conductive or resistive materials. It falls under the broader category of generic processes for manufacturing or treating semiconductor devices within the electricity sector.

[H10P 54/00] Cutting or separating of wafers, substrates or parts of devices -- This group covers cutting or separating of wafers or substrates having semiconductor or solid-state devices formed, or to be formed, therein or thereon. The cutting may be partial, e.g. for making a groove. Groups H10P54/00 , H10P54/20 , H10P54/30 , H10P54/40 , H10P54/50 , H10P54/90 , H10P54/922 , H10P54/924 and H10P54/94 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10P52/00 and H10P58/00 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 54/20] by laser cutting -- H10P 54/20 pertains to the use of laser cutting for separating semiconductor wafers, substrates or individual device parts during manufacturing. It falls within the broader hierarchy of electricity, semiconductor devices and generic processes or apparatus for treating devices covered by the class.
[H10P 54/30] by forming weakened zones for subsequent cutting or separating, e.g. by laser treatment or by ion implantation -- The subclass H10P 54/30 covers processes that create weakened zones in semiconductor wafers, substrates or device parts so they can be cut or separated later, typically by using laser treatment or ion implantation to define fracture lines that simplify subsequent splitting.
[H10P 54/40] by sawing, e.g. using revolving or reciprocating blades -- H10P 54/40 relates to the cutting or separating of semiconductor wafers, substrates or device components by sawing, employing revolving or reciprocating blades as the cutting tool. This falls within the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and generic processes for manufacturing or treating such devices.
[H10P 54/50] by scoring, breaking or cleaving -- H10P 54/50 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps in which wafers, substrates or device parts are cut, separated or cleaved by scoring, breaking or cleaving. It is situated under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices covered by class, describing a mechanical method for dividing semiconductor material.
[H10P 54/52] by cleaving -- Group H10P54/52 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10P10/12 , H10P52/00 , H10P58/00 , H10P90/1914 and H10P90/1916 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 54/90] Auxiliary processes or arrangements -- H10P 54/90 covers auxiliary processes or arrangements that assist in the cutting or separating of semiconductor wafers, substrates or device parts. It falls within the broader category of generic manufacturing processes for semiconductor devices, addressing supplemental steps that support the primary cutting operations.
[H10P 54/92] for protecting or reinforcing the surface of wafers or substrates during cutting or separating, e.g. using adhesive tapes -- Group H10P54/92 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10P52/00 . Groups H10P52/00 and H10P54/92 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 54/922] Arrangements for stress mitigation, e.g. crack stops -- H10P 54/922 covers arrangements that reduce mechanical stress during the cutting or separating of semiconductor wafers, substrates, or device parts, such as crack-stop features or stress-relief structures integrated into the process equipment or the wafer itself. These measures help prevent fracture and improve yield.
[H10P 54/924] using expanding wafer tapes -- This subclass covers methods that use expandable adhesive tapes to hold and separate semiconductor wafers, substrates or device parts during cutting, dicing or thinning operations. The tape is stretched or expanded to apply controlled pressure, enabling clean separation while minimizing damage to the material. It is part of the broader class for processes that cut or separate wafers in semiconductor manufacturing.
[H10P 54/94] After-treatments, e.g. removal of adhesive tapes or supports -- H10P 54/94 pertains to post-processing operations applied to semiconductor wafers, substrates or device parts after they have been cut or separated, specifically the removal of temporary adhesives, tapes, supports or similar bonding layers used during handling or processing. This subclass falls within the CPC hierarchy for electricity-related semiconductor devices and generic manufacturing processes, under the broader category of cutting or separating wafers and related components.

Groups H10P10/12 , H10P90/00 and H10P56/00 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 58/00] Singulating wafers or substrates into multiple chips, i.e. dicing -- When classifying in this group, any process step involving cutting or separating, which is considered to represent information of interest for search, may also be classified in group H10P54/00 . Group H10P58/00 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10P58/20 - H10P58/22 , H10P54/00 , H10P54/20 , H10P54/30 , H10P54/40 , H10P54/50 - H10P54/52 , H10P54/90 , H10P54/922 , H10P54/924 and H10P54/94 . [H10P 58/22] characterised by the singulation processes being performed on multiple sides of the wafer or substrate -- H10P 58/22 pertains to methods for cutting wafers or substrates into individual chips, specifically focusing on techniques that involve processing more than one side of the material. This classification is part of the broader category dealing with semiconductor device manufacturing processes.
[H10P 70/00] Other manufacture or treatment -- This group does not cover the cleaning of package elements, package parts or other constructional details, e.g. cleaning of packages after moulding, which are covered by the related groups of subclass H10W .
[H10P 70/10] Cleaning before device manufacture, i.e. Begin-Of-Line process -- H10P 70/10 covers cleaning operations performed at the beginning of the production line for semiconductor devices, prior to any device fabrication steps. It addresses methods and apparatuses for removing contaminants from wafers or other substrates to ensure proper subsequent processing. This entry falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic manufacturing or treatment processes.
[H10P 70/12] by dry cleaning only  H10P70/52  takes precedence
[H10P 70/125] with gaseous HF -- The CPC subclass H10P 70/125 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that employ gaseous hydrogen fluoride, typically for etching, cleaning or surface modification of devices. It falls under generic processes or apparatus for the treatment of semiconductor components, emphasizing the use of HF gas as a reactive agent in the fabrication sequence.
[H10P 70/15] by wet cleaning only  H10P70/52  takes precedence -- The classification covers semiconductor device cleaning steps that use only liquid-based (wet) cleaning methods, without dry or plasma processes, and it is subordinate to H10P70/52, which takes precedence.
[H10P 70/18] by combined dry cleaning and wet cleaning  H10P70/52  takes precedence -- H10P 70/18 covers semiconductor device manufacturing steps that employ a combination of dry cleaning followed by wet cleaning to remove contaminants. This hybrid cleaning approach is used after formation or before further processing of chips. The more specific subclass H10P70/52 has priority over this entry.
[H10P 70/20] Cleaning during device manufacture -- H10P 70/20 addresses methods and apparatus for cleaning semiconductor devices while they are being manufactured, including the removal of contaminants, residues or particles to preserve device performance and reliability. It falls under the hierarchy of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of such devices.
[H10P 70/23] during, before or after processing of insulating materials -- This CPC subclass H10P 70/23 covers methods applied to semiconductor devices that involve handling of insulating materials at any stage - prior to, during, or after their processing. It includes steps such as cleaning, coating, curing, or inspection of dielectrics used in chips. The subclass falls under generic processes for semiconductor device manufacture.
[H10P 70/234] the processing being the formation of vias or contact holes -- In the CPC, class H10P covers manufacturing processes for semiconductor devices, and subclass 70/234 specifically addresses the step of forming vias or contact holes that connect different layers within a chip.
[H10P 70/237] the processing being a planarisation of insulating layers -- This CPC subgroup covers methods used to planarise insulating layers on semiconductor devices, such as chemical-mechanical polishing or other flattening techniques applied during device fabrication. It is part of the broader category for generic manufacturing or treatment processes of semiconductor devices, focusing specifically on the leveling of dielectric coatings to achieve uniform thickness and surface planarity.
[H10P 70/27] during, before or after processing of conductive materials, e.g. polysilicon or amorphous silicon layers -- This subclass covers methods applied at any stage of handling conductive semiconductor layers, such as polysilicon or amorphous silicon, including surface preparation, doping, annealing or protective coating, performed before, during, or after the main fabrication steps. It is classified under CPC H10P 70/27 within electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes.
[H10P 70/273] the processing being a delineation of conductive layers, e.g. by RIE -- H10P 70/273 covers processes that delineate conductive layers in semiconductor devices, typically by reactive ion etching or similar techniques. It falls under the CPC group for generic manufacturing or treatment of semiconductor devices, specifically other manufacturing steps beyond standard processing.
[H10P 70/277] the processing being a planarisation of conductive layers -- H10P 70/277 relates to methods for planarising conductive layers during the manufacture of semiconductor devices. It includes techniques that level or smooth conductive films to achieve a uniform surface before further processing. The class falls under generic processes for treatment of semiconductor devices within the electricity and semiconductor device sections of the CPC.
[H10P 70/30] Cleaning after the substrates have been singulated -- H10P 70/30 covers processes and apparatus for cleaning semiconductor substrates after they have been singulated, that is, after individual dies or chips have been separated from a larger wafer. The classification is used for post-singulation washing, drying, surface-particle removal and related treatments in semiconductor manufacturing.
[H10P 70/40] Cleaning for reclaiming -- H10P 70/40 deals with cleaning operations used to reclaim semiconductor devices after use. It includes methods for removing residues, oxides, or other contaminants from wafers, chips, or other semiconductor components to enable their reuse or further processing.
[H10P 70/50] characterised by the part to be cleaned -- This CPC subclass groups cleaning methods or apparatus for semiconductor devices where the defining factor is the specific part being cleaned, such as wafers, substrates, contacts or other components. It includes processes and equipment tailored to clean particular elements of a device rather than the device as a whole.
[H10P 70/52] Cleaning of diamond -- H10P 70/52 refers to processes and equipment for cleaning diamond materials employed in semiconductor manufacturing. It includes methods for removing contaminants, residues or surface damage from diamond substrates, films, particles or components before or after device fabrication. The classification falls under generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor devices.
[H10P 70/54] Cleaning of wafer edges -- H10P 70/54 belongs to the CPC subclass for semiconductor devices and covers methods for cleaning the edges of semiconductor wafers. It deals with processes that remove contaminants or residues from the wafer periphery after fabrication steps, improving yield and preventing edge-related defects. This falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic manufacturing or treatment processes.
[H10P 70/56] Cleaning of wafer backside -- H10P 70/56 covers methods and apparatus for cleaning the backside of semiconductor wafers, including chemical, plasma, mechanical or thermal techniques used to remove contaminants, particles, residues or films before subsequent processing steps. It falls under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Processes or Apparatus for Manufacture or Treatment of Devices → Other Manufacture or Treatment.
[H10P 70/58] Cleaning of porous materials -- H10P 70/58 relates to methods and apparatus for cleaning porous semiconductor or electronic materials, such as filters, membranes or porous layers, used in device fabrication. It includes chemical, mechanical, ultrasonic, plasma or other techniques designed to remove contaminants while preserving the material's structure.
[H10P 70/60] Cleaning only by mechanical processes -- H10P 70/60 covers procedures that clean semiconductor components exclusively through mechanical actions such as brushing, wiping, ultrasonic agitation or abrasive polishing, without the use of chemicals or solvents. It applies to the removal of particles, residues or films from wafers, chips and related devices during manufacturing or post-process handling. This mechanical-only cleaning is classified under general processes for semiconductor device treatment.
[H10P 70/70] Cleaning only by lasers processes, e.g. laser ablation -- The H10P 70/70 subclass covers methods that clean semiconductor or other electric devices solely using laser-based techniques, such as laser ablation, without any chemical or mechanical steps. It includes processes that remove contaminants, oxides, or residues by directing laser energy onto the surface to vaporise or detach unwanted material. This subclass is part of the broader category for generic manufacturing or treatment of devices covered by the electricity class.
[H10P 70/80] Cleaning only by supercritical fluids -- H10P 70/80 concerns processes that clean semiconductor devices exclusively with supercritical fluids, where the fluid's temperature and pressure exceed its critical point to achieve solvent properties without surface tension. This method removes contaminants without mechanical abrasion and is suitable for delicate semiconductor structures.
[H10P 72/00] Handling or holding of wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture or treatment thereof -- H10P 72/00 is a CPC subclass under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Processes or Apparatus for the Manufacture or Treatment of Devices. It covers methods, tools and fixtures used to handle, hold, transport or position wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices during manufacturing or processing steps.
[H10P 72/04] Apparatus for manufacture or treatment -- H10P 72/04 covers apparatus used to handle or hold semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment steps. It includes fixtures, carriers, and transport mechanisms that secure the items while they undergo processes such as deposition, etching, or inspection.
[H10P 72/0402] Apparatus for fluid treatment  H10P72/0441 ,  H10P72/0448  take precedence -- H10P 72/0402 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers apparatus for fluid treatment during the handling or holding of wafers, substrates, or devices in semiconductor manufacturing processes. This entry is subordinate to more specific codes H10P72/0441 and H10P72/0448, which take precedence when applicable.
[H10P 72/0404] for general liquid treatment, e.g. etching followed by cleaning -- The subclass defines apparatus or methods for handling semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices while they undergo a generic liquid process, such as an etching step that is subsequently followed by cleaning. It covers the means of holding, transporting or supporting the items throughout the combined treatment.
[H10P 72/0406] for cleaning followed by drying, rinsing, stripping, blasting or the like -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/0406 covers procedures applied to wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices while they are being handled or held, specifically cleaning steps that are followed by drying, rinsing, stripping, blasting or similar treatments. It falls under generic processes for manufacturing or treating such devices.
[H10P 72/0408] for drying -- This subclass covers methods and equipment used to dry semiconductor wafers, substrates, or devices during processing, including apparatus that hold or transport the wafers while moisture is removed. It is part of the broader category for handling or holding of wafers in the CPC hierarchy.
[H10P 72/0411] for wet cleaning or washing -- H10P 72/0411 covers wet cleaning or washing steps applied to semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices while they are being handled or held during manufacture or treatment, such as rinsing, solvent or chemical baths used to remove contaminants before further processing.
[H10P 72/0412] using mainly scrubbing means, e.g. brushes -- This subclass concerns methods for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment that primarily employ scrubbing mechanisms such as brushes. The scrubbing means may be used to clean, transport or position the items within processing equipment.
[H10P 72/0414] using mainly spraying means, e.g. nozzles -- H10P 72/0414 covers methods for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment that employ primarily spraying means, such as nozzles, to move, position, or protect the items. The approach is used to apply fluids, gases or particles while maintaining precise control of the workpiece.
[H10P 72/0416] with the semiconductor substrates being dipped in baths or vessels -- H10P 72/0416 is a CPC subclass under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Processes or Apparatus for the Manufacture or Treatment of Devices Covered by Class, specifically handling or holding of wafers, substrates or devices. It classifies steps where semiconductor substrates are dipped in baths or vessels during manufacturing, covering immersion-type treatments such as cleaning, etching, or coating.
[H10P 72/0418] for etching -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/0418 covers methods and equipment for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices while they undergo an etching step. It falls under the broader category of generic processes for semiconductor device manufacture, specifically the sub-group dealing with wafer handling during treatment. This classification is used by the EPO and USPTO to index patents related to etching-related handling operations.
[H10P 72/0421] for drying etching -- This CPC subclass deals with methods and apparatus that dry semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices immediately after an etching step while the pieces are being handled or held during manufacturing. It covers techniques for removing moisture or volatile residues to ready the material for the next processing stage.
[H10P 72/0422] for wet etching -- H10P 72/0422 covers wet-etching steps applied while wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices are being handled or held during manufacturing or treatment. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for semiconductor device processes, specifically the generic processes or apparatus for handling devices, and defines the chemical removal of material using liquid etchants in such handling operations.
[H10P 72/0424] using mainly spraying means, e.g. nozzles -- The CPC entry H10P 72/0424 covers techniques for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing that rely chiefly on spraying means such as nozzles. It includes processes like cleaning, coating or etching where a spray stream provides the primary means of contact or transport.
[H10P 72/0426] with the semiconductor substrates being dipped in baths or vessels -- The code H10P 72/0426 pertains to processes where semiconductor substrates are immersed in baths or vessels during manufacturing or treatment, covering the handling or holding of wafers while they are dipped for cleaning, etching, deposition or similar steps.
[H10P 72/0428] Apparatus for mechanical treatment or grinding or cutting -- H10P 72/0428 covers equipment used to mechanically treat semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing, specifically apparatus that grinds, cuts or otherwise mechanically processes these items to shape, thin or separate them. The classification applies to tools and machines designed for handling, holding and precision machining of semiconductor materials in the course of fabrication or post-fabrication treatment.
[H10P 72/0431] Apparatus for thermal treatment -- H10P 72/0431 covers apparatus that thermally treats wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices while they are being handled or held during manufacturing, such as furnaces, rapid-thermal processors, annealing chambers or other heating units designed to apply controlled temperature cycles to the items being processed. This subclass falls under generic processes for the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor devices.
[H10P 72/0432] mainly by conduction -- H10P 72/0432 designates processes for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture or treatment where the primary action is carried out by electrical conduction, such as conductive heating, current-driven positioning or charge-based transport.
[H10P 72/0434] mainly by convection -- The H10P 72/0434 subclass refers to handling or holding of semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment where the temperature control is achieved mainly by convection. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for device manufacture or treatment.
[H10P 72/0436] mainly by radiation -- The entry H10P 72/0436 covers handling or holding of semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment when the manipulation is performed mainly by radiation, such as exposure to laser, UV or other energetic beams. It pertains to processes that use radiation to position, transport, align or otherwise manage the items without direct mechanical contact.
[H10P 72/0438] Apparatus for making assemblies not otherwise provided for, e.g. package constructions -- H10P 72/0438 covers equipment used to assemble semiconductor components in ways not covered by more specific categories, such as custom packaging or unconventional module constructions. The apparatus typically handles or holds wafers, substrates or devices during the manufacturing or treatment steps, enabling the creation of novel or bespoke assemblies.
[H10P 72/0441] Apparatus for sealing, encapsulating, glassing, decapsulating or the like -- H10P 72/0441 covers equipment used to seal, encapsulate, glass or decapsulate semiconductor wafers, substrates or other devices during processing. The apparatus assists in handling wafers or devices by accurately placing them on the substrate to facilitate further processing.
[H10P 72/0444] Apparatus for wiring semiconductor or solid-state device -- This CPC sub-class covers machines and tools that create electrical interconnections on semiconductor or solid-state devices, including wire-bonding, bumping or other wiring techniques used while the wafer or chip is handled or held during processing.
[H10P 72/0446] Apparatus for mounting on conductive members, e.g. leadframes or conductors on insulating substrates -- This CPC subclass concerns equipment used to mount semiconductor elements onto conductive members such as leadframes or conductors printed on insulating substrates during processing. [H10P 72/0448] Apparatus for applying a liquid, a resin, an ink or the like -- This CPC subclass defines equipment that deposits liquids, resins, inks or comparable materials onto semiconductor wafers or other substrates during processing, such as spray heads, dispensing nozzles, coating tools or similar applicators. It is situated under the broader category of handling or holding wafers in generic semiconductor manufacturing and treatment operations.
[H10P 72/0451] Apparatus for manufacturing or treating in a plurality of work-stations -- This CPC subclass defines equipment that moves or holds semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices through several manufacturing or treatment stations within a single apparatus. The system integrates multiple processing steps such as cleaning, deposition, etching or inspection, allowing sequential or parallel handling of the workpieces.
[H10P 72/0452] characterised by the layout of the process chambers -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/0452 concerns semiconductor manufacturing processes in which the arrangement of the process chambers is a distinguishing feature. It falls under handling or holding of wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture, within the broader group of generic processes or apparatus for treating semiconductor devices.
[H10P 72/0454] surrounding a central transfer chamber -- The H10P 72/0454 subclass covers arrangements that enclose a central transfer chamber used in handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices. It relates to peripheral structures that provide a controlled environment, such as gas flow or isolation, around the chamber during manufacturing or treatment steps.
[H10P 72/0456] in-line arrangement -- The H10P 72/0456 subclass defines in-line arrangements for handling wafers, substrates or devices, where transport, positioning, and processing steps are integrated into a continuous production line. Such configurations keep the workpiece moving through successive operations without manual transfer, improving throughput and alignment precision in semiconductor manufacturing.
[H10P 72/0458] vertical arrangement -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/0458 concerns the vertical arrangement of tools, fixtures or carriers that handle or hold semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment. It applies to setups where the workpiece is oriented vertically relative to processing equipment, enabling gravity-assisted loading, transport or alignment.
[H10P 72/0461] characterised by the presence of two or more transfer chambers -- This CPC subclass is located under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices covered by class, specifically handling or holding of wafers, substrates or devices. It designates equipment whose operation involves two or more transfer chambers to move wafers or devices during manufacturing or treatment.
[H10P 72/0462] characterised by the construction of the processing chambers, e.g. modular processing chambers -- H10P 72/0462 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that involve handling or holding wafers, substrates or devices, specifically focusing on the design of the processing chambers used. The entry is defined by features such as modular or otherwise specially constructed chambers that facilitate the treatment or fabrication of the devices. It falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic processes for device manufacture.
[H10P 72/0464] characterised by the construction of the transfer chamber -- H10P 72/0464 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps involving the handling or holding of wafers or devices, with particular emphasis on the design of the transfer chamber used to move substrates between process stations. The classification falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic processes or apparatus for manufacture or treatment of devices, focusing on equipment that protects and transports wafers during processing.
[H10P 72/0466] characterised by the construction of the load-lock chamber -- The subclass covers semiconductor fabrication equipment where the load-lock chamber's construction is the defining feature, detailing how the chamber isolates and transfers wafers or substrates into a processing environment. [H10P 72/0471] comprising at least one ion or electron beam chamber -- This CPC subclass covers apparatus used to handle or hold semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during processing that includes at least one ion- or electron-beam chamber, such as equipment for ion implantation or electron-beam treatment within the manufacturing line.

It relates to processes or apparatus used in the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor devices, specifically for polishing steps.
[H10P 72/0474] comprising at least one lithography chamber -- This CPC subclass (H10P 72/0474) falls under handling or holding of wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing, specifically for processes that incorporate at least one lithography chamber. It covers equipment or arrangements that secure wafers while they undergo lithographic exposure, enabling precise pattern transfer in semiconductor fabrication.
[H10P 72/0476] comprising at least one plating chamber -- H10P 72/0476 covers equipment or processes used to handle or hold semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing that incorporate at least one plating chamber, enabling deposition or plating operations as part of the wafer-handling sequence.
[H10P 72/0478] the substrates being processed being not semiconductor wafers, e.g. leadframes or chips -- This CPC subclass addresses handling or holding steps applied to substrates that are not semiconductor wafers, such as leadframes or individual chips, during their manufacture or treatment. It falls under the hierarchy for generic processes or apparatus for semiconductor devices, specifically the handling of wafers, substrates or devices.
[H10P 72/06] Apparatus for monitoring, sorting, marking, testing or measuring -- H10P 72/06 covers equipment used to monitor, sort, mark, test or measure semiconductor wafers and devices while they are being handled during manufacturing or treatment. Such apparatus may include optical inspection stations, electrical test probes, sorting robots or marking tools that operate on wafers, substrates or finished chips.
[H10P 72/0602] Temperature monitoring -- H10P 72/0602 relates to temperature monitoring systems used while handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment steps. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for electricity-based semiconductor devices and generic processes, focusing on sensing or controlling temperature to maintain process stability and product quality.
[H10P 72/0604] Process monitoring, e.g. flow or thickness monitoring -- The subclass H10P 72/0604 relates to methods and apparatus for monitoring manufacturing processes-such as measuring flow rates or film thickness-while wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices are being handled or held during production or treatment.
[H10P 72/0606] Position monitoring, e.g. misposition detection or presence detection -- This CPC subclass addresses techniques for monitoring the position of wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices during handling or processing, including methods for detecting mis-positioning or confirming presence. It covers sensors, optical, mechanical or electronic systems that provide real-time feedback to ensure proper alignment and prevent damage.
[H10P 72/0608] of substrates stored in a container, a magazine, a carrier, a boat or the like -- H10P 72/0608 addresses the handling or holding of semiconductor substrates that are stored in containers such as magazines, carriers, or boats during their manufacture or processing. The classification falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices, focusing on storage and transport solutions for wafers and similar items.
[H10P 72/0611] Sorting devices -- H10P 72/0611 covers sorting devices used to organize wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices during manufacturing or treatment. These machines detect and separate items by size, orientation or other characteristics to improve production flow and yield.
[H10P 72/0612] Production flow monitoring, e.g. for increasing throughput -- The H10P 72/0612 entry covers systems that monitor the production flow of semiconductor wafers or devices during manufacturing to increase throughput, including sensors, data acquisition units and control software integrated with handling or holding equipment.
[H10P 72/0614] Marking devices -- The CPC code H10P 72/0614 covers devices that apply permanent or temporary marks to semiconductor wafers, substrates or individual devices during processing. Such marking devices may use laser, ink-jet, electron-beam or mechanical methods to create identifiers, alignment marks or test patterns. They are employed in handling or holding steps within semiconductor manufacturing.

This subclass falls under generic handling or holding of devices in semiconductor production.

It covers the use of carriers specially adapted for this purpose, such as front-opening unified pods (FOUPs) that protect and transport wafers during fabrication steps.

The design typically employs rod-shaped elements to hold the substrates in place while they are moved or processed.

[H10P 72/70] for supporting or gripping -- Group H10P72/70 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10P72/72 , H10P72/74 and H10P72/7448 - H10P72/745 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 72/72] using electrostatic chucks -- Group H10P72/72 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10P72/70 . Groups H10P72/70 and H10P72/72 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

[H10P 72/743] used as a support during manufacture of interconnect decals or build up layers -- This subclass covers temporary support structures that hold a wafer or substrate while interconnect decals or multilayer build-up films are applied during semiconductor device fabrication. [H10P 72/7434] used in a transfer process involving at least two transfer steps, i.e. including an intermediate handle substrate -- The CPC code H10P 72/7434 applies to processes for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices that employ a multi-step transfer, using an intermediate handle substrate to move the material from one stage to another during manufacture or treatment. It defines methods where at least two transfer actions are involved.
[H10P 72/7436] used to support a device or a wafer when forming electrical connections thereto -- The code H10P 72/7436 covers fixtures, carriers or other supports that hold a semiconductor wafer or device in place while electrical connections are formed, ensuring stable positioning and protection during the interconnect process.
