[H10P 72/7438] with parts of the auxiliary support remaining in the finished device -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/7438 concerns handling or holding of semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture or treatment when parts of the auxiliary support remain attached to the finished product, such as retained frames, carriers or fixtures that become a permanent element of the device.
[H10P 72/744] Details of chemical or physical process used for separating the auxiliary support from a device or a wafer -- H10P 72/744 pertains to the specific chemical or physical steps employed to detach an auxiliary support from a semiconductor device or wafer during manufacturing, falling under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and handling or holding of wafers.
[H10P 72/7442] Separation by peeling -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/7442 covers techniques for separating wafers, substrates or semiconductor devices from one another by means of peeling. It relates to handling or holding steps during manufacture or treatment where a device is detached from a larger wafer or carrier using a controlled peel action.
[H10P 72/7444] using a peeling wedge, a knife or a bar -- This CPC subclass covers methods for handling semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices in which a peeling wedge, a knife or a bar is used to separate, position or remove the material during manufacturing or treatment steps.
[H10P 72/7446] using a peeling wheel -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10P 72/7446 pertains to the use of a peeling wheel in the handling or holding of wafers, substrates, or devices during their manufacture or treatment. This method is part of the broader category of generic processes and apparatus for manufacturing semiconductor devices.
[H10P 72/7448] the bond interface between the auxiliary support and the wafer comprising two or more, e.g. multilayer adhesive or adhesive and release layer -- This CPC subclass deals with the bond interface between an auxiliary support and a semiconductor wafer, specifically where the interface consists of two or more layers such as a multilayer adhesive or a combination of adhesive and release layer. It is applied to processes for handling or holding wafers during fabrication or treatment.
[H10P 72/745] the bond interface between the auxiliary support and the wafer comprises three or more layers -- H10P 72/745 is a CPC subclass within the handling and holding of wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment. It specifies that the bond interface linking an auxiliary support to the wafer is composed of three or more distinct layers, covering multi-layer adhesion structures used in semiconductor processing.
[H10P 72/76] using mechanical means, e.g. clamps or pinches -- H10P 72/76 covers mechanical methods for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment, such as using clamps, pinches, fixtures or other gripping tools to position, transport or secure the items without chemical or thermal processes.
[H10P 72/7602] the wafers being placed on a robot blade or gripped by a gripper for conveyance -- H10P 72/7602 covers methods for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing, specifically when the wafers are placed on a robot blade or secured by a gripper for conveyance. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of electricity - semiconductor devices - generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices.
[H10P 72/7604] the wafers being placed on a susceptor, stage or support -- The classification H10P 72/7604 covers methods and apparatus where semiconductor wafers are positioned onto a susceptor, stage or other support during manufacturing or treatment, facilitating handling, heating or processing of the devices.
[H10P 72/7606] characterised by edge clamping, e.g. clamping ring -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/7606 covers methods and apparatuses for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices in which the workpiece is secured by clamping at its edges, typically using a clamping ring that engages the periphery rather than the surface. This approach is used during manufacturing or treatment steps to stabilize the item without contacting active areas.
[H10P 72/7608] characterised by a plurality of separate clamping members, e.g. clamping fingers -- The H10P 72/7608 subclass concerns handling or holding of semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture using a plurality of separate clamping members such as individual clamping fingers, which provide localized support and adjustable pressure for precise positioning.
[H10P 72/7611] characterised by edge profile or support profile -- This CPC subclass covers methods or apparatuses for handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture or treatment that are defined by a particular edge profile or support profile. The profile is shaped to secure, align or protect the wafer's edges while it undergoes processes such as cleaning, etching, deposition or inspection.
[H10P 72/7612] characterised by lifting arrangements, e.g. lift pins -- This subclass covers equipment and processes for handling semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices where lifting mechanisms such as lift pins are employed to raise or support the items during manufacturing or treatment. It falls under generic processes for the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor devices within the electricity field.
[H10P 72/7614] characterised by a plurality of individual support members, e.g. support posts or protrusions -- H10P 72/7614 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system refers to methods and apparatus for handling or holding wafers, substrates, or devices during their manufacture or treatment, specifically focusing on techniques characterized by using multiple individual support members such as posts or protrusions.
[H10P 72/7616] characterised by a coating, a hardness or a material -- H10P 72/7616 defines handling or holding of semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture where the tool or surface is distinguished by a specific coating, hardness level or material composition, improving grip, protection or process reliability. It sits under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes for device manufacture.
[H10P 72/7618] characterised by a movable susceptor, stage or support, others than those only rotating on their own vertical axis, e.g. susceptors on a rotating carrousel -- The subclass covers wafer or device handling where the susceptor, stage or support moves in ways other than simple rotation about a vertical axis, such as carriers that translate, tilt, or are placed on a rotating carousel. It includes apparatus with movable supports that provide additional degrees of motion during manufacturing or treatment of semiconductor devices.
[H10P 72/7621] characterised by supporting two or more semiconductor substrates -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/7621 relates to equipment or methods used in the handling or holding of semiconductor wafers that are designed to support two or more substrates at the same time during manufacturing or treatment processes. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices covered by class.
[H10P 72/7622] characterised by supporting substrates others than wafers, e.g. chips -- H10P 72/7622 classifies processes or apparatus for handling semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices where the supporting substrate is not a full wafer but individual chips or other non-wafer supports. This sub-category relates to the manipulation, transport or positioning of such pieces during manufacturing or treatment steps. It falls under the CPC group for generic semiconductor device processing within the electricity section.
[H10P 72/7624] characterised by the mechanical construction of the susceptor, stage or support -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/7624 covers semiconductor manufacturing equipment where the mechanical design of the susceptor, stage or support for wafers, substrates or devices is the defining feature. It addresses how these holders are constructed to secure and position the items during processes such as deposition, annealing or etching.
[H10P 72/7626] characterised by the construction of the shaft -- The CPC subclass H10P 72/7626 covers handling or holding of semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment where the distinguishing feature is the construction of the shaft used in the handling mechanism.
[H10P 72/78] using vacuum or suction, e.g. Bernoulli chucks -- The CPC entry H10P 72/78 covers methods of handling or holding semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing by applying vacuum or suction, for example Bernoulli chucks. It is placed in the hierarchy electricity, semiconductor devices, generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices covered by class, specifically the sub-category for wafer handling.
[H10P 74/00] Testing or measuring during manufacture or treatment of wafers, substrates or devices -- H10P 74/00 covers methods for testing or measuring semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices while they are being manufactured or treated. It falls under the electricity > semiconductor devices > generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices covered by class hierarchy. This classification is used in the Cooperative Patent Classification system shared by the EPO and USPTO.
[H10P 74/20] characterised by the properties tested or measured, e.g. structural or electrical properties -- H10P 74/20 covers processes or apparatus for testing or measuring semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture that are defined by the specific properties being examined, such as structural or electrical characteristics.
[H10P 74/203] Structural properties, e.g. testing or measuring thicknesses, line widths, warpage, bond strengths or physical defects -- H10P 74/203 pertains to techniques for assessing structural properties of semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment, including measurement of thickness, line width, warpage, bond strength and detection of physical defects.
[H10P 74/207] Electrical properties, e.g. testing or measuring of resistance, deep levels or capacitance-voltage characteristics -- H10P 74/207 covers methods and apparatus for evaluating the electrical properties of semiconductor wafers or devices during manufacturing, including resistance, deep-level, and capacitance-voltage measurements used to monitor and control process quality.
[H10P 74/23] characterised by multiple measurements, corrections, marking or sorting processes -- H10P 74/23 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps where the wafer or device undergoes several measurement operations followed by corrective actions, marking or sorting. The code applies to processes that integrate multiple test-and-adjust cycles to ensure device quality during production.
[H10P 74/232] comprising connection or disconnection of parts of a device in response to a measurement -- The CPC subclass H10P 74/232 addresses methods or apparatuses used in semiconductor manufacturing that automatically connect or disconnect portions of a wafer, substrate or device in response to a measured parameter. It falls under electricity-related semiconductor devices, specifically testing or measuring steps that trigger inter-part connections or disconnections during production.
[H10P 74/235] comprising optical enhancement of defects or not-directly-visible states -- H10P 74/235 covers methods and apparatus that use optical techniques to enhance the visibility of defects or otherwise invisible states in semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacturing or treatment, enabling more accurate testing and measurement of such hidden features. It is a sub-group of testing or measuring processes for semiconductor devices.
[H10P 74/238] comprising acting in response to an ongoing measurement without interruption of processing, e.g. endpoint detection or in-situ thickness measurement -- H10P 74/238 refers to semiconductor manufacturing techniques that use continuous, in-situ measurements to automatically adjust processing, for example endpoint detection or real-time thickness monitoring, without pausing the production line.
[H10P 74/27] Structural arrangements therefor -- H10P 74/27 covers the design and layout of fixtures, probes, carriers, test structures and other physical configurations used to hold, align, or connect semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during in-process testing or measurement. It addresses how these structural arrangements are organized to enable reliable electrical, optical or dimensional inspection while the device is being manufactured or treated.
[H10P 74/273] Interconnections for measuring or testing, e.g. probe pads -- H10P 74/273 refers to the interconnections employed for testing or measuring semiconductor wafers, substrates or devices during manufacture or treatment, such as probe pads that provide temporary electrical contact. It falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and generic processes for their production.
[H10P 74/277] Circuits for electrically characterising or monitoring manufacturing processes, e.g. circuits in tested chips or circuits in testing wafers -- H10P 74/277 covers circuits integrated into semiconductor chips or test wafers that electrically characterise or monitor manufacturing steps, enabling real-time measurement of parameters during wafer or device processing. These circuits provide on-line testing, diagnostics, or process control within the production line.
[H10P 76/00] Manufacture or treatment of masks on semiconductor bodies, e.g. by lithography or photolithography -- H10P 76/00 pertains to processes for producing and applying masks on semiconductor wafers, typically using lithographic or photolithographic methods to transfer circuit patterns. It includes steps such as mask fabrication, alignment, exposure and subsequent treatment of the semiconductor body.
[H10P 76/20] of masks comprising organic materials -- H10P 76/20 covers the manufacture and treatment of semiconductor masks that contain organic materials, such as photoresist or polymer layers, used in lithographic or photolithographic steps on semiconductor wafers. The entry relates to processes for preparing, applying, and developing these organic-based masks in device fabrication.
[H10P 76/202] for lift-off processes -- H10P 76/202 pertains to lift-off processes used in the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor masks, such as those applied in lithography or photolithography. The method involves depositing a sacrificial layer and then removing it together with unwanted material, leaving the desired pattern on the device.
[H10P 76/204] of organic photoresist masks -- H10P 76/204 refers to the use of organic photoresist masks in semiconductor fabrication, covering processes and apparatus for applying, patterning and treating organic photoresist layers on semiconductor bodies, such as lithography or photolithography steps. This subclass falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for manufacturing or treating devices covered by the class.
[H10P 76/2041] Photolithographic processes -- H10P 76/2041 refers to photolithographic processes employed to form or alter masks on semiconductor bodies, encompassing steps such as coating, exposure, development and pattern transfer. It is situated within the electricity domain, under semiconductor devices and generic manufacturing or treatment methods for such devices.
[H10P 76/2042] using lasers -- H10P 76/2042 pertains to the use of laser sources in the production or modification of photomasks applied to semiconductor wafers, such as during lithography or photolithography steps. It falls under the hierarchy Electricity - Semiconductor Devices - Generic Processes or Apparatus for the Manufacture or Treatment of Devices Covered by Class.
[H10P 76/2043] using an anti-reflective coating -- H10P 76/2043 covers the application of anti-reflective coatings to photomasks or other masking layers used in semiconductor lithography. The coating reduces unwanted reflections during exposure, improving pattern fidelity on the wafer.
[H10P 76/2045] Electron beam lithography processes -- This CPC subclass covers methods using a focused electron beam to pattern photoresist masks on semiconductor wafers, enabling high-resolution lithography for device fabrication. It includes beam exposure, control of dose and focus, and subsequent development steps for mask creation.
[H10P 76/2047] X-ray beam lithography processes -- H10P 76/2047 relates to X-ray beam lithography processes used to pattern semiconductor devices, particularly masks, by exposing a resist with an X-ray beam to define features. It falls under manufacturing or treatment of masks on semiconductor bodies within the electricity-semiconductor devices hierarchy.
[H10P 76/2049] Ion beam lithography processes -- Ion beam lithography processes involve using focused ion beams to pattern masks directly on semiconductor wafers, enabling high-resolution feature definition without traditional photoresist steps. This method is used in the manufacture or treatment of semiconductor devices where masks are created by ion implantation or sputtering, fitting within the broader class of semiconductor mask fabrication.
[H10P 76/40] of masks comprising inorganic materials -- H10P 76/40 refers to processes for manufacturing or treating semiconductor masks that contain inorganic materials, such as those used in lithographic or photolithographic steps during device fabrication. It sits under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for the manufacture or treatment of devices covered by the class.
[H10P 76/403] for lift-off processes -- The H10P 76/403 subclass covers lift-off processes used in semiconductor mask fabrication, where a patterned sacrificial layer is removed after material deposition to leave the desired pattern on the chip. It belongs to the CPC group for generic processes and apparatus for making or treating masks on semiconductor bodies, typically employed with lithography or photolithography steps.
[H10P 76/405] characterised by their composition, e.g. multilayer masks -- This subclass covers semiconductor lithographic masks whose defining feature is the material composition, including multilayer structures, used in processes such as photolithography for patterning semiconductor wafers. It pertains to the manufacture or treatment of masks on semiconductor bodies within the CPC section H10P.
[H10P 76/408] characterised by their sizes, orientations, dispositions, behaviours or shapes -- The CPC subclass H10P 76/408 pertains to methods or equipment for manufacturing or treating semiconductor masks-such as those used in lithography-where the masks are distinguished by their size, orientation, layout, behavior or shape. It classifies processes that rely on these geometric or functional characteristics to define how the mask is applied to a semiconductor device.
[H10P 76/4083] characterised by their behaviours during the lithography processes, e.g. soluble masks or redeposited masks -- H10P 76/4083 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system pertains to the specific behaviors of masks used during semiconductor manufacturing, particularly focusing on how these masks interact with lithographic processes, such as their solubility or redeposition characteristics.
[H10P 76/4085] characterised by the processes involved to create the masks -- The subclass H10P 76/4085 covers methods and steps for producing the photomasks or lithographic masks used to pattern semiconductor bodies, including mask design, material preparation, exposure, development, etching and inspection. It sits within the broader CPC groups for electricity, semiconductor devices and generic processes for manufacturing or treating those devices.
[H10P 76/4088] Processes for improving the resolution of the masks -- This CPC subclass covers methods that increase the resolution of photomasks used in semiconductor lithography, such as techniques to sharpen pattern edges, reduce line-width variation, and enhance imaging fidelity. It is part of the hierarchy electricity → semiconductor devices → generic processes or apparatus for manufacture or treatment of devices covered by class, focusing on mask fabrication.
[H10P 90/00] Preparation of wafers not covered by a single main group of this subclass, e.g. wafer reinforcement -- This group covers multistep processes for the preparation of wafers before the subsequent manufacture of semiconductor devices or solid-state devices therein or thereon. This group does not cover the single-crystal growth of semiconductor ingots, which is covered by subclass C30B . Group H10P90/00 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10P90/19 , H10P90/1902 , H10P90/1904 , H10P90/1906 , H10P90/21 - H10P90/212 , H10P90/22 , H10P90/24 , H10P56/00 and H10P10/12 . It addresses generic processes for wafer preparation that fall outside the narrower main groups of the subclass.

It applies to wafer-reinforcement processes that do not fall under any other specific main group within the generic manufacturing subclass for semiconductor devices.
[H10P 90/129] by polishing -- Polishing is a mechanical or chemical-mechanical process that smooths and planarizes semiconductor wafers, often after reinforcement or thinning, to remove surface defects and achieve a uniform surface suitable for further device fabrication.
[H10P 90/14] by setting crystal orientation -- H10P 90/14 pertains to semiconductor manufacturing processes that involve setting the crystal orientation of wafers. It covers methods for preparing wafers-particularly those not classified elsewhere, such as wafer reinforcement-by controlling the crystallographic direction to achieve desired electrical properties. This subclass falls under generic processes for making or treating semiconductor devices within the broader electricity and semiconductor device categories.
[H10P 90/15] by making porous regions on the surface -- H10P 90/15 covers methods for creating porous zones on the surface of semiconductor wafers, typically to reinforce the wafer or modify its properties. It falls under the electricity section, semiconductor devices subclass, and the generic processes for manufacturing or treating devices covered by the class. The classification is used when the porous region technique does not fit any more specific main group.
[H10P 90/16] by reclaiming or re-processing -- H10P 90/16 is a CPC subclass for semiconductor device manufacturing that covers processes for preparing wafers that do not fall under a single main group, specifically methods that involve reclaiming or re-processing used wafers, such as reinforcement or recycling steps to restore wafer quality.
[H10P 90/18] by shaping -- H10P 90/18 covers shaping operations applied to semiconductor wafers during their preparation, such as cutting, grinding, polishing or other mechanical forms of size or surface adjustment that are not classified elsewhere in the subclass. It is used for processes that modify the wafer's shape after bulk formation but before device fabrication.

Group H10P95/90 is also impacted by reclassification into group H10P30/28 . Groups H10P95/00 , H10P95/90 and H10P30/28 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 95/902] for the formation of PN junctions without addition of impurities -- The entry covers generic semiconductor manufacturing methods that create p-n junctions by intrinsic techniques rather than introducing dopant impurities, such as diffusion-less processes or crystal-growth strategies that establish the junction through controlled material composition.
[H10P 95/904] of Group III-V semiconductors -- Group H10P95/904 is impacted by reclassification into group H10P30/28 . Groups H10P95/904 and H10P30/28 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 95/906] for altering the shape of semiconductors, e.g. smoothing the surface -- H10P 95/906 covers methods that change the geometry of semiconductor components, for example by smoothing or otherwise reshaping their surfaces, as part of generic manufacturing or treatment processes not classified elsewhere. This entry falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic processes for device fabrication.
[H10P 95/92] Formation of n- or p-type semiconductors, e.g. doping of graphene -- Group H10P95/92 is impacted by reclassification into group H10P32/173 . Groups H10P95/92 and H10P32/173 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10P 95/94] Hydrogenation or deuterisation, e.g. using atomic hydrogen from a plasma -- H10P 95/94 covers methods for hydrogenating or deuterising semiconductor components, such as applying atomic hydrogen or deuterium generated in a plasma to modify material properties. These treatments are used to passivate surfaces, alter electrical characteristics, or improve device performance and are not covered by more specific process groups in the subclass.
[H10W] GENERIC PACKAGES, INTERCONNECTIONS, CONNECTORS OR OTHER CONSTRUCTIONAL DETAILS OF DEVICES COVERED BY CLASS -- This subclass covers : packages of devices and parts of such packages; interconnections of devices in chips, wafers, substrates or packages; connectors of devices in packages; other constructional details of devices in chips, wafers, substrates or packages, e.g. isolation regions between components of integrated devices; detachable holders for supporting packaged chips in operation; the manufacture or treatment of aspects (a)-(e); when aspects (a)-(e) are (1) applicable to devices covered by subclass H10B ; (2) applicable to devices covered by subclass H10D , except for semiconductor bodies or electrodes thereof, which are covered by subgroups H10D62/00 or H10D64/00 ; or (3) generically applicable to devices covered by subclasses H10B , H10D , H10F , H10H , H10K or H10N . In this subclass, the periodic system used is the I to VIII group system indicated in the Periodic Table under Note (3) of section C .
[H10W 10/00] Constructional details of integrated devices in chips, wafers or substrates -- H10W 10/00 covers the constructional details of integrated semiconductor devices, including the layout and interconnection structures on chips, wafers or other substrates. It addresses how individual components are packaged, connected and built into the overall device architecture within the broader class of generic packages and connectors for semiconductor devices.
[H10W 10/01] Manufacture or treatment -- The H10W 10/01 entry concerns processes for manufacturing or treating the constructional details of integrated devices such as chips, wafers or substrates, including generic packages, interconnections and connectors. It covers methods that modify or finish these structures to achieve functional or reliability requirements.
[H10W 10/011] of isolation regions comprising dielectric materials -- H10W 10/011 covers constructional details of semiconductor devices that define isolation regions formed from dielectric materials, such as the insulating zones separating active components on chips, wafers or substrates. It falls under the generic package and interconnection category of electric semiconductor devices.
[H10W 10/012] using local oxidation of silicon [LOCOS] -- The H10W 10/012 entry covers the use of local oxidation of silicon (LOCOS) as a technique for forming insulating regions on silicon wafers or substrates in integrated semiconductor devices. It relates to constructional details that define how devices are isolated and structured within generic packages, interconnections and other device constructions.
[H10W 10/0121] in regions recessed from the surface, e.g. in trenches or grooves -- H10W 10/0121 covers constructional details of semiconductor devices in which components or interconnections are formed in regions recessed below the surface, such as trenches or grooves on chips, wafers or substrates. This subclass falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic packages, interconnections and connectors.
[H10W 10/0123] using auxiliary pillars in the regions -- H10W 10/0123 relates to the use of auxiliary pillars in specific regions of integrated semiconductor devices such as chips, wafers or substrates to provide mechanical support, stress relief or alignment during manufacturing. The pillars are incorporated as constructional details of generic packages, interconnections or connectors, enhancing device stability and reliability.
[H10W 10/0124] the regions having non-rectangular shapes, e.g. rounded  H10W10/0123  takes precedence -- H10W 10/0124 covers constructional details of integrated semiconductor devices that define regions with non-rectangular shapes, such as rounded or curved areas, on chips, wafers or substrates. It specifies how these shaped regions are formed or used within the device layout. The broader subclass H10W 10/0123 on rectangular regions takes precedence when applicable.
[H10W 10/0125] comprising introducing electrical impurities in local oxidation regions, e.g. to alter LOCOS oxide growth characteristics -- H10W 10/0125 covers processes that add electrical impurities into locally oxidized regions of a semiconductor to modify the growth characteristics of the LOCOS oxide layer. The technique is used to control oxide thickness and profile during device fabrication, affecting isolation and overall device performance.
[H10W 10/0126] introducing electrical active impurities in local oxidation regions to create channel stoppers -- The CPC subclass H10W 10/0126 covers techniques for introducing electrically active impurities into locally oxidised regions of semiconductor structures in order to create channel stoppers that restrict current flow. It pertains to the constructional details of integrated devices such as chips, wafers or substrates, where selective oxidation combined with dopant implantation or diffusion defines isolated regions that improve device performance and reliability.
[H10W 10/0127] using both n-type and p-type impurities, e.g. for isolation of complementary doped regions -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry H10W 10/0127 pertains to the constructional details of integrated devices in chips, wafers, or substrates, specifically addressing techniques that involve using both n-type and p-type impurities for tasks such as isolating complementary doped regions.
[H10W 10/0128] comprising multiple local oxidation process steps -- H10W 10/0128 relates to semiconductor device fabrication that employs several local oxidation steps to form isolation regions or other structural features on chips, wafers or substrates. It is situated in the CPC class for constructional details of integrated devices.
[H10W 10/014] using trench refilling with dielectric materials, e.g. shallow trench isolations -- H10W 10/014 addresses the construction of semiconductor devices by refilling etched trenches with dielectric materials, such as shallow trench isolation, to provide electrical isolation between active regions. It falls under generic package and interconnection details for integrated chips, wafers or substrates.
[H10W 10/0142] the dielectric materials being chemical transformed from non-dielectric materials -- This CPC subgroup covers methods and structures in which non-dielectric substances are chemically altered to become dielectric materials used within the constructional details of integrated semiconductor devices such as chips, wafers or substrates. It relates to the formation of insulating layers in generic packages and interconnections.
[H10W 10/0143] comprising concurrently refilling multiple trenches having different shapes or dimensions -- The CPC H10W 10/0143 class covers methods that refill multiple trenches of different shapes or dimensions at the same time in semiconductor substrates such as chips, wafers, or other integrated-device constructions.
[H10W 10/0145] of trenches having shapes other than rectangular or V-shape  H10W10/0143  takes precedence -- H10W 10/0145 is a CPC subclass that covers constructional details of integrated semiconductor devices, specifically trenches whose cross-sectional shape is neither rectangular nor V-shaped. It falls under Electricity → Semiconductor devices → Generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices covered by class. The broader subclass H10W 10/0143, addressing trenches of any shape, takes precedence over this narrower entry.
[H10W 10/0147] the shapes being altered by a local oxidation of silicon process, e.g. trench corner rounding by LOCOS -- H10W 10/0147 covers constructional details of semiconductor devices where the geometry of silicon structures is modified by locally oxidising the material, for example using the LOCOS (local oxidation of silicon) technique to round trench corners or reshape features on chips, wafers or substrates.
[H10W 10/0148] comprising introducing impurities in side walls or bottom walls of trenches, e.g. for forming channel stoppers -- The H10W 10/0148 entry covers a technique for introducing dopant impurities into the sidewalls or bottom of etched trenches in semiconductor substrates, typically to create channel-stopping regions that confine current flow. This method is used in the construction of integrated devices such as chips and wafers to improve device isolation.
[H10W 10/018] using selective deposition of crystalline silicon, e.g. using epitaxial growth of silicon -- The subclass covers methods that deposit crystalline silicon only in selected areas of a chip, wafer or substrate, typically by epitaxial growth, to form or modify semiconductor structures. It relates to constructional details of integrated devices where selective silicon deposition defines device regions.
[H10W 10/019] using epitaxial passivated integrated circuit [EPIC] processes -- H10W 10/019 covers the use of epitaxial passivated integrated circuit (EPIC) processes in constructing semiconductor devices, detailing how epitaxial layers are employed to passivate and improve the performance of chips, wafers or substrates. The classification falls under constructional details of integrated devices.
[H10W 10/021] of air gaps -- H10W 10/021 covers the incorporation of air gaps within integrated semiconductor structures-chips, wafers or substrates-where the voids are used as dielectric isolation, to lower parasitic capacitance or aid thermal management. The code applies to design or manufacturing features that create and maintain such gaps in device construction.
[H10W 10/031] of isolation regions comprising PN junctions -- H10W 10/031 designates constructional details of integrated semiconductor devices that employ isolation regions formed by pn junctions; it covers structures and methods for creating electrically isolated areas on chips, wafers or substrates using dopant diffusion to establish pn-junction barriers.
[H10W 10/041] of isolation regions comprising polycrystalline semiconductor materials -- This subclass covers isolation regions made from polycrystalline semiconductor material that are incorporated into integrated chips, wafers or substrates to electrically separate circuit elements. It relates to the constructional details of semiconductor devices, focusing on the design, formation and layout of these polycrystalline isolation zones within the device architecture.
[H10W 10/051] of isolation region based on field-effect -- The H10W 10/051 subclass covers constructional details of integrated semiconductor devices where isolation regions are formed by field-effect mechanisms, such as using depletion or reverse-biased junctions to electrically separate circuit areas on chips, wafers or substrates. It is part of the broader class for generic packages, interconnections, connectors and other constructional details of devices.
[H10W 10/061] using SOI processes together with lateral isolation, e.g. combinations of SOI and shallow trench isolations -- This subclass concerns integrated semiconductor devices built on silicon-on-insulator substrates that employ lateral isolation methods, such as combining SOI with shallow trench isolation, to define circuit elements. It addresses the combined use of SOI process steps and lateral isolation structures within the generic package and interconnection group.
[H10W 10/10] Isolation regions comprising dielectric materials -- The H10W 10/10 subclass deals with isolation regions formed from dielectric materials that separate functional areas on integrated circuits, chips, wafers or substrates. It includes the design and implementation of such dielectric isolation structures within semiconductor device packages and interconnections.
[H10W 10/13] formed using local oxidation of silicon [LOCOS], e.g. sealed interface localised oxidation [SILO] or side-wall mask isolation [SWAMI] -- The entry covers constructional details of integrated semiconductor devices that employ local oxidation of silicon to create isolation regions, such as LOCOS, SILO or SWAMI techniques. These methods form oxide sidewalls or sealed interfaces to separate active areas on chips, wafers or substrates.
[H10W 10/17] formed using trench refilling with dielectric materials, e.g. shallow trench isolations -- H10W 10/17 covers semiconductor manufacturing steps that create isolation by filling etched trenches in chips, wafers or substrates with dielectric material, for example shallow trench isolation. It relates to constructional details of integrated devices, focusing on trench-refill techniques that define electrical separation within the semiconductor structure.
[H10W 10/181] Semiconductor-on-insulator [SOI] isolation regions, e.g. buried oxide regions of SOI wafers -- H10W 10/181 covers semiconductor-on-insulator isolation regions such as the buried oxide layer in SOI wafers that provides electrical separation between active devices on a chip. The code belongs to CPC class H10W, which deals with constructional details of semiconductor devices, including packages and interconnections.
[H10W 10/20] Air gaps -- Air gaps are intentional voids introduced within or between semiconductor structures, such as chips, wafers or substrates, to provide electrical insulation, reduce parasitic capacitance, improve thermal isolation or enhance signal speed. The classification covers methods of forming, locating and integrating these gaps in device construction.
[H10W 10/30] Isolation regions comprising PN junctions -- H10W 10/30 covers isolation regions in semiconductor chips that are created using p-n junctions to electrically separate circuit elements on wafers, substrates or integrated devices. The classification applies to constructional details where these junction-based isolation structures are incorporated within generic packages, interconnections or other device layouts.
[H10W 10/40] Isolation regions comprising polycrystalline semiconductor materials -- H10W 10/40 covers the design of isolation regions in semiconductor chips that use polycrystalline semiconductor materials to electrically separate circuit elements. The classification pertains to constructional details of integrated devices such as chips, wafers, or substrates within the broader categories of electricity and semiconductor devices.
[H10W 10/50] Isolation regions based on field-effect -- This subclass covers techniques that create electrically isolated regions in a semiconductor using field-effect mechanisms such as depletion or accumulation zones formed by biased electrodes or built-in electric fields, allowing separation of circuitry without physical trenching. It applies to the constructional details of integrated devices on chips, wafers or substrates.
[H10W 15/00] Highly-doped buried regions of integrated devices -- The H10W 15/00 subgroup covers integrated semiconductor devices that incorporate highly-doped buried regions, such as buried layers or wells, used to control electrical characteristics and isolation within chips. It relates to the design and fabrication of these regions in generic packages, interconnections and other constructional details of semiconductor devices.
[H10W 15/01] Manufacture or treatment -- The H10W 15/01 subclass in the CPC pertains to the manufacture or treatment of highly-doped buried regions within integrated semiconductor devices. It covers processes that create or modify deep, heavily-doped layers-such as diffusion, ion implantation or epitaxial steps-used to form buried contacts, wells or isolation structures in chips.
[H10W 20/00] Interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates -- This group covers : interconnections in chips; interconnections in or on wafers; interconnections in or on substrates. This group does not cover interconnections in packages, such as in or on package substrates, which are covered by subgroups H10W70/00 or H10W72/00 .
[H10W 20/01] Manufacture or treatment -- H10W 20/01 covers processes for manufacturing or treating the interconnections that link components within semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates. It includes methods such as deposition, patterning, etching, plating or annealing used to form conductive lines, vias or contacts in integrated-circuit packages and similar structures.
[H10W 20/021] of interconnections within wafers or substrates -- The CPC subclass H10W 20/021 covers interconnections that are formed within semiconductor wafers or substrates. It includes the design, fabrication and arrangement of conductive paths, vias and other linking structures that connect devices on the same wafer or substrate.
[H10W 20/023] the interconnections being through-semiconductor vias -- Group H10W20/023 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10W20/0234 , H10W20/0238 , H10W20/0242 , H10W20/0245 , H10W20/0249 , H10W20/0253 , H10W20/0257 , H10W20/0261 and H10W20/0265 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/0234] comprising etching via holes that stop on pads or on electrodes -- Groups H10W20/0234 - H10W20/0265 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W20/023 . Groups H10W20/023 and H10W20/0234 - H10W20/0265 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/0238] comprising etching via holes through pads or through electrodes -- H10W 20/0238 covers semiconductor interconnect structures in which via holes are formed by etching through the conductive pads or electrodes on a chip, wafer or substrate. The feature enables vertical electrical connections between layers without additional metallization steps.
[H10W 20/0242] comprising etching via holes from the back sides of the chips, wafers or substrates -- H10W 20/0242 covers semiconductor interconnect methods that create conductive vias by etching holes from the back side of chips, wafers or substrates. This backside-via approach enables three-dimensional integration and reduces front-side processing, allowing more compact and efficient device layouts.
[H10W 20/0245] comprising use of blind vias during the manufacture -- The code H10W 20/0245 covers methods that incorporate blind vias - holes that connect only to one side of a semiconductor wafer or chip - during fabrication. Blind vias enable routing of signals without penetrating the entire substrate, improving density and performance.
[H10W 20/0249] wherein the through-semiconductor via protrudes from backsides of the chips, wafers or substrates during the manufacture -- H10W 20/0249 covers semiconductor interconnect structures in which a through-semiconductor via extends beyond the backside of the chip, wafer or substrate as a result of the manufacturing process. It is used to classify devices that intentionally create protruding vias for routing, testing or packaging purposes.
[H10W 20/0253] comprising forming the through-semiconductor vias after stacking of the chips, wafers or substrates -- H10W 20/0253 is a CPC subclass for semiconductor device manufacturing that deals with forming through-semiconductor vias after the chips, wafers or substrates have been stacked. It falls under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Packages, Interconnections, Connectors or Other Constructional Details of Devices Covered by Class, specifically addressing interconnections within stacked semiconductor components.
[H10W 20/0257] comprising using a sacrificial placeholder, e.g. using a sacrificial plug -- The subclass H10W 20/0257 covers methods for creating interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that involve placing a temporary, sacrificial element such as a plug or placeholder during fabrication. After the surrounding structures are formed, the sacrificial component is removed, leaving a void or conductive path that completes the interconnection. This technique is used to simplify routing, protect delicate features, or enable subsequent processing steps.
[H10W 20/0261] characterised by the filling method or the material of the conductive fill -- H10W 20/0261 covers interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates where the distinction lies in the method used to fill the conductive pathways or in the material employed for the conductive fill, such as solder, copper, or conductive polymer. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices. This subclass isolates technologies defined by how the conductive material is deposited or what it is made of.
[H10W 20/0265] characterised by the sidewall insulation -- H10W 20/0265 covers semiconductor interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates that are defined by having sidewall insulation. The feature isolates the conductive pathways from surrounding structures, improving electrical isolation and reliability in integrated circuit layouts.
[H10W 20/031] of conductive parts of the interconnections -- H10W 20/031 relates to the conductive elements that form the interconnections within semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates. It includes metal lines, vias, contacts and other conductive structures that electrically link circuit components on the die. The classification addresses their materials, geometry and integration in the device architecture.
[H10W 20/032] of conductive barrier, adhesion or liner layers -- The H10W 20/032 subclass deals with conductive barrier, adhesion or liner layers applied in chip, wafer or substrate interconnections. It covers thin-film structures that prevent metal diffusion, enhance mechanical bonding and act as under-layers for subsequent metallisation in semiconductor packages.
[H10W 20/033] in openings in dielectrics -- The H10W 20/033 entry covers interconnections that are formed through openings in dielectric materials of chips, wafers or substrates, such as vias, trenches or other patterned holes used to route electrical signals between conductive layers.
[H10W 20/034] bottomless barrier, adhesion or liner layers -- H10W 20/034 relates to bottomless barrier, adhesion or liner layers employed in interconnection structures of semiconductor devices. These layers protect, mechanically support or bond conductive features on chips, wafers or substrates without forming a closed barrier, thereby enhancing the reliability of the connections.
[H10W 20/035] combinations of barrier, adhesion or liner layers, e.g. multi-layered barrier layers -- The entry H10W 20/035 covers semiconductor device interconnections where multiple barrier, adhesion or liner layers are combined, such as multi-layered barrier structures applied to chips, wafers or substrates to protect or isolate conductive features. It relates to the constructional details of generic packages and connectors within the electricity-semiconductor devices hierarchy.
[H10W 20/036] the barrier, adhesion or liner layers being within a main fill metal -- The H10W 20/036 entry covers semiconductor interconnections where barrier, adhesion or liner layers are incorporated inside the primary metal fill that forms the conductive path. It pertains to chip, wafer or substrate interconnect structures within generic packages, focusing on the layered composition of the main metal conductor.
[H10W 20/037] the barrier, adhesion or liner layers being on top of a main fill metal -- Group H10W20/037 is impacted by reclassification into group H10W20/0372 . Groups H10W20/037 and H10W20/0372 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/0372] comprising multiple barrier, adhesion or liner layers -- Group H10W20/0372 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W20/037 . Groups H10W20/037 and H10W20/0372 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/0375] comprising multiple stacked thin barrier, adhesion or liner layers not being formed in openings in dielectrics -- H10W 20/0375 relates to semiconductor interconnections in which several thin barrier, adhesion or liner layers are stacked on a chip, wafer or substrate, but the layers are not formed inside openings in the dielectric. The entry covers structures and processes that apply these continuous multilayer films to improve isolation, protection or bonding of interconnect lines.
[H10W 20/038] covering conductive structures  H10W20/037  takes precedence -- H10W 20/038 concerns the covering of conductive structures in semiconductor devices, specifically interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates. It deals with protective or insulating layers applied over metal lines, vias or other conductive features. The more specific sub-group H10W 20/037 is considered prior art for this subject.
[H10W 20/039] also covering sidewalls of the conductive structures -- H10W 20/039 designates interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates that include the sidewalls of conductive structures. It falls under the generic-package subclass of semiconductor devices within the electricity section, covering constructional details such as connectors, interconnections and related features of semiconductor packages.
[H10W 20/041] the barrier, adhesion or liner layers being discontinuous -- H10W 20/041 covers semiconductor interconnections where the barrier, adhesion or liner layers are not continuous across the chip, wafer or substrate. It applies to designs that deliberately create discontinuities in these layers to manage stress, improve reliability or enable specific manufacturing processes.
[H10W 20/042] the barrier, adhesion or liner layers being seed or nucleation layers -- H10W 20/042 covers the use of seed or nucleation layers that function as barrier, adhesion or liner films in semiconductor interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates. These layers provide a foundation for subsequent metallization, improving adhesion and preventing diffusion between conductive materials.
[H10W 20/0425] comprising multiple stacked seed or nucleation layers -- This subclass covers semiconductor interconnection structures that incorporate multiple stacked seed or nucleation layers to form conductive paths on chips, wafers or substrates, enhancing adhesion, barrier function or electrical performance.
[H10W 20/043] for electroplating -- This group covers electroplating processes for forming interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates within generic packages, providing conductive pathways between device elements.
[H10W 20/044] for electroless plating -- H10W 20/044 refers to interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that are formed by electroless plating, a chemical process that deposits metal without external current. It covers the structures, bonding pads or conductive paths created by this plating technique and related methods for establishing electrical connections within generic semiconductor packages.
[H10W 20/045] for deposition from the gaseous phase, e.g. for chemical vapour deposition [CVD] -- The subclass H10W 20/045 pertains to forming interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by depositing material from the gaseous phase, such as chemical vapour deposition (CVD). It falls under the hierarchy of electricity, semiconductor devices and generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices covered by the class.
[H10W 20/046] the barrier, adhesion or liner layers being associated with interconnections of capacitors -- H10W 20/046 covers the barrier, adhesion or liner layers employed in the interconnections of capacitors on chips, wafers or substrates. It is situated in the hierarchy electricity → semiconductor devices → generic packages, interconnections, and addresses materials that provide reliable electrical and mechanical coupling within capacitor structures.
[H10W 20/047] by introducing additional elements therein -- This subclass addresses semiconductor interconnection techniques that create or modify connections by introducing additional elements-such as conductive lines, vias, bumps or other structures-into chips, wafers or substrates.
[H10W 20/048] by using plasmas or gaseous environments, e.g. by nitriding -- This CPC subgroup details methods for creating interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by exposing the material to a plasma or other gaseous environment, such as nitriding to alter surface properties. It falls under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Packages, Interconnections, Connectors or Other Constructional Details of Devices Covered by Class.
[H10W 20/049] by diffusing alloying elements -- The H10W 20/049 subclass covers methods of forming interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by diffusing alloying elements into the material to create conductive paths, rather than using deposited or etched metal layers. This technique modifies the substrate composition to establish electrical connections within the device.
[H10W 20/051] by ion implantation -- H10W 20/051 covers the use of ion implantation to create or modify interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates. It includes processes where dopant ions are implanted to form conductive paths, contact regions or buried layers within the device's internal wiring structure.
[H10W 20/052] by treatments not introducing additional elements therein -- This sub-group of the CPC covers methods of creating interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates by treatments that alter the material itself without introducing new conductive elements. Techniques include annealing, laser, plasma or chemical modification that induce conductive paths within the existing structure.
[H10W 20/0523] by irradiating with ultraviolet or particle radiation -- This subclass covers techniques for creating or modifying electrical interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates by exposing the material to ultraviolet light or particle radiation such as lasers, electron beams or ion beams, typically to pattern, bond or alter conductive paths.
[H10W 20/0526] by thermal treatment thereof -- H10W 20/0526 is a CPC subgroup for semiconductor devices that addresses interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates when they are created or altered by thermal treatment. It includes processes such as annealing, reflow or sintering that modify the electrical connections within generic packages or connector structures.
[H10W 20/054] by selectively removing parts thereof  H10W20/034  takes precedence -- H10W 20/054 defines methods for creating interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by selectively removing portions of the material, such as etching or laser ablation. It falls under the generic-package interconnection subclass and is subordinate to H10W 20/034, which has priority over this entry.
[H10W 20/055] by formation methods other than physical vapour deposition [PVD], chemical vapour deposition [CVD] or liquid deposition -- H10W 20/055 covers the creation of electrical interconnections within semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates using manufacturing techniques that are not physical vapour deposition, chemical vapour deposition or any liquid-based deposition. It includes alternative methods such as electro-plating, printing, laser-induced or other solid-state processes for forming conductive paths.
[H10W 20/0552] by diffusing metallic dopants to react with dielectrics -- This subclass covers methods that introduce metallic dopants by diffusion into dielectric layers of chips, wafers or substrates so that the dopants react with the dielectric material, forming conductive regions or altering dielectric properties to create or improve interconnections within semiconductor devices.
[H10W 20/0554] of nanotubes or nanowires -- H10W 20/0554 covers interconnections within semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that employ nanotubes or nanowires as conductive elements. The classification addresses the use of these nanoscale structures for electrical connections in integrated circuits.
[H10W 20/056] by filling conductive material into holes, grooves or trenches -- Group H10W20/056 is impacted by reclassification into group H10W20/0595 . Groups H10W20/056 and H10W20/0595 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/057] by selectively depositing, e.g. by using selective CVD or plating -- This CPC entry covers techniques for creating interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates by depositing material only where needed, such as selective chemical-vapor deposition or selective plating, allowing localized metallisation without full-area processing. It is classified under H10W 20/057 within the electricity → semiconductor devices → generic packages and interconnections hierarchy.
[H10W 20/058] by depositing on sacrificial masks, e.g. using lift-off -- H10W 20/058 relates to forming interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates by first depositing material on a sacrificial mask and then removing the mask (lift-off) to leave the desired conductive pattern. This method is used in generic semiconductor packages and connector structures to create wiring or contacts without etching the underlying layers.
[H10W 20/059] by reflowing or applying pressure -- The H10W 20/059 subclass covers methods of creating interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that use reflow processes or the application of pressure, such as solder-reflow bonding or thermocompression techniques, to join conductive elements within generic packages or connector structures.
[H10W 20/0595] by using multiple deposition steps separated by etching steps -- Group H10W20/0595 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W20/056 . Groups H10W20/056 and H10W20/0595 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/062] by smoothing of conductive parts, e.g. by planarisation -- H10W 20/062 relates to interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates and covers methods that smooth conductive parts, for example by planarisation processes that level or flatten metal layers to improve electrical contact.
[H10W 20/063] by forming conductive members before forming protective insulating material -- Group H10W20/063 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10W20/0633 and H10W20/0636 . Groups H10W20/063 , H10W20/0633 and H10W20/0636 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/0633] using subtractive patterning of the conductive members -- Groups H10W20/0633 - H10W20/0636 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W20/063 . Groups H10W20/063 and H10W20/0633 - H10W20/0636 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 20/0636] the conductive members being on sidewalls -- H10W 20/0636 relates to interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates where the conductive elements are positioned on the sidewalls of the device. This sub-category falls under generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of semiconductor devices covered by the CPC. It specifies sidewall-mounted conductors as a means of linking circuit features within the chip.
[H10W 20/064] by modifying the conductivity of conductive parts, e.g. by alloying -- The CPC subclass H10W 20/064 relates to semiconductor device interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates and covers methods of altering the conductivity of conductive parts, for example by alloying, to achieve desired electrical performance.
[H10W 20/065] by making at least a portion of the conductive part non-conductive, e.g. by oxidation -- H10W 20/065 covers techniques for creating interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates where a section of a conductive line is converted to a non-conductive material, typically through oxidation, to achieve isolation or patterning.
[H10W 20/066] by forming silicides of refractory metals -- H10W 20/066 relates to forming low-resistance interconnections on semiconductor chips by creating silicide layers of refractory metals such as molybdenum, tungsten, titanium or tantalum. The process produces conductive contacts within the device, enhancing performance and reliability of integrated circuits.
[H10W 20/067] by modifying the pattern of conductive parts -- H10W 20/067 is a CPC subclass that applies to interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates where the conductive pattern is deliberately altered. It falls under the hierarchy of electricity, semiconductor devices and generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices covered by class. This classification captures methods that modify the layout of conductive parts to achieve the desired electrical connections.
[H10W 20/068] by using a laser, e.g. laser cutting or laser direct writing -- The CPC subclass H10W 20/68 covers methods for forming interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates by employing laser processes, such as laser cutting or laser direct writing, within generic semiconductor packages and connector structures. It is part of the broader hierarchy covering electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic package constructions.
[H10W 20/069] by forming self-aligned vias or self-aligned contact plugs -- Group H10W20/069 is impacted by reclassification into groups H10W20/0693 and H10W20/0696 . Groups H10W20/069 , H10W20/0693 and H10W20/0696 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.

[H10W 20/0696] by using sacrificial placeholders, e.g. using sacrificial plugs -- The H10W 20/0696 subclass relates to methods of forming interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by employing sacrificial placeholders such as sacrificial plugs that are later removed to create the final conductive pathways.
[H10W 20/0698] Local interconnections -- Local interconnections refer to conductive paths that link circuit elements within a single chip, wafer or substrate over short distances, such as metal lines, vias or buried interconnects used to route signals or power between components on the same semiconductor die.
[H10W 20/071] of dielectric parts thereof -- H10W 20/071 covers the dielectric components used in the interconnections of semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates. It includes insulating layers, passivation films and other non-conductive structures that separate conductive lines within a package. The classification is part of the CPC group dealing with generic packages and interconnections for semiconductor devices.
[H10W 20/072] of dielectric parts comprising air gaps -- H10W 20/072 pertains to semiconductor interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates that use dielectric components incorporating air gaps. The air gaps serve to lower parasitic capacitance, enhance isolation, or provide mechanical relief within the packaging.
[H10W 20/074] of dielectric parts comprising thin functional dielectric layers, e.g. dielectric etch-stop, barrier, capping or liner layers -- This classification covers structures where dielectric components in semiconductor devices consist of thin functional layers such as etch-stop, barrier, capping or liner layers used for interconnections in chips, wafers, or substrates. It pertains to the design and integration of these thin dielectric parts within generic packages, interconnections, connectors, or other constructional details.
[H10W 20/075] of multilayered thin functional dielectric layers -- The H10W 20/075 subclass pertains to interconnection structures in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that incorporate multilayered thin functional dielectric layers. It covers the design, composition and application of such dielectric stacks used to insulate, isolate or modify electrical properties within integrated circuits. This includes layered materials such as oxides, nitrides or polymer films engineered for specific dielectric functions.
[H10W 20/076] in via holes or trenches -- The CPC subclass H10W 20/076 refers to the creation of electrical interconnections within semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that are formed by drilling or etching via holes or trenches. It falls under the hierarchy of Electricity → Semiconductor devices → Generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices, specifically addressing the use of through-hole or trench structures to link circuit elements.
[H10W 20/0765] the thin functional dielectric layers being temporary, e.g. sacrificial layers -- The CPC entry H10W 20/0765 covers semiconductor devices where thin functional dielectric layers are used temporarily, such as sacrificial layers, to form interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates. These layers are deposited and later removed to create the final electrical connections within generic packages and connectors.
[H10W 20/077] on sidewalls or on top surfaces of conductors  H10W20/076  takes precedence -- H10W 20/077 covers interconnections made on the sidewalls or top surfaces of conductors within chips, wafers or substrates, detailing how such connections are formed or modified. It resides in the hierarchy Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Packages, Interconnections, Connectors or Other Constructional Details of Devices Covered by Class. H10W 20/076 is the preceding, higher-ranking entry.
[H10W 20/081] by forming openings in the dielectric parts -- The H10W 20/081 subclass covers methods for creating interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates by forming openings in dielectric layers. It includes processes such as photolithographic patterning, etching or laser drilling to open vias, trenches or contact holes that allow conductive paths to be established through insulating material.
[H10W 20/082] the openings being tapered via holes -- This subcategory covers interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that use tapered-hole openings to form contacts, improving electrical performance or mechanical reliability.
[H10W 20/083] the openings being via holes penetrating underlying conductors -- The entry H10W 20/083 covers the use of openings that are formed as via holes which pass through one or more underlying conductors in semiconductor devices. It pertains to interconnection structures on chips, wafers or substrates where such vias provide electrical connection between different conductive layers. This classification falls under generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices.
[H10W 20/084] for dual-damascene structures -- The CPC subclass H10W 20/084 refers to dual-damascene structures used to create interconnections within chips, wafers or substrates. It covers the combined formation of dielectric trenches and vias that are later filled with metal, enabling stacked interconnect layers in semiconductor devices.
[H10W 20/085] involving intermediate temporary filling with material -- H10W 20/085 pertains to semiconductor device interconnections where an intermediate, temporary filling material is introduced into the connection region of chips, wafers or substrates during manufacture, typically to support or protect features before it is removed or replaced later in the process. This technique falls under the broader categories of electricity, semiconductor devices and generic packaging, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details.
[H10W 20/086] involving buried masks -- H10W 20/086 covers methods and structures where interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates are formed using buried masks, i.e. mask layers placed beneath subsequent metallization to define hidden routing patterns. This approach is used to reduce surface congestion and improve reliability in semiconductor device packaging.
[H10W 20/087] involving multiple stacked pre-patterned masks -- H10W 20/087 pertains to interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that are created by using multiple stacked pre-patterned masks. The technique layers several mask patterns to define circuit features in a single process step, enhancing alignment accuracy and manufacturing efficiency.
[H10W 20/088] involving partial etching of via holes -- H10W 20/088 covers semiconductor interconnections where the via holes are only partially etched, creating a controlled opening that can be completed later or left as a shallow trench. This technique is used in chip, wafer or substrate packages to modify electrical pathways without full removal of the substrate material.
[H10W 20/0882] wherein the dual damascene structure is in a photoresist layer -- The H10W 20/0882 subclass relates to semiconductor devices and their generic package or interconnection details, specifically addressing interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates in which a dual-damascene structure is created within a photoresist layer.
[H10W 20/0884] involving forming vias by burying sacrificial pillars in the dielectric parts and removing the pillars -- The H10W 20/0884 subclass concerns semiconductor interconnection techniques that create vias by embedding sacrificial pillars within the dielectric layers of a chip or wafer and subsequently removing those pillars to form the conductive pathways. This method is used to produce vertical connections between different metal levels in integrated circuits.
[H10W 20/0886] involving forming a via in a via-level dielectric prior to deposition of a trench-level dielectric -- The entry H10W 20/0886 covers methods for creating a conductive via within a dielectric layer that already defines a lower-level interconnection, before a subsequent dielectric for a higher-level trench is deposited. It addresses the sequence of forming the via-level via to enable stacked interconnect structures in semiconductor chips.
[H10W 20/0888] wherein via-level dielectrics are compositionally different than trench-level dielectrics -- The subclass defines semiconductor interconnection structures in which the dielectric material surrounding vertical vias is compositionally distinct from the dielectric used in trench-level features. This distinction influences electrical isolation, capacitance and manufacturing processes for chips, wafers or substrates.
[H10W 20/089] using processes for implementing desired shapes or dispositions of the openings, e.g. double patterning -- The H10W 20/089 entry covers methods that shape or arrange openings in chip, wafer or substrate interconnections, employing processes such as double-patterning to achieve the required geometry or placement. It pertains to the constructional details of semiconductor device packages and connectors within the broader electricity and semiconductor device classes.
[H10W 20/091] by printing or stamping -- H10W 20/091 covers methods for creating interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by using printing or stamping techniques such as ink-jet, screen-printing or embossing to form conductive patterns, providing an alternative to conventional photolithographic processes. This subgroup falls under generic packages and interconnections for semiconductor devices.
[H10W 20/092] by smoothing the dielectric parts -- H10W 20/092 covers techniques for smoothing the dielectric regions of interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates, focusing on how the insulating layers are planarized or refined to improve electrical performance and device reliability.
[H10W 20/093] by modifying materials of the dielectric parts -- A subclass of H10W 20/09 that covers chip, wafer or substrate interconnections in which the dielectric material is altered to achieve desired electrical or mechanical properties, for example by changing composition, adding fillers, doping, using alternative polymers or forming composite dielectrics.
[H10W 20/094] by transforming insulators into conductors -- The code H10W 20/094 covers methods that convert insulating regions of a chip, wafer or substrate into conductive elements to form interconnections. It includes techniques such as localized doping, laser-induced crystallisation, or other processes that change an insulator's electrical properties, enabling electrical routing within semiconductor devices without adding separate conductive material.
[H10W 20/095] by irradiating with electromagnetic or particle radiation  plasma treatment  H10W20/096 -- The H10W 20/095 subclass deals with techniques for creating or altering interconnections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by exposing them to electromagnetic or particle radiation, including plasma-based treatments. [H10W 20/096] by contacting with gases, liquids or plasmas -- H10W 20/096 covers interconnections in chips, wafers or substrates that are formed by contacting the connection areas with gases, liquids or plasmas, such as deposition, etching, plating or doping processes that create conductive paths without mechanical bonding. This subgroup falls under generic package and connector details for semiconductor devices.
[H10W 20/097] by thermally treating -- The subclass H10W 20/097 covers methods of creating or modifying electrical interconnections in semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that rely on thermal treatment, such as annealing, diffusion bonding or heat-induced crystallization, to join conductors or improve connection reliability within generic package structures.
[H10W 20/098] by filling between adjacent conductive parts -- H10W 20/98 covers methods of forming electrical connections on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by depositing a conductive filler material into the space between neighboring conductive features, thereby joining them without the use of metal lines or wire bonds. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Packages, Interconnections, Connectors or Other Constructional Details of Devices Covered by Class, specifically addressing interconnections created by filling between adjacent conductive parts.

[H10W 20/494] changeable by the use of an external beam, e.g. laser beam or ion beam -- The subclass H10W 20/494 refers to semiconductor interconnections on chips, wafers or substrates that can be altered after manufacture by directing an external beam such as a laser or ion beam. This enables re-routing, trimming or repairing of conductive paths within the package without physical contact.
[H10W 20/495] Capacitive arrangements or effects of, or between wiring layers -- This subclass concerns the design, implementation and analysis of capacitance created by or affecting wiring layers in semiconductor chips and wafers, including intentional capacitive structures and parasitic coupling between metal or conductive layers.
[H10W 20/496] Capacitor integral with wiring layers -- H10W 20/496 refers to semiconductor devices in which a capacitor is formed as part of the wiring layers that interconnect circuit elements on a chip, wafer or substrate, integrating the capacitor directly within the device's interconnect structure.
[H10W 20/497] Inductive arrangements or effects of, or between, wiring layers -- H10W 20/497 covers inductive configurations and phenomena that arise within or between wiring layers in semiconductor devices, such as mutual inductance, stray coupling, and intentional inductive structures built into interconnect stacks. The classification is used for patents describing how these inductive effects are managed, exploited, or mitigated in chip, wafer or substrate interconnections.
[H10W 20/498] Resistive arrangements or effects of, or between, wiring layers -- H10W 20/498 covers the design and analysis of resistive structures that arise within or between wiring layers of semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates. It includes intentional or parasitic resistive arrangements, their impact on circuit performance, and methods for managing resistance in interconnect stacks used in generic package and connector constructions.
[H10W 29/00] Generic parts of integrated devices, not otherwise provided for -- H10W 29/00 in the CPC covers generic components used in integrated semiconductor devices that are not defined elsewhere, such as unspecified packages, interconnections, or connectors. It applies to any part of an integrated circuit that serves a structural or functional role but lacks a more specific classification within the semiconductor-device hierarchy.
[H10W 29/01] Manufacture or treatment -- H10W 29/01 covers processes for manufacturing or treating generic parts of integrated semiconductor devices that are not separately defined elsewhere, such as basic structures used in generic packages, interconnections, or connectors. The classification is applied to routine fabrication steps or post-fabrication treatments of these unspecified components.

It includes the mechanical joining, alignment and bonding steps that bind the parts together to form a functional assembly. This falls under constructional details for semiconductor devices within the broader electricity and semiconductor device categories.

The classification includes any arrangement intended to raise temperature for processing, testing or operation, such as built-in heaters, resistive films or integrated thermal pads.

[H10W 40/233] attached to chips -- H10W 40/233 concerns the means of attaching other elements directly to semiconductor chips, including bonding, mounting, adhesive or other joining techniques employed in chip, wafer or package construction.
