It pertains to elements that are attached to the package parts, such as mounting features, clips, leads or bonding structures.
[H10W 40/237] attached to additional arrangements for cooling -- H10W 40/237 relates to semiconductor device constructions where the package includes or is connected to auxiliary cooling elements such as heat sinks, thermal spreaders, cooling plates or similar structures that provide additional heat dissipation. These cooling arrangements are treated as part of the package's constructional details for managing temperature.
[H10W 40/242] comprising thermal conductors between chips and the and the arrangements for cooling, e.g. compliant heat-spreaders -- H10W 40/242 covers semiconductor devices that incorporate thermal conductors between chips and cooling structures, such as compliant heat-spreaders, to provide efficient heat removal. It belongs to the generic packages, interconnections, and connectors subclass within electricity-related semiconductor technology.
[H10W 40/25] characterised by their materials -- The H10W 40/25 subgroup covers constructional details of semiconductor chips, wafers, substrates or packages that are defined primarily by the material from which they are made, such as metals, ceramics, polymers or composites. It classifies features where the material choice is the distinguishing technical characteristic.
[H10W 40/251] Organics -- The H10W 40/251 subclass covers semiconductor packaging features that employ organic materials, such as polymeric die-attach layers, organic passivation or encapsulation compounds, and adhesive interconnects. It includes the design and use of organic films, resins, and other carbon-based substances that form part of chips, wafers, substrates or package structures.
[H10W 40/253] Semiconductors -- H10W 40/253 pertains to semiconductor devices and specifically addresses constructional details of chips, wafers, substrates, or packages, including generic packages, interconnections, connectors and other structural features that facilitate the integration and mounting of semiconductor components.
[H10W 40/254] Diamond -- The subclass H10W 40/254 relates to semiconductor devices in which diamond is used as a material for chips, wafers, substrates or other constructional details of the package, typically for heat-spreading, insulation or protective functions. It covers any structural or functional element of the device that incorporates diamond.
[H10W 40/255] having a laminate or multilayered structure, e.g. direct bond copper [DBC] ceramic substrates -- The code H10W 40/255 pertains to semiconductor devices whose construction employs a laminate or multilayered structure, such as direct-bond-copper (DBC) ceramic substrates. It addresses how chips, wafers, or packages are built by stacking layers to achieve specific electrical and mechanical properties.
[H10W 40/257] having a heterogeneous or anisotropic structure, e.g. powder or fibres in a matrix, wire mesh or porous structures  H10W40/254 ,  H10W40/251  take precedence -- This CPC subclass H10W 40/257 covers semiconductor devices where the material has a heterogeneous or anisotropic structure such as powder or fibers embedded in a matrix, wire mesh or porous structures. It is a constructional detail for chips, wafers, substrates or packages and takes precedence over H10W40/254 and H10W40/251.
[H10W 40/258] Metallic materials  H10W40/254 ,  H10W40/257 ,  H10W40/255 ,  H10W40/251 ,  H10W40/253  take precedence -- H10W 40/258 pertains to the use of metallic materials in the construction of semiconductor device packages, interconnections, and related structures such as chips, wafers, or substrates. It specifies that earlier defined metallic material subclasses (H10W40/254, H10W40/257, H10W40/255, H10W40/251, H10W40/253) take precedence over this entry. The code falls under the broader CPC groups for electricity and semiconductor devices.
[H10W 40/259] Ceramics or glasses  H10W40/254 ,  H10W40/257 ,  H10W40/255 ,  H10W40/251 ,  H10W40/253  take precedence -- H10W 40/259 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers constructional details related to ceramics or glasses used in chips, wafers, substrates, or packages, with specific focus on areas not covered by more detailed codes such as H10W40/254, H10W40/257, and others.
[H10W 40/28] comprising Peltier coolers -- The H10W 40/28 subclass designates semiconductor devices whose construction includes a Peltier cooler integrated into the generic package, interconnection, connector or related structural element. It covers details of how the thermoelectric cooling element is incorporated with chips, wafers, substrates or other package components.
[H10W 40/30] wherein the packaged device is completely immersed in a fluid other than air, e.g. immersed in a cryogenic fluid -- This subclass covers semiconductor packages that are wholly immersed in a fluid other than air, such as cryogenic liquids, to provide cooling, insulation, or environmental protection. The immersion applies to the entire packaged device, including chips, wafers, or substrates, and is relevant for designs that rely on liquid environments rather than ambient air.
[H10W 40/305] the fluid being a liquefied gas, e.g. liquid nitrogen -- The H10W 40/305 subclass covers semiconductor or other electronic packages in which the cooling medium is a liquefied gas, such as liquid nitrogen, used to remove heat from chips, wafers or substrates. It addresses the constructional details of packages that incorporate this cryogenic fluid for thermal management.
[H10W 40/40] involving heat exchange by flowing fluids -- H10W 40/40 covers constructional features of semiconductor chips, wafers, substrates or packages that provide heat removal by directing flowing fluids through or across the device. It includes integrated liquid-cooling channels, micro-fluidic passages and other fluid-based heat-exchange structures designed to dissipate heat from the active semiconductor elements.
[H10W 40/43] by flowing gases, e.g. forced air cooling -- H10W 40/43 refers to constructional features of semiconductor chips, wafers, substrates or packages that use flowing gases, such as forced-air cooling, to remove heat from the device. It falls under electricity → semiconductor devices → generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details.
[H10W 40/47] by flowing liquids, e.g. forced water cooling -- H10W 40/47 covers methods of cooling semiconductor chips, wafers, substrates or their packages by directing a flowing liquid, such as forced water cooling, over or through the device or its interconnections.

[H10W 40/735] by melting or evaporation of solids -- This subgroup concerns processes that form or modify semiconductor chips, wafers, substrates or package structures by melting or evaporating solid materials, such as crystal growth, melt-casting, or physical vapor deposition techniques used to create conductive or insulating layers within the device.

[H10W 44/00] Electrical arrangements for controlling or matching impedance -- The CPC subclass H10W 44/00 covers electrical arrangements incorporated in semiconductor device packages that are designed to control or match impedance, such as transmission-line structures, terminations, and impedance-balancing networks. These features are intended to optimise signal integrity, minimise reflections and ensure proper power transfer between the device and its external circuitry.
[H10W 44/20] at high-frequency [HF] or radio frequency [RF] -- A subclass of semiconductor device packaging that deals with electrical arrangements used to control or match impedance when operating at high-frequency or radio-frequency bands. It falls under generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices covered by the broader electricity-semiconductor-devices hierarchy.
[H10W 44/203] Electrical connections -- H10W 44/203 covers electrical connections used to control or match impedance in semiconductor devices, focusing on generic packages, interconnections, connectors, and other constructional details. It is part of the CPC hierarchy under Electricity, Semiconductor Devices, and Generic Packages, Interconnections, Connectors or other Constructional Details of Devices.
[H10W 44/206] Wires -- H10W 44/206 covers wires used in semiconductor device packages and related interconnections, focusing on electrical arrangements that control or match impedance. It includes the design and construction of wiring elements that form part of connectors, leads or other conductive paths within generic semiconductor packages.
[H10W 44/209] Vertical interconnections, e.g. vias -- The H10W 44/209 subclass covers vertical interconnections, commonly called vias, that link different layers within semiconductor packages or circuit assemblies. It relates to designs that manage signal routing and impedance matching across stacked structures, often used in integrated circuit interconnect technologies.
[H10W 44/212] Coaxial feed-throughs in substrates -- The CPC entry H10W 44/212 covers coaxial feed-through structures that are built into semiconductor substrates to route electrical signals through the package while preserving controlled impedance. These components are used in electric arrangements for impedance matching and are part of the broader category of generic semiconductor package interconnections.
[H10W 44/216] Waveguides, e.g. strip lines -- H10W 44/216 covers waveguide structures, such as strip-line configurations, used to guide electromagnetic signals within electric circuits. These elements form part of the impedance-matching and control arrangements in semiconductor devices, providing defined transmission paths for high-frequency or microwave signals.
[H10W 44/219] characterised by transitions between different types of waveguides -- H10W 44/219 covers electrical arrangements that provide impedance matching by using transitions between dissimilar waveguides. It includes design features for connecting or adapting waveguide sections of different dimensions, shapes or materials within semiconductor or other electronic packages. Such transitions are used to guide electromagnetic energy efficiently across component interfaces.
[H10W 44/223] Differential pair signal lines -- The subclass H10W 44/223 concerns the design and layout of paired signal conductors that carry opposite-phase signals, i.e., differential pairs, within semiconductor packages or their interconnections. It covers routing, spacing, shielding and impedance-matching techniques used to minimise crosstalk and improve signal integrity. The classification is applied to electrical arrangements that control or match impedance for such differential signal lines.
[H10W 44/226] for HF amplifiers -- This subclass concerns electrical arrangements that control or match impedance in high-frequency (HF) amplifiers, focusing on the circuitry and interconnections within semiconductor packages and related connector structures. It addresses impedance-matching networks and related circuit topologies used to optimise HF amplifier performance.
[H10W 44/231] Arrangements for applying bias -- H10W 44/231 is a CPC entry covering circuit arrangements that provide a bias voltage or current to semiconductor devices. It falls under the electricity sector, specifically semiconductor devices and their generic packages, connectors, or other construction details, and relates to electrical arrangements for controlling or matching impedance. Such biasing schemes are used to set operating points, improve performance or protect components.
[H10W 44/234] Arrangements for impedance matching -- H10W 44/234 covers electrical arrangements designed to match the impedance of semiconductor devices or their interconnections, ensuring efficient power transfer and minimizing reflections. It includes circuits, networks, and structures that adjust impedance between components within generic packages and connectors.
[H10W 44/241] for passive devices or passive elements -- The CPC code H10W 44/241 falls under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Packages, Interconnections, Connectors or Other Constructional Details of Devices Covered by Class, specifically concerning electrical arrangements for controlling or matching impedance. It is applied to designs that use passive devices or passive elements-such as resistors, inductors, capacitors or filters-within semiconductor packages to achieve impedance matching or control.
[H10W 44/243] for decoupling, e.g. bypass capacitors -- H10W 44/243 covers electrical arrangements that provide decoupling, such as bypass capacitors, used to control or match impedance in semiconductor devices and their interconnections.

[H10W 44/251] for monolithic microwave integrated circuits [MMIC] -- H10W 44/251 covers electrical arrangements that control or match impedance in monolithic microwave integrated circuits. [H10W 44/255] for operation at multiple different frequencies -- H10W 44/255 designates semiconductor devices or generic packages whose electrical arrangements are intended to control or match impedance when the device must operate at several distinct frequencies, covering circuits and interconnections that maintain optimal performance across those multiple bands.
[H10W 44/259] Optical signal interface -- H10W 44/259 covers optical signal interfaces that connect optical fibers or waveguides to semiconductor devices, providing the electrical arrangements needed to control and match impedance between optical and electronic circuitry. It includes coupling structures, connectors and matching networks that enable efficient transmission of light-derived signals into electrical domains.
[H10W 44/401] Resistive arrangements  H10W44/20 ,  H10W42/80  take precedence -- H10W 44/401 covers resistive arrangements used in semiconductor device packages to control or match impedance. It includes resistors integrated into interconnections, connectors, or other structural features of the device. [H10W 44/501] Inductive arrangements  H10W44/20  takes precedence -- H10W 44/501 designates inductive arrangements used in semiconductor device packages, interconnections or connectors to control or match electrical impedance. It falls under the broader category of electrical arrangements for impedance control, and its content is subordinated to the more general H10W 44/20 class. This code applies to inductors, chokes or similar components integrated into the device's construction for impedance-matching purposes.
[H10W 44/601] Capacitive arrangements  H10W44/20  takes precedence -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code H10W 44/601 pertains to capacitive arrangements within the broader category of electrical arrangements for controlling or matching impedance in semiconductor devices. This classification specifically addresses capacitive elements and their integration, with a note that H10W44/20 takes precedence when applicable.
[H10W 46/00] Marks applied to devices, e.g. for alignment or identification -- This group covers marks in or on chips, wafers, substrates or packages.

[H10W 46/103] alphanumeric information, e.g. words, letters or serial numbers -- The entry H10W 46/103 relates to alphanumeric information applied to semiconductor devices, such as words, letters, or serial numbers used for alignment, identification or traceability. This includes any printed, etched, embossed or attached markings on generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of the devices.

[H10W 46/201] located on the periphery of wafers, e.g. orientation notches or lot numbers -- H10W 46/201 covers markings placed on the outer edge of semiconductor wafers, such as orientation notches, lot numbers or other identifiers used for alignment and identification of the device.
[H10W 46/301] for alignment -- H10W 46/301 covers the use of marks on semiconductor devices to provide alignment, orientation or identification during assembly, testing or inspection. These markings may be printed, etched or otherwise applied to the package, interconnections or other constructional details of the device.
[H10W 46/401] for identification or tracking -- The H10W 46/401 subclass concerns marks, symbols or other visual identifiers that are applied to semiconductor packages or related components. It includes features used to align, identify or track devices during manufacturing, testing, assembly or service, such as labeling, color codes, or alignment marks.
[H10W 46/403] for non-wireless electrical read out -- The subclass H10W 46/403 pertains to non-wireless electrical read-out features incorporated in semiconductor device packages, such as marks, indicators or other conductive elements used to align, identify or otherwise provide a direct electrical signal from the device without employing wireless transmission.
[H10W 46/407] for wireless electrical read out -- H10W 46/407 covers the use of marks that enable a wireless electrical read-out of semiconductor devices, such as tags or sensors embedded in generic packages, interconnections or connectors, to allow identification, alignment or data retrieval without direct contact. This falls under constructional details for devices in the electricity-semiconductor field.
[H10W 46/501] for use before dicing -- The subclass H10W 46/501 covers markings that are placed on semiconductor wafers or devices to aid alignment, orientation or identification before the wafer is diced into individual chips. Such marks are applied as part of the package or interconnection preparation, not as functional circuitry.
[H10W 46/503] Located in scribe lines -- This subclass concerns semiconductor packages where identification or alignment marks are formed within the scribe lines that separate individual chips, forming part of the generic package or interconnection details for device tracking or alignment.
[H10W 46/507] Located in dummy chips or in reference chips -- The H10W 46/507 subclass defines markings that are placed on semiconductor devices situated in dummy or reference chips, such as alignment or identification features used during testing or assembly. It falls under generic packages, interconnections and connectors for semiconductor devices, and pertains to any marks applied to aid positioning, calibration or identification of the chip within the package.
[H10W 46/601] for use after dicing -- The code H10W 46/601 refers to marks or identifiers that are applied to semiconductor chips after they have been diced from the wafer, serving purposes such as alignment, tracking or device identification during later packaging or assembly steps.
[H10W 46/603] Formed on wafers or substrates before dicing and remaining on chips after dicing -- H10W 46/603 covers marks that are applied to semiconductor wafers or substrates before they are diced and remain on the individual chips after dicing, such as alignment or identification markings integrated into the device structure.
[H10W 46/607] Located on parts of packages, e.g. on encapsulations or on package substrates -- This CPC subclass H10W 46/607 concerns markings placed on elements of semiconductor packages, such as the encapsulation or the package substrate, that serve purposes like alignment, identification or other functional indications. It falls under the generic-package and interconnection details category for semiconductor devices.
[H10W 70/00] Aspects of packages -- Groups H10W70/00 , H10W70/01 and H10W70/04 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W99/00 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 70/01] Manufacture or treatment -- H10W 70/01 covers processes for manufacturing or treating semiconductor device packages, including treatments that modify the physical or chemical characteristics of generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other structural details of devices within the electricity-semiconductor-generic-package hierarchy. It addresses the fabrication steps that affect package performance or reliability.

Group H10W70/02 is also impacted by reclassification into group H10W40/03 . Groups H10W99/00 , H10W70/02 and H10W40/03 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 70/023] Connecting or disconnecting interconnections thereto or therefrom, e.g. connecting bond wires or bumps -- H10W 70/023 relates to semiconductor device packages and covers methods for connecting or disconnecting interconnections to the package, such as attaching or removing bond wires, bumps, or similar conductive links. It falls under the CPC hierarchy electricity → semiconductor devices → generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices.
[H10W 70/027] Mechanical treatments, e.g. deforming, punching or cutting -- Group H10W70/027 is impacted by reclassification into group H10W40/033 . Groups H10W70/027 and H10W40/033 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 70/04] of leadframes -- H10W 70/04 concerns the design and use of leadframes in semiconductor device packages, including their geometry, material composition, and methods of attachment to the chip. It falls under aspects of packages within the generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices covered by class.
[H10W 70/041] Connecting or disconnecting interconnections to or from leadframes, e.g. connecting bond wires or bumps -- The H10W 70/041 classification covers techniques for attaching or removing electrical interconnections, such as bond wires or bump contacts, to semiconductor leadframes. It includes the processes, fixtures and structures used to form or break these connections within generic package assemblies.
[H10W 70/042] Etching -- H10W 70/042 covers etching techniques applied to the constructional features of semiconductor device packages, interconnections and connectors, such as selective removal of material to form or modify conductive paths, openings or surface structures within the package architecture.
[H10W 70/045] Cleaning -- The H10W 70/045 subclass refers to cleaning operations applied to semiconductor device packages, interconnections, connectors or related constructional details. It includes methods for removing particles, residues, oxides or other contaminants from the surfaces of packaged semiconductor components during or after assembly, using mechanical, chemical, thermal or plasma techniques.
[H10W 70/047] Attaching leadframes to insulating supports, e.g. for tape automated bonding [TAB] -- H10W 70/047 pertains to methods of attaching leadframes to insulating supports, such as those employed in tape automated bonding (TAB) processes for semiconductor packages. It falls within the aspects of generic packages and interconnections in the broader electricity-semiconductor devices hierarchy.
[H10W 70/048] Mechanical treatments, e.g. punching, cutting, deforming or cold welding -- H10W 70/048 covers mechanical treatments applied to semiconductor device packages, such as punching, cutting, deforming or cold welding of package components. These processes shape, form or join package parts without the use of heat.

[H10W 70/08] by depositing layers on the chip or wafer, e.g. "chip-first" RDLs -- Groups H10W70/08 and H10W70/09 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10W70/05 and H10W99/00 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 70/09] extending onto an encapsulation that laterally surrounds the chip or wafer, e.g. fan-out wafer level package [FOWLP] RDLs -- The CPC subclass H10W 70/09 covers techniques that extend redistribution layers onto an encapsulation that laterally surrounds a semiconductor chip or wafer, such as fan-out wafer-level packages. It relates to package designs where interconnects are formed on the outer surface of the molded compound surrounding the device.
[H10W 70/092] Adapting interconnections, e.g. making engineering charges, repairing -- Groups H10W70/092 - H10W70/098 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W70/05 . Groups H10W70/05 and H10W70/092 - H10W70/098 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 70/093] Connecting or disconnecting other interconnections thereto or therefrom, e.g. connecting bond wires or bumps -- The subclass H10W 70/093 covers techniques for attaching or removing extra interconnections to a semiconductor package, such as bonding wires or bump contacts, as part of the package's construction. It pertains to the aspect of how these additional connections are made or released within generic package and connector designs.
[H10W 70/095] of vias therein -- The H10W 70/095 subclass concerns the use, formation, arrangement or modification of vias-vertical interconnect accesses-within semiconductor device packages. It addresses how these conductive channels are implemented to provide electrical connections between different levels of a package's internal circuitry, as part of the broader aspects of generic package construction, interconnections and connectors.
[H10W 70/096] Cutting or separating -- H10W 70/96 pertains to methods and apparatus for cutting or separating semiconductor device packages, interconnections, connectors or related constructional details, including techniques to detach, trim or segment packaged components during manufacturing, testing or repair.
[H10W 70/097] Cleaning -- H10W 70/097 pertains to cleaning operations applied to semiconductor device packages, interconnections, connectors or related constructional details, encompassing methods and apparatus for removing contaminants, residues, particles or flux in order to preserve device performance and reliability during manufacture or maintenance.
[H10W 70/098] Applying pastes or inks, e.g. screen printing  H10W70/095  takes precedence -- H10W 70/098 covers methods of applying pastes or inks, such as screen printing, to semiconductor device packages and interconnections. It falls under aspects of packages within the generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices covered by class. The entry is subordinate to H10W 70/095, which has precedence.
[H10W 70/099] Connecting interconnections to insulating or insulated package substrates, interposers or redistribution layers -- The class covers techniques for joining electrical interconnections to insulating or insulated substrates within semiconductor packages, including the use of interposers and redistribution layers that reroute signals across the package. It addresses the constructional details required to attach conductors to these non-conductive surfaces in generic semiconductor packaging.
[H10W 70/20] Conductive package substrates serving as an interconnection, e.g. metal plates -- H10W 70/20 pertains to conductive substrates that serve as interconnections in semiconductor device packages, for example metal plates that provide electrical paths between components. It falls under the CPC hierarchy ELECTRICITY → SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES → GENERIC PACKAGES, INTERCONNECTIONS, CONNECTORS OR OTHER CONSTRUCTIONAL DETAILS OF DEVICES COVERED BY CLASS, specifically the aspects of packages.
[H10W 70/22] having an heterogeneous or anisotropic structure -- The H10W 70/22 entry characterises semiconductor device packages whose structure is heterogeneous or anisotropic, meaning they comprise different materials or exhibit direction-dependent properties. It covers designs that use varied layers, fillers or interconnects to create non-uniform thermal, mechanical or electrical behaviour within the package.
[H10W 70/24] characterised by materials -- H10W 70/24 is a CPC subclass for semiconductor devices that focuses on the material used in generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details. It falls under the hierarchy Electricity → Semiconductor devices → Generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of devices - aspects of packages, and specifically characterises packages by the materials employed.
[H10W 70/26] Semiconductor materials -- H10W 70/26 relates to semiconductor materials as they apply to generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of semiconductor devices. The subclass addresses aspects of the package that involve the material composition, properties, and treatment of the semiconductor elements within the device.
[H10W 70/28] Carbon-containing materials -- H10W 70/28 addresses carbon-containing materials used in the generic packages, interconnections and connectors of semiconductor devices. It includes any carbon-based conductive inks, pastes, composites or other substances employed for electrical conductivity, thermal management or structural purposes within the package construction.
[H10W 70/40] Leadframes -- Leadframes are metal frameworks that provide mechanical support and electrical interconnections for semiconductor dies within a package, linking the chip to external circuit contacts. They are typically formed from copper, aluminum or their alloys and may include bonding pads, lead fingers and reinforcing structures that define the package's layout and reliability.
[H10W 70/411] Chip-supporting parts, e.g. die pads -- The code H10W 70/411 covers chip-supporting parts that provide mechanical stability and electrical contact for a semiconductor die, such as die pads, heat spreaders or similar structures. These components are integral to the construction of semiconductor packages and their interconnections.
[H10W 70/413] Insulating or insulated substrates serving as die pads  H10W70/468  takes precedence -- H10W 70/413 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to insulating or insulated substrates used as die pads in semiconductor device packaging, with a note that classification H10W 70/468 should be considered first if applicable.
[H10W 70/415] Leadframe inner leads serving as die pads -- The CPC subclass H10W 70/415 covers semiconductor packages in which the inner leads of a leadframe are employed as the pads onto which the die is mounted. It pertains to the constructional details of generic semiconductor packages, focusing on the use of leadframe leads as die-pad interconnections.
[H10W 70/417] Bonding materials between chips and die pads -- A bonding material used to attach a semiconductor chip to its die pad, providing electrical connection and mechanical support while withstanding thermal cycling and processing steps. The material may be an epoxy, solder, anisotropic conductive film or another conductive adhesive designed for reliable chip-to-pad interconnects.
[H10W 70/421] Shapes or dispositions -- H10W 70/421 covers the geometric configuration and arrangement of semiconductor device packages, including the overall shape and spatial disposition of package components, interconnections and connectors. It addresses how these physical forms influence integration and performance within electronic assemblies.
[H10W 70/424] Cross-sectional shapes  H10W70/481  takes precedence -- H10W 70/424 in the Cooperative Patent Classification (EPO/USPTO) pertains to specific cross-sectional shapes of semiconductor device packages, with a note that classification H10W 70/481 should be considered first if applicable.
[H10W 70/427] Bent parts -- H10W 70/427 covers the bent portions of semiconductor device packages, interconnections, connectors or other structural elements, focusing on how these parts are curved or folded within a package. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for electricity-semiconductor devices-generic packages and addresses the geometry of bent components that affect mechanical stability or electrical performance.
[H10W 70/429] being the outer leads -- The H10W 70/429 subclass covers aspects of semiconductor device packages that pertain to the outer leads. It includes the design, configuration and external connection features of the leads that extend from the package to a circuit. This subclass is used to classify patents dealing with the physical details of how the leads are presented on the package surface.
[H10W 70/433] of deformation-absorbing parts, e.g. leads having meandering shapes  H10W70/481  takes precedence -- H10W 70/433 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to aspects of packages for semiconductor devices, specifically addressing deformation-absorbing parts such as leads with meandering shapes. This classification is used when the focus is on the design of these parts to absorb mechanical stresses, but it takes precedence only if not covered by H10W 70/481.
[H10W 70/435] of insulating layers on leadframes, e.g. bridging members -- H10W 70/435 covers insulating layers applied to leadframes in semiconductor packages, including bridging members that provide electrical separation between conductors. The entry addresses the design and implementation of such insulating structures as part of the overall package construction.
[H10W 70/438] of side rails, e.g. having holes -- The H10W 70/438 subclass covers semiconductor package designs that incorporate side rails, such as rails provided with holes to enable mounting, mechanical support, or electrical interconnection within the device. It relates to structural features of generic semiconductor packages and their interconnection details.
[H10W 70/442] of multiple leadframes in a single chip -- The subclass H10W 70/442 relates to semiconductor device packages that incorporate several leadframes within a single chip. It covers designs where multiple leadframes are combined to form one integrated package, detailing the arrangement, interconnection and structural aspects of such multi-leadframe constructions.
[H10W 70/451] Multilayered leadframes -- The H10W 70/451 subclass addresses multilayered leadframes, which are metal structures composed of two or more stacked layers used to support and electrically connect semiconductor chips within a package. These leadframes provide mechanical strength, heat dissipation and routing of signals through the multiple metal layers.
[H10W 70/453] comprising flexible metallic tapes -- The subclass H10W 70/453 pertains to semiconductor device packages that incorporate flexible metallic tapes as part of the package construction, such as for interconnections, mechanical support, or thermal management. It falls under the "Aspects of packages" group within generic package and connector details for semiconductor devices.
[H10W 70/456] Materials -- This subclass concerns the specific materials used in semiconductor device packages, interconnections and connectors, including substances for encapsulation, heat-dissipation and protective layers. It covers the selection and application of polymers, ceramics, metals and composites that influence the mechanical, electrical and thermal performance of the package.
[H10W 70/457] of metallic layers on leadframes -- This subclass deals with metallic layers that are applied to leadframes in semiconductor device packages, including processes such as plating, sputtering, evaporation or other metallisation methods. It addresses how these metal layers improve electrical connectivity, thermal management or barrier protection within the package.
[H10W 70/458] of insulating layers on leadframes -- H10W 70/458 relates to the application of insulating layers on leadframes in semiconductor device packages. It covers dielectric coatings or films that isolate leads, protect them from contamination, and provide electrical isolation within generic package constructions. The entry is used to classify methods, materials and structures of such insulating layers in semiconductor packages.
[H10W 70/461] specially adapted for cooling -- H10W 70/461 relates to semiconductor packages that incorporate features specifically designed for cooling, such as heat sinks, thermal interfaces, cooling channels or other thermal-management structures, to improve heat dissipation of the device.
[H10W 70/464] Additional interconnections in combination with leadframes -- This CPC subgroup covers semiconductor device packages where extra electrical connections are provided together with a leadframe, such as supplemental bond wires, redistribution layers, or conductive paths that extend beyond the basic leadframe structure. It includes methods and structures for integrating these additional interconnects within the package.
[H10W 70/465] Bumps or wires -- H10W 70/465 pertains to the use of conductive bumps or wire interconnections within semiconductor device packages, covering methods and structures for forming and attaching these connections that link the chip to external circuitry. The entry addresses packaging aspects that involve bump or wire technology.
[H10W 70/466] Tape carriers or flat leads -- H10W 70/466 covers tape carriers and flat leads used as interconnections in semiconductor packages. These components consist of thin conductive traces on a flexible substrate that can be attached to chips or circuit boards, providing a compact, planar routing solution. The classification addresses their design, materials and manufacturing methods within the broader context of semiconductor device packaging.
[H10W 70/467] Multilayered additional interconnections -- H10W 70/467 concerns the design and implementation of extra interconnections that are formed in multiple layers within a semiconductor package or similar device. These multilayered connections are added to improve routing, signal integrity, or integration of functions beyond the primary wiring scheme.
[H10W 70/468] Circuit boards -- H10W 70/468 covers circuit boards used as part of semiconductor device packages, including printed, flexible or rigid substrates that provide electrical interconnections among components. It includes the design, layout, mounting and integration of conductive traces, vias and contact pads within generic package structures. The classification is applied to devices where the board serves as the primary interconnection medium for the semiconductor elements.
[H10W 70/474] Batteries in combination with leadframes -- This entry classifies inventions that integrate batteries with leadframes, focusing on the way the power source is incorporated into the metal support structure of semiconductor packages. It deals with design aspects of combined battery-leadframe assemblies used to provide localized energy within electronic devices.
[H10W 70/475] Capacitors in combination with leadframes -- The CPC subclass H10W 70/475 covers aspects of semiconductor device packaging that involve integrating capacitors with leadframes. It relates to the design and construction of combined capacitor-leadframe structures within generic packages, interconnections, or connectors for electronic devices.
[H10W 70/476] Oscillators in combination with leadframes -- H10W 70/476 covers semiconductor oscillators that are directly integrated with leadframes as part of the device package, including the structural arrangement and electrical interconnection of the oscillator circuitry on the leadframe. It pertains to packaging aspects where the oscillator function is combined with the leadframe architecture.
[H10W 70/479] on or in insulating or insulated package substrates, interposers, or redistribution layers -- This CPC subclass covers technologies and methods involving insulating or insulated package substrates, interposers, or redistribution layers within semiconductor devices, emphasizing their design, fabrication, and integration to route electrical connections. It includes material selection, structural configurations, and processes that enable signal or power distribution in a packaged semiconductor.
[H10W 70/481] for devices being provided for in groups  H10D8/00  -  H10D48/00 -- The CPC subclass H10W 70/481 relates to aspects of packages for semiconductor devices that are provided as groups defined in the range H10D 8/00-H10D 48/00. It covers design features, interconnections and other constructional details of generic packages, connectors and related components used for those grouped devices.

[H10W 70/6528] of the portions that connect to chips, wafers or package parts -- The CPC entry H10W 70/6528 pertains to semiconductor device packages and focuses on the specific portions of a package that make the electrical connections to chips, wafers, or other package components. [H10W 70/655] Fan-out layouts -- Fan-out layouts describe the arrangement of interconnects that extend beyond the semiconductor die to the outer package surface, enabling a higher density of external contacts than traditional flip-chip or wire-bond approaches. This technique is used in advanced packaging such as fan-out wafer-level or panel-level packages to improve electrical performance and reduce package size.
[H10W 70/656] Fan-in layouts -- H10W 70/656 covers fan-in layouts in semiconductor device packages, describing how multiple input connections are routed to converge onto a smaller area of a chip or module. It addresses design details that optimise space, electrical performance and thermal management within generic package constructions.

[H10W 70/666] Organic materials or pastes -- The subclass covers the use of organic substances such as polymers, inks, adhesives or pastes in semiconductor package structures, for purposes like sealing, encapsulation, dielectric filling or forming interconnections. [H10W 70/67] characterised by their insulating layers or insulating parts -- Groups H10W70/67 , H10W70/68 , H10W70/681 and H10W70/682 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10W20/40 and H10W70/60 . It addresses how these forms influence interconnection and overall device construction. This entry is used to classify patents that focus on the physical configuration of package components rather than their electrical function.
[H10W 70/681] comprising holes not having chips therein, e.g. for outgassing, underfilling or bond wire passage -- H10W 70/681 covers semiconductor packages that incorporate empty holes, i.e. apertures without chips, intended for functions such as outgassing, under-filling, or providing a passage for bond wires within the device structure. These holes aid manufacturing processes or reliability by allowing gas to escape, filling material to flow, or wires to be routed through the package.
[H10W 70/682] comprising holes having chips therein -- The H10W 70/682 subclass refers to semiconductor device packages that incorporate holes or cavities in which chips or other components are mounted, such as through-silicon vias or perforated structures designed to embed chips within the package. It addresses constructional details of generic packages that use these internal openings.

For example, bond pads specially adapted for wire connectors are classified in group H10W72/59 . [H10W 72/07602] using an auxiliary member -- This CPC subclass concerns semiconductor device packages in which an auxiliary member - such as a support tab, spring, or other supplemental structure - is used to provide interconnections or connectors within the package. It falls under the broader category of generic packages, interconnections, or connectors in devices covered by the class.
[H10W 72/07604] the auxiliary member being temporary, e.g. a sacrificial coating -- This CPC subgroup H10W 72/07604 covers interconnections or connectors in semiconductor packages that use a temporary auxiliary member, such as a sacrificial coating applied during manufacturing and later removed.
[H10W 72/07607] the auxiliary member being a temporary substrate, e.g. a removable substrate -- The CPC subclass H10W 72/07607 covers interconnections or connectors in semiconductor packages where an auxiliary member serves as a temporary, removable substrate. It addresses designs that use a provisional substrate during manufacturing or testing, which is later taken away, allowing the final package to function without it.
[H10W 72/07611] Treating the bonding area before connecting, e.g. by applying flux or cleaning -- The H10W 72/07611 subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification details processes for preparing the bonding area of interconnections or connectors within semiconductor packages before joining, such as cleaning the surface or applying flux to improve adhesion and electrical contact. This treatment step is aimed at removing contaminants and enhancing the reliability of the subsequent connection.
[H10W 72/07621] Aligning -- This CPC subclass H10W 72/07621, titled Aligning, covers features and methods for aligning interconnections or connectors within semiconductor device packages, ensuring precise positioning of contacts to achieve reliable electrical connections and correct package assembly.
[H10W 72/07623] Active alignment, e.g. using optical alignment using marks or sensors -- In CPC H10W 72/07623 the focus is on active alignment techniques for semiconductor packages, where optical marks or sensors are employed to precisely position interconnections or connectors during assembly. This sub-group falls under generic package interconnections within the electricity-semiconductor devices hierarchy.
[H10W 72/07627] involving guiding structures, e.g. spacers or supporting members -- This CPC entry (H10W 72/07627) covers interconnections or connectors in semiconductor device packages, specifically guiding structures such as spacers or supporting members that provide alignment and mechanical support.
[H10W 72/07631] Techniques -- The H10W 72/07631 entry defines techniques for forming or applying interconnections and connectors within semiconductor device packages, covering methods that join internal circuit elements to external leads or other components in the package structure. It pertains to manufacturing and assembly processes used to create reliable electrical pathways inside generic semiconductor packages.

[H10W 72/07641] Controlling the environment, e.g. atmosphere composition or temperature -- H10W 72/07641 pertains to interconnections or connectors within semiconductor packages that incorporate means to control the internal environment, such as adjusting gas composition, humidity, pressure or temperature. The claim covers sealed or partially sealed structures, thermal management features, or gas-exchange pathways designed to protect the device during operation.

It falls under Electricity → Semiconductor Devices → Generic Packages, Interconnections, Connectors or Other Constructional Details of Devices Covered by Class, specifically addressing changes in strap connector behavior during joining.
[H10W 72/07652] changes in structures or sizes -- The H10W 72/07652 entry concerns design alterations that modify the geometry, dimensions or overall size of interconnection structures within semiconductor packages. It includes changes to conductor layouts, via shapes, thicknesses or spacing that affect how components are linked, without introducing new types of connectors.
[H10W 72/07653] changes in shapes -- This subclass pertains to semiconductor packages where the interconnections or connectors are deliberately altered in shape, such as reshaping leads, bond wires or other internal conductive paths to enhance performance, manufacturability, or reliability.
[H10W 72/07654] changes in dispositions -- H10W 72/07654 covers modifications in the disposition of interconnections or connectors within semiconductor device packages, such as alterations of layout, position, or orientation of conductive features, bond wires, or contact elements that affect how components are electrically linked inside the package.
[H10W 72/07655] changes in materials -- The H10W 72/07655 entry classifies semiconductor package interconnections or connectors whose material composition has been altered. It covers any changes in the constituent materials of these internal or external connections, such as adopting new metals, alloys, polymers or composites, and the impact of those material modifications on the performance or reliability of the device.

This subclass falls under the broader category of interconnections or connectors in semiconductor device packages.

They are part of the interconnection or connector aspects of generic package designs.

It is used for the design of bump-type contacts and other die-attachment features that join the chip to external circuitry within generic semiconductor packages.

[H10W 72/881] Bump connectors and strap connectors -- H10W 72/881 pertains to bump connectors and strap connectors that provide short, low-inductance electrical interconnections within semiconductor packages, linking chips to substrates or to other stacked dies. These structures are used to route signals and power in compact, high-frequency devices.
[H10W 72/884] Die-attach connectors and bond wires -- H10W 72/884 pertains to the connectors that attach a semiconductor die to its package and the bond wires used for that attachment, covering both the mechanical and electrical interfaces that secure the die within generic semiconductor packages.
[H10W 72/886] Die-attach connectors and strap connectors -- H10W 72/886 covers die-attach and strap connectors used to mechanically and electrically link semiconductor chips to their packages. These interconnections secure the die to the substrate and provide the necessary bonding paths, forming part of the generic package and connector structures for electronic devices.
[H10W 72/889] Bond wires and strap connectors -- H10W 72/889 covers bond wires and strap connectors used to interconnect semiconductor dies to external leads within packaged devices, detailing the conductive elements that provide electrical and mechanical connections between the chip and its package terminals.

The classification covers designs that combine the pad and the internal metal interconnections in a single process or layer, enabling direct electrical connection without separate discrete pads.

It is used for devices where the RDL forms part of the connector or interconnect architecture of the package.

This feature applies to generic package designs where connectors or internal wiring are built with interposed layers.

The arrangement provides increased current handling, reduced resistance, and improved mechanical reliability for chip-to-package connections.
[H10W 72/925] Bond pads having a filler embedded in a matrix -- A bond pad is a metallic area on a semiconductor package used for electrical connections, and in this subclass the pad contains a filler material that is embedded within a surrounding matrix. The filler improves mechanical stability, reduces stress, and can enhance the reliability of the wire-bond or flip-chip interconnection. This feature is defined under CPC code H10W 72/925.

[H10W 80/011] Manufacture or treatment of pads or other interconnections to be direct bonded -- The CPC entry H10W 80/011 covers processes for making or treating pads and other interconnection features that will be directly bonded to chips, wafers or substrates. It includes methods for preparing the bonding surfaces, such as surface cleaning, planarization or metallization, to enable reliable direct chip-to-chip or chip-to-substrate connections without intermediate adhesive layers.
[H10W 80/016] Cleaning -- H10W 80/016 covers cleaning procedures applied during the direct bonding of chips, wafers or substrates, which are part of generic semiconductor packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details. The subclass addresses methods for removing contaminants, residues or surface films to ensure reliable bonding and device performance.
[H10W 80/023] Applying a coating on the bonding area -- H10W 80/023 concerns applying a coating to the bonding area in direct chip, wafer or substrate bonding processes. It falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, generic packages and interconnections. The classification addresses coating methods that facilitate or protect direct bonding.
[H10W 80/031] Changing or setting shapes of the pads -- The subclass covers techniques for altering the geometry of bond pads on semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates to enable or improve direct bonding. It includes processes that reshape, trim, or form pad structures as part of package, interconnection or connector construction.
[H10W 80/033] by chemical means, e.g. etching -- This subclass covers chemical techniques for directly bonding semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates, such as etching processes that modify surfaces to enable adhesion. It is situated within the generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of semiconductor devices.
[H10W 80/035] by heating, e.g. melting or causing diffusion -- This subclass covers direct bonding of chips, wafers or substrates where heating is employed to melt interfaces or induce diffusion between surfaces, creating a permanent electrical and mechanical connection.
[H10W 80/037] by mechanical treatment, e.g. by cutting, pressing or stamping -- This subclass covers methods of directly bonding semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by mechanical treatment such as cutting, pressing or stamping, typically without intermediate layers. It falls within the generic packages, interconnections and connectors category of electricity and semiconductor devices.
[H10W 80/041] Thermally treating -- H10W 80/041 relates to thermally treating processes used in direct bonding of chips, wafers or substrates within semiconductor device packaging. It covers heat-based steps such as annealing, curing or sintering applied before or after bonding to improve adhesion, reduce voids, or modify material properties. The classification is part of the CPC group for generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details of semiconductor devices.
[H10W 80/102] Controlling the environment during the bonding, e.g. the temperature or pressure -- H10W 80/102 covers methods for controlling the bonding environment, such as regulating temperature or pressure, during direct bonding of semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates. This classification falls under generic packages and interconnections for semiconductor devices.
[H10W 80/161] Aligning -- The H10W 80/161 "Aligning" entry covers methods and apparatus for positioning chips, wafers or substrates relative to each other before direct bonding. It includes techniques that ensure precise lateral and angular registration to achieve reliable interconnections in semiconductor packages. This classification falls under electricity, semiconductor devices, and generic package construction details.
[H10W 80/163] using active alignment, e.g. detecting marks and correcting position -- The CPC entry H10W 80/163 covers direct bonding of semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates that uses active alignment methods, such as detecting alignment marks and correcting the relative position during the bonding step. It belongs to electricity → semiconductor devices → generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details, improving placement accuracy for reliable connections.
[H10W 80/165] using passive alignment, e.g. using self-alignment -- This subclass pertains to methods of directly bonding semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates where the parts are aligned without active positioning, relying on passive or self-alignment mechanisms such as surface tension, electrostatic forces, or geometric features that guide the components into registration before bonding.
[H10W 80/168] using guiding structures -- H10W 80/168 covers methods for direct bonding of chips, wafers or substrates that employ guiding structures to align and position the components during the bonding process, ensuring precise contact and improving bonding yield and reliability.
[H10W 80/211] using auxiliary members, e.g. aids for protecting the bonding area -- The subgroup H10W 80/211 covers direct bonding of semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates in which auxiliary members are employed to protect the bonding area. It includes protective aids such as temporary masks, shields or fixtures that prevent contamination, oxidation or mechanical damage during the bonding process.
[H10W 80/301] Bonding techniques, e.g. hybrid bonding -- The CPC subclass H10W 80/301 covers methods for directly bonding semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates, including hybrid bonding and similar techniques that form electrical and mechanical connections without solder or wire bonds. It focuses on low-temperature, high-density interconnect processes used in advanced package and device construction.
[H10W 80/312] characterised by the direct bonding of electrically conductive pads -- This CPC subclass covers semiconductor devices whose electrical connection is achieved by directly bonding electrically-conductive pads on chips, wafers or substrates, eliminating the need for separate interconnects. It resides under electricity, semiconductor devices, generic packages, interconnections, connectors or other constructional details.
[H10W 80/314] characterized by direct bonding of pads or other interconnections -- The H10W 80/314 subclass pertains to semiconductor devices whose pads, chips, wafers or other interconnections are linked by direct bonding rather than by solder or wire bonds. It includes techniques such as thermocompression, adhesive or eutectic bonding that create both electrical and mechanical connections within generic packages, connectors or other constructional details.
[H10W 80/327] characterised by the direct bonding of insulating parts, e.g. of silicon oxide layers -- The H10W 80/327 subclass covers semiconductor devices whose insulating parts, such as silicon-oxide layers, are joined directly without an intermediate bonding medium. It applies to generic packages, interconnections and connectors that use direct bonding of chips, wafers or substrates, and is defined by the direct bonding of insulating components.
[H10W 80/331] characterised by the application of energy for connecting -- H10W 80/331 refers to semiconductor device packages where chips, wafers or substrates are joined by applying energy such as heat, laser, ultrasonic or plasma to create a direct bond without intermediate materials. The method emphasizes energy-driven connection of the components within the package.
[H10W 80/333] Compression bonding -- H10W 80/333 covers compression bonding, a direct-bonding technique in which pressure is applied to join semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates without the use of intermediate layers or adhesives. It is used in the construction of generic packages, interconnections and connectors for electronic devices.
[H10W 80/334] Thermocompression bonding -- H10W 80/334, titled Thermocompression bonding, covers a direct bonding technique in which heat and pressure are applied to join semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates without an intermediate adhesive. The method is used to create reliable electrical and mechanical connections in generic semiconductor packages, interconnections and connectors.
[H10W 80/335] Ultrasonic bonding -- Ultrasonic bonding joins semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by applying high-frequency mechanical vibrations through a sonotrode, producing localized heat and pressure that fuse the materials together without extra filler. The technique is widely used for direct interconnections in generic semiconductor packages and other device constructions.
[H10W 80/337] causing reflow -- The H10W 80/337 subclass covers methods that intentionally cause a reflow step during direct bonding of chips, wafers or substrates to improve interconnect integrity or remove voids. It is part of the broader category dealing with generic packages, interconnections, connectors and other constructional details of semiconductor devices.
[H10W 80/338] using EM radiation or electron beams, e.g. using lasers -- The subclass H10W 80/338 covers techniques for directly bonding semiconductor chips, wafers or substrates by applying electromagnetic radiation or electron beams, such as laser irradiation, to the joining surfaces. These methods create interconnections without the use of adhesives, solders or other intermediate materials, enabling precise and low-temperature bonding of device components.
[H10W 80/339] using integrated means for heating, e.g. embedded heater -- The H10W 80/339 subclass covers semiconductor devices whose packages incorporate an integrated heating element, such as an embedded heater, to provide temperature control during operation or processing. It applies to chip, wafer or substrate assemblies where the heating means are built into the package rather than added externally. This enables precise thermal management for devices that are directly bonded or interconnected.
[H10W 80/341] Anodic bonding -- H10W 80/341 - anodic bonding - refers to a direct bonding method that joins chips, wafers or substrates by applying an electric field at elevated temperature, usually through a glass or silicon-nitride interface. It is classified under generic packages, interconnections and connectors for semiconductor devices in the electricity section.
[H10W 80/701] characterised by the pads after the direct bonding -- H10W 80/701 covers semiconductor device constructions in which, after direct bonding of chips, wafers or substrates, the interconnection pads are formed, modified or characterized to provide electrical contact, addressing the pad layout and features that follow the bonding step.
[H10W 80/721] having structure or size changed during the connecting -- In CPC H10W 80/721 the claim covers semiconductor devices whose interconnection process alters the component's structure or dimensions, such as when chips, wafers or substrates are directly bonded and experience deformation, size reduction, or reshaping during the connection step. This sub-group addresses methods where the physical form changes as part of the bonding.
[H10W 80/732] having shape changed during the connecting -- H10W 80/732 covers semiconductor packages, interconnections or connectors whose physical shape is altered during the joining process, such as when chips, wafers or substrates are directly bonded and the structure deforms to achieve the connection. This includes any constructional detail where shape change is integral to the bond.
[H10W 80/743] having disposition changed during the connecting -- H10W 80/743 concerns semiconductor package designs where the relative orientation or position of a chip, wafer or substrate is altered during the direct bonding process, such as rotating, flipping or shifting components as they are connected. This includes methods that change the disposition of the elements during bonding to achieve specific electrical or mechanical characteristics.
[H10W 80/754] having material changed during the connecting -- H10W 80/754 covers direct bonding techniques for chips, wafers or substrates in which the material of at least one bonding surface is altered during the connection, for example by diffusion, alloying, deposition or other in-situ modifications that improve adhesion or electrical performance. This sub-class falls under generic packages, interconnections and connectors for semiconductor devices, focusing on bonding processes that involve material changes as part of the joining step.

This layout is used to reduce package size and enhance electrical performance in integrated circuits.

[H10W 90/288] characterised by arrangements for thermal management of the stacked chips -- H10W 90/288 covers semiconductor packages where the stacked chips incorporate specific thermal-management arrangements, such as heat spreaders, thermal vias, cooling plates or integrated heat-sink structures designed to remove heat from the chip stack.

[H10W 90/768] between a chip and a stacked discrete passive device -- This subclass covers package configurations where an integrated circuit chip is directly connected to a stacked discrete passive component such as a resistor, capacitor or inductor, using interposers, bonding wires or other interconnection structures that bridge the chip and the passive element. It falls within the H10W class for semiconductor device packages.
[H10W 90/769] between a chip and a laterally-adjacent discrete passive device -- The classification covers interconnections that join a semiconductor chip to a discrete passive component placed laterally adjacent on the same package, such as resistors, capacitors or inductors, forming a local wiring or bond structure within the package layout. It addresses the physical arrangement and coupling between the active chip and the neighboring passive element.
[H10W 90/791] of direct-bonded pads -- Groups H10W90/791 , H10W90/792 , H10W90/794 , H10W90/796 and H10W90/798 are incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W90/00 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 90/792] between multiple chips -- H10W 90/792 covers the arrangement of semiconductor packages where two or more chips are placed together within a single package, including the internal interconnections and mechanical features that link the chips. This subclass pertains to multi-chip module designs used to integrate functions while sharing a common external case.
[H10W 90/794] between a chip and a stacked insulating package substrate, interposer or RDL -- It pertains to structures that are positioned between the semiconductor chip and a stacked insulating package substrate, an interposer, or a redistribution layer, providing electrical interconnection, signal routing or mechanical support. This sub-group falls under package configurations within the generic packages and interconnections class of semiconductor device classifications.
[H10W 90/796] between a chip and a stacked lead frame, conducting package substrate or heat sink -- H10W 90/796 relates to the conductive coupling between a semiconductor chip and a stacked lead frame, a conducting package substrate or a heat sink within a semiconductor package. It addresses the structural and electrical features that provide both interconnection and thermal path between the die and these components. This sub-group falls under the CPC hierarchy ELECTRICITY - SEMICONDUCTOR DEVICES - GENERIC PACKAGES, INTERCONNECTIONS, CONNECTORS OR OTHER CONSTRUCTIONAL DETAILS OF DEVICES COVERED BY CLASS - Package configurations.
[H10W 90/798] between a chip and a stacked discrete passive device -- H10W 90/798 classifies package configurations that join a semiconductor chip directly to a stacked discrete passive component, such as a resistor, capacitor or inductor, forming an integrated interconnection within the same package.
[H10W 90/811] Multiple chips on leadframes -- Group H10W90/811 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W90/00 . Groups H10W90/00 and H10W90/811 should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 95/00] Packaging processes not covered by the other groups of this subclass -- Group H10W95/00 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from groups H10W72/07232 , H10W72/07233 , H10W72/07236 and H10W99/00 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
[H10W 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- Group H10W99/00 is incomplete pending reclassification of documents from group H10W42/00 . Group H10W99/00 is also impacted by reclassification into groups H10W40/00 , H10W40/01 , H10W42/00 , H10W70/00 , H10W70/01 , H10W70/02 , H10W70/04 , H10W70/05 , H10W70/06 , H10W70/08 , H10W70/09 , H10W74/01 , H10W76/01 and H10W95/00 . All groups listed in this Warning should be considered in order to perform a complete search.
